questions.question: How many rational numbers can be found between two distinct rational numbers?
questions.explanation:
Let a and b be any two distinct rational numbers.

Their mean {tex}a+b\over 2{/tex}, is between a and b and it is rational. Now {tex}a+b\over 2{/tex} and b are two rational numbers.Find their mean, which is again rational,and also is in between a and b. continue until you have had enough. there are infinitely many rational numbers between a and b

{tex}\frac{-2}{3}{/tex}, {tex}\frac{-4}{5}{/tex}

LCM = 15,
So, {tex}\frac{-2 \times 5}{3 \times 5}{/tex}, {tex}\frac{-4 \times 3}{5 \times 3}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}\frac{-10}{15}{/tex}, {tex}\frac{-12}{15}{/tex}

and since, -10 > -12
So, {tex}\frac{-10}{15}{/tex} > {tex}\frac{-12}{15}{/tex}
thus, {tex}\frac{-2}{3}{/tex} > {tex}\frac{-4}{5}{/tex}

questions.question: The sum of rational and an irrational number
questions.explanation:
By definition, an irrational number in decimal form goes on forever without repeating (a non-repeating, non-terminating decimal). By definition, a rational number in the decimal form either terminates or repeats. If you add a non-repeating, non-terminating decimal to a terminating decimal, you still have a non-repeating, non-terminating decimal. If you add a non-repeating, non-terminating decimal to a repeating decimal, you will no longer have a repeating decimal.

Examples:

  • 3 + {tex}\sqrt{5}{/tex} = 3 + 2.236067977... = 5.236067977... ; which is irrational
  • {tex}4\frac{1}{3}{/tex} + {tex}\pi{/tex} = 4.33333333... + 3.141592653... = 4.47925986 which is also irrational

questions.question: If x = {tex} 2 + \sqrt 3 {/tex}, then {tex}x + \frac 1 x{/tex} =
questions.explanation:

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{x^{2}+1}{x}{/tex}
now, put x = {tex}2+\sqrt{3}{/tex}
we have,
{tex}\frac{(2+\sqrt{3})^{2}+1}{2+\sqrt{3}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{4+3+2(2 \sqrt{3})+1}{2+\sqrt{3}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{8+4 \sqrt{3}}{2+\sqrt{3}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{4(2+\sqrt{3})}{2+\sqrt{3}}{/tex}
= 4

questions.question: If {tex}\frac{2+\sqrt{3}}{2-\sqrt{3}}=a+b \sqrt{3}{/tex}, then
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{2+\sqrt{3}}{2-\sqrt{3}}{/tex}
Multiplying numerator and denominator by 2 + {tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex} 
So, {tex}\frac{(2+\sqrt{3})^{2}}{(2-\sqrt{3})(2+\sqrt{3})}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{4+3+4 \sqrt{3}}{4-3}{/tex},
= 7 + 4{tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex} 
Now equating 7 + 4{tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex} and a + b{tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex} 
We get,
a = 7 and b = 4

questions.question: If {tex}x = 3 + \sqrt 8 {/tex} , then the value of {tex}\left(x^2+\frac{1}{x^2}\right){/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Given: x = {tex}(3+\sqrt{8}){/tex}
{tex}\frac{1}{x}=\frac{1}{(3+\sqrt{8})}=\frac{1}{(3+\sqrt{8})} \times \frac{(3-\sqrt{8})}{(3-\sqrt{8})}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{(3-\sqrt{8})}{\left(3^2-(\sqrt{8})^2\right)}=\frac{(3+\sqrt{8})}{(9-8)}=(3-\sqrt{8}){/tex}
{tex}\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)=(3+\sqrt{8})+(3-\sqrt{8})=6{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)^2=6^2{/tex} = 36
{tex}\Rightarrow\left(x^2+\frac{1}{x^2}\right)+2 \times x \times \frac{1}{x}=36{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow\left(x^2+\frac{1}{x^2}\right)+2=36{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow\left(x^2+\frac{1}{x^2}\right)=36-2{/tex} = 34

questions.question: If {tex}x = 4 - \sqrt {15} {/tex}, then the value of {tex}\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right){/tex} is
questions.explanation:
{tex}x+\frac{1}{x}=\frac{x^{2}+1}{x}{/tex}
Now, put x=4- {tex}\sqrt{15}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{(4-\sqrt{15})^{2}+1}{4-\sqrt{15}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{16+15-8 \sqrt{15}+1}{4-\sqrt{15}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{32-8 \sqrt{15}}{4-\sqrt{15}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 8{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{(3+2+2\sqrt6)+3+2-2\sqrt{6}}{3-2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 10

questions.question: {tex}\frac{\sqrt{32}+\sqrt{48}}{\sqrt{8}+\sqrt{12}}{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{4}{2}\left(\frac{\sqrt{2}+\sqrt{3}}{\sqrt{2}+\sqrt{3}}\right){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2

questions.question: If {tex}\sqrt 3 = 1.732{/tex} and {tex}\sqrt 2 = 1.414{/tex}, then the value of {tex}\frac {1} {\sqrt 3 - \sqrt 2 }{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}-\sqrt{2}}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}-\sqrt{2}} \times \frac{\sqrt{3}+\sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{3}+\sqrt{2}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\sqrt{3}+\sqrt{2}}{3-2}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{3}+\sqrt{2}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1.732 + 1.414
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3.146

questions.question: The simplified form of {tex}16^{\frac{-1}{4}} \times \sqrt[4]{16}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
{tex}16^{\frac{-1}{4}} \times \sqrt[4]{16}{/tex}
But, 16 = 24
So,
{tex}\Rightarrow 16^{\frac{-1}{4}} \times \sqrt[4]{16}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow\left\{(2)^4\right\}^{\frac{-1}{4}} \times(2)^{4 \times \frac{1}{4}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow(2)^{4 \times \frac{-1}{4}} \times 2{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 2^{-1} \times 2{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac 22{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1

questions.question: The value of x in 3 + 2x = {tex}(64)^{\frac{1}{2}}+(27)^{\frac{1}{3}}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3 + 2x = {tex}\sqrt {64} + \sqrt[3]{27}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3 + 2x = 8 + 3
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x= 8 = 23
equating both,
x = 3

{tex}\Rightarrow x^{0}{/tex}
=1

questions.question: If a = -2, b = -1, then ab - ba is equal to
questions.explanation:
{tex}a^b -b^a {/tex} 
= (-2)-1 - (-1)-2
= {tex}{1\over(-2)} - { 1\over(-1)^2} {/tex} 
= {tex} {-1 \over2}-1 {/tex} 
= {tex} {-3 \over2} {/tex} 
= -1.5

questions.question: If {tex}\frac{3-\sqrt{5}}{3+2 \sqrt{5}}=a \sqrt{5}-\frac{19}{11} b{/tex} , then the value of b is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{3-\sqrt{5}}{3+2 \sqrt{5}}=a \sqrt{5}-\frac{19}{11} b{/tex}
taking LHS,
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{3-\sqrt{5}}{3+2 \sqrt{5}} \times \frac{3-2 \sqrt{5}}{3-2 \sqrt{5}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{3(3-2 \sqrt{5})-\sqrt{5}(3-2 \sqrt{5})}{9-20}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{9-6 \sqrt{5}-3 \sqrt{5}+10}{-11}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{19-9 \sqrt{5}}{-11}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{-19}{11}+\frac{9 \sqrt{5}}{11}{/tex}
equating this with RHS,
we get,
{tex}\frac{-19}{11} b=-\frac{19}{11}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} b = 1

questions.question: The value of {tex}\left(\frac{81}{16}\right)^{\frac{-3}{4}} \times\left\{\left(\frac{25}{9}\right)^{\frac{-3}{2}} \div\left(\frac{5}{2}\right)^{-3}\right\}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\left(\frac{81}{16}\right)^{\frac{-3}{4}} \times\left\{\left(\frac{25}{9}\right)^{\frac{-3}{2}} \div\left(\frac{5}{2}\right)^{-3}\right\}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow\left(\frac{3}{2}\right)^{4 \times \frac{-3}{4}} \times\left\{\left(\frac{5}{3}\right)^{2 \times \frac{-3}{2}} \div\left(\frac{5}{2}\right)^{-3}\right\}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow\left(\frac{3}{2}\right)^{-3} \times\left\{\left(\frac{5}{3}\right)^{-3} \div\left(\frac{5}{2}\right)^{-3}\right\}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow\left(\frac{3}{2}\right)^{-3} \times\left(\frac{5}{3} \times \frac{2}{5}\right)^{-3}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow\left(\frac{3}{2}\right)^{-3} \times\left(\frac{2}{3}\right)^{-3}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow\left(\frac{3}{2} \times \frac{2}{3}\right)^{-3}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (1)-3 = 1

questions.question: {tex}\sqrt 8 + 2\sqrt {32} - 5\sqrt 2 {/tex} is equal to

{tex}\sqrt{8}+2 \sqrt{32}-5 \sqrt{2}{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow 5 \sqrt{2}{/tex}

questions.question: If x = 3 + 2{tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex}, then the value of {tex}x + \frac 1 x{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
{tex}x+\frac{1}{x}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{x^{2}+1}{x}{/tex} 
Put the value of x,
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{(3+2 \sqrt{2})^{2}+1}{3+2 \sqrt{2}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{9+8+12 \sqrt{2}+1}{3+2 \sqrt{2}}{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{6(3+2 \sqrt{2})}{3+2 \sqrt{2}}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 6

questions.question: The value of {tex}\frac{9^{\frac{1}{3}} \times 27^{\frac{1}{2}}}{3^{\frac{-1}{6}} \times 3^{\frac{1}{3}}}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{9^{\frac{1}{3}} \times 27^{\frac{1}{2}}}{3^{\frac{-1}{6}} \times 3^{\frac{1}{3}}}{/tex}

questions.question: If 2x = 4y = 8z and {tex}\frac{1}{2 x}+\frac{1}{4 y}+\frac{1}{4 z}=4{/tex} then the value of x is

questions.question: If a = 7 - 4{tex}\sqrt 3 {/tex}, then the value of {tex}\sqrt{a}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{a}}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Let {tex}\sqrt{a}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{a}}{/tex} = x
Then, squaring both side, we get
{tex}a+\frac{1}{a}+2{/tex} = x2 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{a^{2}+1}{a}+2{/tex} = x2 
Now, put the value of a,
{tex}\frac{(7-4 \sqrt{3})^{2}+1}{7-4 \sqrt{3}}+2{/tex} = x2 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{49+48-56 \sqrt{3}+1}{7-4 \sqrt{3}}+2{/tex} = x2 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{98-56 \sqrt{3}}{7-4 \sqrt{3}}+2{/tex} = x2 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}14\left(\frac{7-4 \sqrt{3}}{7-4 \sqrt{3}}\right)+2{/tex} = x2 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 16 = x2 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 4
So, x = {tex}\sqrt{a}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{a}}{/tex} = 4

questions.question: Every rational number is
questions.explanation:
Every rational number (1, 4.5, 10, 1/2, -27, 75/5, 0) is a real number.

questions.question: The difference of a rational and an irrational number is always
questions.explanation:
Rational Numbers say {tex}4 \over 9{/tex}, {tex}p \over q{/tex}, {tex}\sqrt4{/tex}, fraction, whole numbers, terminating decimal, repeating decimal, perfect square,  can be expressed as a ratio of two integers provided the denominator is not equal to zero
Irrational Numbers {tex}\sqrt 2{/tex} ,{tex}\sqrt 5{/tex} ,{tex}\sqrt 7{/tex} , {tex}\pi{/tex} not a fraction, decimal does not repeat, decimal does not end, non-perfect square,  we cannot express as a ratio but both can be expressed as decimal numbers
The difference between a rational and an irrational number is always an irrational number.
e.g. rational - irrational = irrational   say 2 - {tex} \sqrt2{/tex}  = irrational

questions.question: The difference between two distinct irrational numbers is always
questions.explanation:
The difference between two distinct irrational numbers can be either a rational number or an irrational number.
e.g difference between pi and  ({tex}\pi{/tex} - 3) is equal to 3 which is rational
{tex}\sqrt2{/tex} and  {tex} \sqrt2{/tex} + 1 both are irrational but their difference is 1 which is rational
Similarly, {tex} \sqrt2{/tex}  and {tex} \sqrt3{/tex} are irrational and their difference ( {tex} \sqrt3{/tex}  - {tex} \sqrt2{/tex} ) is also irrational

questions.question: The product of a rational number and an irrational number is
questions.explanation:
The product of a rational number and an irrational number can be either a rational number or an irrational number.
e.g {tex} \sqrt5{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\sqrt2{/tex} = {tex} \sqrt10{/tex} which is  irrational
but  {tex} \sqrt8{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex} \sqrt2{/tex} = {tex} \sqrt16{/tex}  = 4 which is a rational number
Thus, the product of two irrational numbers can be either rational or irrational 
similarly, the product of rational and irrational numbers can be either rational or irrational 
5 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex} \sqrt2{/tex} = 5{tex} \sqrt2{/tex}  which is irrational.
but 0 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex} \sqrt3{/tex}  = 0 which is rational.

questions.question: (1 + {tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex}) + (1 - {tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex}) is
questions.explanation:
(1 + {tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex}) + (1 - {tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex}) = 1 + {tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex} + 1 - {tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex} = 1 + 1 = 2 And 2 is a rational number.
Therefore the given number is rational number.

questions.question: If a is rational and {tex}\sqrt b {/tex} is irrational, then {tex}a + \sqrt b {/tex} is:
questions.explanation:
Let a be rational and {tex}\sqrt b {/tex} is irrational.
If possible let {tex}a + \sqrt b {/tex} be rational.
Then {tex}a + \sqrt b {/tex} is rational and a is rational.
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}\left[ {\left( {a + \sqrt b } \right) - a} \right]{/tex} is rational [Difference of two rationals is rational]
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}\sqrt b {/tex}  is rational.
This contradicts the fact that {tex}\sqrt b {/tex} is irrational.
The contradiction arises by assuming that {tex}a + \sqrt b {/tex} is rational.
Therefore, {tex}a + \sqrt b {/tex} is irrational.

questions.question: The sum of a rational and an irrational number is
questions.explanation:
Let rational number + irrational number = rational number
And we know " rational number can be expressed in the form of PQ, where p, q are any integers, 
So, we can express our assumption As :
PQ + x = ab ( Here x is a irrational number )
x = ab - PQ 
So,
x is a rational number, but that contradicts our starting assumption. 
Hence rational number + irrational number = irrational number

questions.question: If {tex}\sqrt{2}=1.4142,{/tex} then {tex}\sqrt{\frac{\sqrt{2}-1}{\sqrt{2}+1}}{/tex} is equal to

=1.4142-1

questions.question: If {tex}x=\frac{2}{3+\sqrt{7}}{/tex}, then (x - 3)2
questions.explanation:

= {tex}\frac{2(3-\sqrt{7})}{(3)^2-(\sqrt{7})^2}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{2(3-\sqrt{7})}{9-7}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{2(3-\sqrt{7})}{2}{/tex}
= 3 - {tex}\sqrt{7}{/tex}
Now (x - 3)2 = ( 3 - {tex}\sqrt{7}{/tex} -3)2
= (-{tex}\sqrt{7}{/tex})2
=7

questions.question: If x = 7 + 4{tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex} and xy = 1, then {tex}\frac{1}{x^{2}}+\frac{1}{y^{2}}={/tex}
questions.explanation:
Given  x = 7 + 4{tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex} and xy = 1
{tex}\frac{1}{x}{/tex}= {tex}\frac{1}{7+ 4\sqrt{3}}{/tex}
={tex}\frac{1}{7+ 4\sqrt{3}}\times \frac{7-4\sqrt{3}}{7-4\sqrt{3}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1.(7-4\sqrt{3)}}{(7)^2- (4\sqrt{3})^2}{/tex}
={tex}\frac{7-4\sqrt{3}}{49- 48}{/tex}
= 7-4{tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}  {tex}\frac{1}{x}{/tex}= 7-4{tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} ({tex}\frac{1}{x}{/tex})2 =(7-4{tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex})2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{x^2}{/tex} = 49 + 48 - 56{tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}\frac{1}{x^2}{/tex} = 97-56{tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex}
Also given. x.y = 1
{tex}\therefore{/tex} y = {tex}\frac{1}{x}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = 7-4{tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}\frac{1}{y}{/tex} = x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{y}{/tex} = 7+4{tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}({tex}\frac{1}{y}{/tex})2 = ( 7+4{tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex})2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{y^2}{/tex} = 49+48+56{tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{y^2}{/tex} = 97+56{tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex}
Now
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{x^{2}}+\frac{1}{y^{2}}{/tex} = 97-56{tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex} + 97+56{tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex} 
= 194

questions.question: If x= {tex}\frac{\sqrt{5}+\sqrt{3}}{\sqrt{5}-\sqrt{3}}{/tex} and {tex}y=\frac{\sqrt{5}-\sqrt{3}}{\sqrt{5}+\sqrt{3}}{/tex} , then x + y + xy =
questions.explanation:
Given x= {tex}\frac{\sqrt{5}+\sqrt{3}}{\sqrt{5}-\sqrt{3}}{/tex} and y = {tex}\frac{\sqrt{5}-\sqrt{3}}{\sqrt{5}+\sqrt{3}}{/tex} 
Then,
x + y + xy =  {tex}\frac{\sqrt{5}+\sqrt{3}}{\sqrt{5}-\sqrt{3}}{/tex}+ {tex}\frac{\sqrt{5}-\sqrt{3}}{\sqrt{5}+\sqrt{3}}{/tex}+{tex}\frac{\sqrt{5}+\sqrt{3}}{\sqrt{5}-\sqrt{3}}​​\times \frac{\sqrt{5}-\sqrt{3}}{\sqrt{5}+\sqrt{3}} {/tex}
={tex}\frac{\sqrt{5}+\sqrt{3}}{\sqrt{5}-\sqrt{3}}{/tex}{tex}\times{/tex}{tex}\frac{\sqrt{5}+\sqrt{3}}{\sqrt{5}+\sqrt{3}}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{\sqrt{5}-\sqrt{3}}{\sqrt{5}+\sqrt{3}}{/tex}{tex}\times{/tex}{tex}\frac{\sqrt{5}-\sqrt{3}}{\sqrt{5}-\sqrt{3}}{/tex}+ 1
= {tex}\frac{(\sqrt{5}+\sqrt{3})^2}{5-3}{/tex}+ {tex}\frac{(\sqrt{5}-\sqrt{3})^2}{5-3}{/tex} +1
= {tex}\frac{(\sqrt{5})^2+(\sqrt{3})^2+2(\sqrt{5})(\sqrt{3)}}{2}{/tex}+ {tex}\frac{(\sqrt{5})^2+(\sqrt{3})^2-2(\sqrt{5})(\sqrt{3)}}{2}{/tex}+1
= {tex}\frac{5+3+2\sqrt{15}}{2}{/tex}+ {tex}\frac{5+3-2\sqrt{15}}{2}{/tex}+1
= {tex}\frac{8+2\sqrt{15}}{2}{/tex}+ {tex}\frac{8-2\sqrt{15}}{2}{/tex}+1
= 4 + {tex}\sqrt{15}{/tex} + 4 - {tex}\sqrt{15}{/tex} +1
= 8+1
=9

questions.question: If x ={tex}\frac{\sqrt{3}-\sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{3}+\sqrt{2}}{/tex}and y{tex}=\frac{\sqrt{3}+\sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{3}-\sqrt{2}}{/tex}, then x2 + xy + y2 =
questions.explanation:
Given x ={tex}\frac{\sqrt{3}-\sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{3}+\sqrt{2}}{/tex}and y{tex}=\frac{\sqrt{3}+\sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{3}-\sqrt{2}}{/tex},
Consider,
x = {tex}\frac{\sqrt{3}-\sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{3}+\sqrt{2}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{\sqrt{3}-\sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{3}+\sqrt{2}}\times\frac{\sqrt{3}-\sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{3}-\sqrt{2}} {/tex}
={tex}\frac{(\sqrt{3}-\sqrt{2})^2}{(\sqrt{3})^2+(\sqrt{2})^2} {/tex}
= {tex}\frac{(\sqrt{3})^2+(\sqrt{2})^2 -2(\sqrt{3})(\sqrt{2})}{3-2} {/tex}
= {tex}\frac{3+2 -2\sqrt{6}}{1} {/tex}
= 5-2{tex}\sqrt{6}{/tex}
Hence  x = 5-2{tex}\sqrt{6}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x 2 = (5-2{tex}\sqrt{6}{/tex})2
= 25 + 24 -20{tex}\sqrt{6}{/tex}
= 49-20{tex}\sqrt{6}{/tex}
i.e. x2 = 49-20{tex}\sqrt{6}{/tex} ----(i)
Again consider 
y = {tex}\frac{\sqrt{3}+\sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{3}-\sqrt{2}}{/tex}
={tex}\frac{\sqrt{3}+\sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{3}-\sqrt{2}}\times\frac{\sqrt{3}+\sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{3}+\sqrt{2}}{/tex}
={tex}\frac{(\sqrt{3}+\sqrt{2})^2}{(\sqrt{3})^2-(\sqrt{2})^2}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{(\sqrt{3})^2+(\sqrt{2})^2+2(\sqrt{3})(\sqrt{2})}{3-2}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{3+2+2\sqrt{6}}{1}{/tex}
= 5+2{tex}\sqrt{6}{/tex}
Hence y=5+2{tex}\sqrt{6}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y2 = (5+2{tex}\sqrt{6}{/tex})2
= 25+24+20{tex}\sqrt{6}{/tex}
= 49+20{tex}\sqrt{6}{/tex} 
i.e. y2 = 49 + 20{tex}\sqrt{6}{/tex} ---(ii)
Then x2 + xy + y2 
=  49-20{tex}\sqrt{6}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{\sqrt{3}-\sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{3}+\sqrt{2}}{/tex}{tex}\times{/tex}{tex}\frac{\sqrt{3}+\sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{3}-\sqrt{2}}{/tex} + 49 + 20{tex}\sqrt{6}{/tex} [ from (i) nd (ii) ]
= 98+1
= 99

questions.question: The value of {tex}\frac{\sqrt{48}+\sqrt{32}}{\sqrt{27}+\sqrt{18}}{/tex}, is

questions.question: If {tex}\frac{5-\sqrt{3}}{2+\sqrt{3}}=x+y \sqrt{3}{/tex}, then

={tex}\frac{5(2-\sqrt{3}) -\sqrt{3}(2-\sqrt{3})}{4-3}{/tex}

 Hence, x+y{tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex} = 13-7{tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex}

questions.question: If x ={tex}\sqrt[3]{2+\sqrt{3}}{/tex}, then {tex}x^{3}+\frac{1}{x^{3}}={/tex}
questions.explanation:
Given x ={tex}\sqrt[3]{2+\sqrt{3}}{/tex},
x3 = ({tex}\sqrt[3]{2+\sqrt{3}}{/tex})3 
= {tex}({(2+\sqrt{3})^{\frac{1}{3}}})^3{/tex}
={tex}{(2+\sqrt{3})^{\frac{1}{3}\times 3}}{/tex}
= {tex}{2+\sqrt{3}}{/tex}
Hence x 3 ={tex}{2+\sqrt{3}}{/tex} ----(i)
Now, 
{tex}\frac{1}{x^3}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2+\sqrt{3}}{/tex} 
={tex}\frac{1}{2+\sqrt{3}}\times\frac{2-\sqrt{3}}{2-\sqrt{3}} {/tex}
={tex}\frac{2-\sqrt{3}}{(2)^2-(\sqrt{3})^2} {/tex}
={tex}\frac{2-\sqrt{3}}{4-3} {/tex}
= {tex}{2-\sqrt{3}}{/tex} 
Hence {tex}\frac{1}{x^3}{/tex} ={tex}{2-\sqrt{3}}{/tex} ----(ii)
Then {tex}x^{3}+\frac{1}{x^{3}}{/tex}
= 2 + {tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex}+ 2 -{tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex} 
= 4

questions.question: The value of{tex}\sqrt{3-2 \sqrt{2}}, \text { is }{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\sqrt{3-2 \sqrt{2}}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{2+1-2 \sqrt{2}}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{(\sqrt2)^2+(1)^2-2(1)( \sqrt{2})}{/tex}
={tex}\sqrt{(\sqrt2-1)^2}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{2}-1{/tex}

questions.question: The value of {tex}\sqrt{5+2 \sqrt{6}}, \text { is }{/tex}

{tex}\sqrt{5+2 \sqrt{6}}{/tex}

={tex}\sqrt{(\sqrt{3})^2+(\sqrt{2} )^2+2 (\sqrt{2})({\sqrt{3})}}{/tex}
={tex}\sqrt{(\sqrt{2}+{\sqrt{3})^2}}{/tex}
={tex}\sqrt{3}+\sqrt{2}{/tex}

questions.question: If {tex}\sqrt{2}=1.414{/tex} and {tex}\sqrt{3}=1.732{/tex}  then the value of {tex}\sqrt{6}-\sqrt{3}{/tex}  upto three places of decimal is
questions.explanation:
Given {tex}\sqrt{2}=1.414{/tex} 
{tex}\sqrt{6}-\sqrt{3}{/tex}

={tex}\sqrt{2}\times\sqrt{3}-\sqrt{3}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{3}(\sqrt{2}-1){/tex}
={tex}\sqrt{3}(1.414-1){/tex}
= 1.732{tex}\times{/tex}0.414
=0.717

questions.question: The positive square root of 7 + {tex}\sqrt{48}{/tex}, is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\sqrt{7+\sqrt{48}}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{7+\sqrt{4\times4\times3} }{/tex}
={tex}\sqrt{7 + 4\sqrt{3}}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{4+3 + 4\sqrt{3}}{/tex}
={tex}\sqrt{(2)^2+(\sqrt3)^2 + 2(2)(\sqrt{3})}{/tex}
={tex}\sqrt{(2+\sqrt{3})^2}{/tex}
= {tex}2+\sqrt{3}{/tex}

questions.question: If x ={tex}\sqrt{6}+\sqrt{5}{/tex} , then {tex}x^{2}+\frac{1}{x^{2}}-2={/tex}
questions.explanation:
Given x ={tex}\sqrt{6}+\sqrt{5}{/tex} 
x2 = ({tex}\sqrt{6}+\sqrt{5}{/tex})2
= 6+5+2{tex}\sqrt{6}\sqrt{5}{/tex}
= 11+2{tex}\sqrt{30}{/tex} 
Hence x2 = 11+ 2{tex}\sqrt{30}{/tex}
Now,
{tex}\frac{1}{x^2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{11+2\sqrt{30}}{/tex}
={tex}\frac{1}{11+2\sqrt{30}}\times\frac{11-2\sqrt{30}}{11-2\sqrt{30}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{11-2\sqrt{30}}{(11)^2-(2\sqrt{30})^2}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{11-2\sqrt{30}}{121-120}{/tex}
= 11 - 2{tex}\sqrt{30}{/tex}
Hence {tex}\frac{1}{x^2}{/tex}= 11 - 2{tex}\sqrt{30}{/tex}
Then {tex}x^{2}+\frac{1}{x^{2}}-2{/tex}
= 11 + 2{tex}\sqrt{30}{/tex} + 11 - 2{tex}\sqrt{30}{/tex} -2
=22-2
=20

questions.question: If {tex}\sqrt{13-a \sqrt{10}}=\sqrt{8}+\sqrt{5}{/tex}, then a =
questions.explanation:
{tex}\sqrt{13-a \sqrt{10}}=\sqrt{8}+\sqrt{5}{/tex}
Squaring both sides we get,
{tex}(\sqrt{13-a \sqrt{10}})^2 {/tex} = {tex}(\sqrt{8}+\sqrt{5})^2 {/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}13-a \sqrt{10}{/tex} = 8 + 5+ 2 {tex}(\sqrt{8})(\sqrt{5}) {/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 13 - a{tex}\sqrt{10}{/tex} = 13 + 2{tex}\sqrt{40}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -a{tex}\sqrt{10}{/tex} = 2(2{tex}\sqrt{10}{/tex})
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -a{tex}\sqrt{10}{/tex} = 4 {tex}\sqrt{10}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = -4

questions.question: Which of the following is rational:
questions.explanation: Since 0 is rational number  it is in the form of {tex}\frac pq{/tex} , and where q {tex}\ne{/tex} 0 as {tex}\frac 01{/tex}

questions.question: If n is a natural number, then {tex}\sqrt{n}{/tex} is

options.option: sometimes a natural number and sometimes an irrational number

questions.explanation:
If n is a natural number, then {tex}\sqrt{n}{/tex} is
for eg. if n =2 (where 2 is natural number)
 then {tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex} is irrational
but if n=4 ( which is also a natural number)
then {tex}\sqrt{4}{/tex} = 2 
Which is rational
hence If n is a natural number, then {tex}\sqrt{n}{/tex} is sometimes a natural number and sometimes an irrational number

questions.question: The number {tex}0 . \overline{3}{/tex} in the form {tex}\frac{p}{q}{/tex}, where p and q are integers and q {tex}\neq{/tex} 0, is
questions.explanation:
Let x = {tex}0 . \overline{3}{/tex}
i,e, x = 0.333...---(i)
multiply eq.(i) by 10 we get, 
10 x = 3.333... ---(ii)
Subtracting eq. (i) from (ii) we get
10 x -x = 3.333... - 0.333...
9 x = 3
x = {tex}\frac{3}{9}{/tex}
x = {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex}

questions.question: The number of consecutive zeros in 23 {tex}\times{/tex} 34 {tex}\times{/tex} 54 {tex}\times{/tex} 7, is
questions.explanation:
The number of consecutive zeros in 23 {tex}\times{/tex} 34 {tex}\times{/tex} 54 {tex}\times{/tex} 7, is 3
As in the expression 23 {tex}\times{/tex} 34 {tex}\times{/tex} 54 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 has 23 {tex}\times{/tex} 53 which yields zeroes in expression.

questions.question: The smallest rational number by which {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} should be multiplied so that its decimal expansion terminates after one place of decimal, is
questions.explanation:
The smallest rational number by which {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} should be multiplied so that its decimal expansion terminates after one place of decimal, is {tex}\frac{3}{10}{/tex}
Since,  {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex}{tex}\frac{3}{10}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{10}{/tex}= 0.1
Since, among them {tex}\frac{3}{10}{/tex} is the only number which when multiplied, then its decimal expansion terminates after one place of decimal.

questions.question: If 8x+1 = 64, what is the value of 32x+1?
questions.explanation:
Given 8x+1 = 64
8x+1 = 64

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x=2-1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x=1
Now 32x+1 = 32(1)+1 
= 32+1
= 33 
= 27

questions.question: If (23)2 = 4x, then 3x =
questions.explanation:
(23)2 = 4x 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 23{tex}\times{/tex}2 = (22)x

Comparing,
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x = 6

Now 3x = (3)3 = 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 = 27

questions.question: If  x-2 = 64, then {tex}x^{\frac{1}{3}}{/tex} + x0 =
questions.explanation:
x-2 = 64 
{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} x-2 = 82 
{tex}\Rightarrow\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)^{2}{/tex} = (8)2
{tex}\therefore \frac{1}{x}{/tex} = 8 {tex}\Rightarrow x=\frac{1}{8}{/tex}
{tex}x^{\frac{1}{3}}+x^{0}=\left(\frac{1}{8}\right)^{\frac{1}{3}}{/tex} + 1
= {tex}\left[\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{3}\right]^{\frac{1}{3}}{/tex} + 1 = {tex}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{3 \times \frac{1}{3}}{/tex} + 1
= {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} + 1 = {tex}\frac{3}{2}{/tex}

questions.question: Simplify: {tex}\left(-\frac{1}{27}\right)^{\frac{-2}{3}}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\left(-\frac{1}{27}\right)^{\frac{-2}{3}} {/tex}
= {tex}\left[\left(-\frac{1}{3}\right)^{3}\right]^{\frac{-2}{3}} {/tex}
= {tex}\left(-\frac{1}{3}\right)^{3 \times \frac{-2}{3}} {/tex}
={tex}\left(-\frac{1}{3}\right)^{-2} {/tex}
= (-3)2 
= (-3) {tex}\times{/tex} (-3)
= 9

questions.question: Which one of the following is not equal to {tex}\left(\frac{100}{9}\right)^{\frac {-3} 2}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\left(\frac{100}{9}\right)^{\frac {-3} 2}{/tex}
= {tex}\left(\frac{9}{100}\right)^{\frac {3} 2}{/tex}
={tex}\left(\frac{3^2}{10^2}\right)^{\frac {3} 2}{/tex}
= {tex}\left(\frac{3}{10}\right)^{2\times{\frac {3} 2}}{/tex}
={tex}\left(\frac{3}{10}\right)^3{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{27}{1000}{/tex}
and 
{tex}\sqrt{\frac{100}{9} \times \frac{100}{9} \times \frac{100}{9}}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{\left(\frac{100}{9}\right)^{3}}=\left(\frac{100}{9}\right)^{\frac{3}{2}}{/tex}
= {tex}\left(\frac{10}{3}\right)^{2 \times \frac{3}{2}} {/tex}
={tex}\left(\frac{10}{3}\right)^{3}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1000}{27}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{27}{1000}{/tex} {tex}\neq{/tex} {tex}\frac{1000}{27}{/tex}

questions.question: If {tex}\left(\frac{2}{3}\right)^{x}\left(\frac{3}{2}\right)^{2 x}=\frac{81}{16}{/tex}, then x =
questions.explanation:
{tex}\left(\frac{2}{3}\right)^{x}\left(\frac{3}{2}\right)^{2 x}=\frac{81}{16}{/tex}
{tex}\left(\frac{3}{2}\right)^{-x}\left(\frac{3}{2}\right)^{2 x}=(\frac{3^4}{2^4}){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left(\frac{3}{2}\right)^{-x+2x}=(\frac{3}{2})^4{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left(\frac{3}{2}\right)^{x}=(\frac{3}{2})^4{/tex}
Comparing, we get
x = 4

questions.question: The value of {tex}\left\{8^{\frac {-4} 3} \div 2^{-2}\right\}^{\frac 1 2}{/tex}, is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\left\{8^{\frac {-4} 3} \div 2^{-2}\right\}^{\frac 1 2}{/tex}
= {tex}\left[\left(2^{3}\right)^{\frac{-4}{3}} \div 2^{-2}\right]^{\frac{1}{2}}\\{/tex}
= {tex}\left[2^{3 \times \frac{-4}{3}} \div 2^{-2}\right]^{\frac{1}{2}}\\ {/tex}
= {tex}\left[2^{-4} \div 2^{-2}\right]^{\frac{1}{2}}\\ {/tex}
={tex}\left[2^{-4-(-2)} \right]^{\frac{1}{2}}\\ {/tex}
={tex}\left[2^{-4+2} \right]^{\frac{1}{2}}\\ {/tex}
= {tex}\left[2^{-2} \right]^{\frac{1}{2}}\\ {/tex}
= {tex} \left(\frac{1}{2^{2}}\right)^{\frac{1}{2}}\\{/tex}
={tex} \left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{2\times\frac{1}{2}}\\{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}

questions.question: The value of m for which {tex}\left[\left\{\left(\frac{1}{7^{2}}\right)^{-2}\right\}^{\frac {-1} 3}\right]^{\frac1 4}{/tex} = 7m, is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\left[\left\{\left(\frac{1}{7^{2}}\right)^{-2}\right\}^{\frac {-1} 3}\right]^{\frac1 4}{/tex} = 7m
{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex}{tex}\left. \left[\left(7^{-2}\right)^{-2}\right\}^{-\frac{1}{3}}\right]^{\frac{1}{4}}{/tex} = 7m
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}\left[\left\{7^{-2(-2)}\right\}^{\frac{-1}{3}}\right]^{\frac{1}{4}}{/tex} = 7m
{tex}\Rightarrow\left\{7^{4 \times \frac{-1}{3}}\right\}^{\frac{1}{4}}=7^{m} {/tex} 
{tex} \Rightarrow\left\{7^{\frac{-4}{3} \times \frac{1}{4}}\right\} {/tex} = 7m
{tex}\Rightarrow 7^{\frac{-1}{3}}{/tex} = 7m
Comparing both sides,
m = {tex}-\frac{1}{3}{/tex}

questions.question: The value of {tex}\left\{\left(23+2^{2}\right)^{\frac 2 3}+(150-29)^{\frac 1 2}\right\}^{2}{/tex}, is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\left\{\left(23+2^{2}\right)^{\frac 2 3}+(150-29)^{\frac 1 2}\right\}^{2}{/tex}
= {tex} \left[(23 + 4)^{\frac 2 3}+(150-29)^{\frac 1 2}\right]^{2}{/tex}
= {tex}\left[(27)^{\frac{2}{3}}+(121)^{\frac{1}{2}}\right]^{2}{/tex}
= {tex}\left[\left(3^{3}\right)^{\frac{2}{3}}+\left(11^{2}\right)^{\frac 1{2}}\right]^{2}{/tex}
 = (9 + 11)2
= (20)2 
= 400

questions.question: If 102y = 25, then 10-y equals
questions.explanation:
102y = 25
102y =  52
(10y)2 = (5)2 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 10y = 5
Now 10-y
= {tex}\frac{1}{10^{y}} {/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{5} {/tex}

questions.question: If 9x+2 = 240 + 9x, then x =
questions.explanation:
9x+2 = 240 + 9x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 9x+2 - 9x = 240
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}9^{x} \cdot{/tex} 92 - 9x = 240
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 9x(92 - 1) = 240
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 9x(81 - 1) = 240  
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}9^{x} \times{/tex} 80 = 240
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 9x = {tex}\frac{240}{80}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (32)x = 31
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 32x = 31
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 2x = 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} = 0.5

questions.question: If x is a positive real number and x2 = 2, then x3 =
questions.explanation:
x2 = 2 
{tex}\Rightarrow x=\sqrt{2}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore x^{3}{/tex} = x2 . x
= {tex}2 \times \sqrt{2} {/tex}
={tex}2 \sqrt{2} {/tex}

questions.question: If {tex}\frac{x}{x^{1.5}}{/tex} = 8x-1 and x > 0, then x =
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{x}{x^{1.5}}{/tex} = 8x-1
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{x}{x^{1.5}}=\frac{8}{x} {/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex} \frac{x^{2}}{x^{15}} {/tex} = 8
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x 2-1.5 = 8 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x0.5 = 8
{tex}\Rightarrow x^{\frac{1}{2}}{/tex} = 8
Squaring both sides we get
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = (8)2 
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 64

questions.question: If g = {tex}t^{\frac 2 3} + 4t^ {\frac {-1} 2}{/tex}, what is the value of g when t = 64?
questions.explanation:
g = {tex}t^{\frac 2 3} + 4t^ {\frac {-1} 2}{/tex}
= {tex}t^{\frac{2}{3}}{/tex} + 4 {tex}\times \frac{1}{t^{\frac{1}{2}}}{/tex}
= {tex}(64)^{\frac{2}{3}}{/tex} + 4 {tex}\times \frac{1}{64^{\frac{1}{2}}}{/tex}
= {tex}\left(4^{3}\right)^{\frac{2}{3}}{/tex} + 4 {tex}\times \frac{1}{\left(8^{2}\right)^{\frac{1}{2}}} {/tex}
= {tex}4^{\frac{2}{3} \times 3}{/tex} + 4 {tex}\times \frac{1}{8^{2 \times \frac{1}{2}}} {/tex}
= 42 + {tex}\frac{4}{8} {/tex}
= 16 + {tex}\frac{1}{2} {/tex}
= {tex}\frac{33}{2} {/tex}

questions.question: If 4x - 4x-1 = 24, then (2x)x equals
questions.explanation:
4x - 4x-1 = 24 
{tex}\Rightarrow 4^{x}-\frac{4^{x}}{4^{1}}{/tex} = 24
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4x{tex}\left(1-\frac{1}{4}\right){/tex} = 24 
 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4x{tex}\left(\frac{3}{4}\right) {/tex}= 24
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4x ={tex}\frac{24\times4}{3}{/tex}
 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4x = 32
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (22)x = (2)5
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 22x = 25
Comparing, we get
2x = 5 {tex}\Rightarrow x=\frac{5}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} (2x)x {tex}\left(2 \times \frac{5}{2}\right)^{\frac{5}{2}}=(5)^{\frac{5}{2}}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{5^{5}}=\sqrt{5 \times 5 \times 5 \times 5 \times 5}{/tex}
= {tex}5 \times 5 \sqrt{5}=25 \sqrt{5}{/tex}

questions.question: When simplified {tex}(256)^{-\left(4^{\frac {-3} 2}\right)}{/tex}, is
questions.explanation:
{tex}(256)^{-\left(4^{\frac {-3} 2}\right)}{/tex}
={tex}(256)^{-(2^2)^{\frac{-3}{2}}}{/tex}
 = {tex}(256)^{-(2)^{2\times\frac{-3}{2}}}{/tex}
={tex}(256)^{-(2)^{-3}}{/tex}
= {tex}(256)^{-(\frac{1}{2})^{3}}{/tex}
= {tex}(256)^{-(\frac{1}{8})}{/tex}
={tex}(2^8)^{-(\frac{1}{8})}{/tex}
= {tex}(2)^{-(8\times\frac{1}{8})}{/tex}
= 2-1
={tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}

questions.question: If {tex}\frac{3^{2 x-8}}{225}=\frac{5^{3}}{5^{x}}{/tex}, then x =
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{3^{2 x-8}}{225}=\frac{5^{3}}{5^{x}} {/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 32x-8 {tex}\times{/tex} 5x = 53 {tex}\times{/tex} 225 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{3^{2 x}}{3^{8}} \times{/tex} 5x = 53 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 32x {tex}\times{/tex} 5x = 38 {tex}\times{/tex} 32 {tex}\times{/tex} 55
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (32)x {tex}\times{/tex} 5x = 310 {tex}\times{/tex} 55
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 9x {tex}\times{/tex} 5x = 95 {tex}\times{/tex} 55
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (45)x = (45)5
Comparing, we get
x = 5

questions.question: The value of {tex}64^{\frac {-1} 3}\left(64^{\frac 1 3}-64^{\frac 2 3}\right){/tex}, is
questions.explanation:
{tex}64^{\frac {-1} 3}\left(64^{\frac 1 3}-64^{\frac 2 3}\right){/tex}
= {tex}\left(4^{3}\right)^{\frac{-1}{3}}\left[4^{3 \times \frac{1}{3}}-(4)^{3 \times \frac{2}{3}}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}4^{3 \times\left(\frac{-1}{3}\right)}\left[4^{3 \times \frac{1}{3}}-4^{3 \times \frac{2}{3}}\right]{/tex}
= 4-1[41 - 42]
= {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex}[4 - 16]
= {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex}(-12)
= -3

questions.question: If {tex}\sqrt{5^{n}}{/tex} = 125, then {tex}5^{\sqrt[n]{64}}{/tex} =
questions.explanation:
{tex}\sqrt{5^{n}}{/tex} = 125
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} {tex} 5^{\frac{n}{2}}{/tex} = 53
Comparing, we get
{tex}\frac{n}{2}{/tex} = 3 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n = 6
Now
{tex}5^{\sqrt[n]{64}}{/tex}
= {tex}5^{\sqrt[6]{64}}{/tex}
= {tex}5^{{(64)}^\frac{1}{6}}{/tex}
= {tex}5^{\left(2^{6}\right)^{\frac{1}{6}}} {/tex}
= {tex}5^{2^{6 \times \frac{1}{6}}} {/tex}
= 52
= 25

questions.question: If (16)2x+3 = (64)x+3, then 42x-2 =
questions.explanation:
(16)2x+3 = (64)x+3 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (24)2x+3 = (26)x+3
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 28x+12 = 26x+18
Comparing, we get
8x + 12 = 6x +18
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 8x - 6x = 18 - 12
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x = 6 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{6}{2}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x=3
Now 42x-2 = 42{tex}\times{/tex}(3)-2 = 46-2 = 44
= 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 = 256

questions.question: If 2-m {tex}\times \frac{1}{2^{m}}=\frac{1}{4}{/tex}, then {tex}\frac{1}{14}\left\{\left(4^{m}\right)^{\frac 1 2}+\left(\frac{1}{5^{m}}\right)^{-1}\right\}{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
2-m {tex}\times \frac{1}{2^{m}}=\frac{1}{4}{/tex},
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2-m-m = {tex}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{2} {/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} 2-2m = 2-2
Comparing, we get
-2m = -2 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} m = {tex}\frac{-2}{-2}{/tex} 
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} m = 1
Now, {tex}\frac{1}{14}\left\{\left(4^{m}\right)^{\frac 1 2}+\left(\frac{1}{5^{m}}\right)^{-1}\right\}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{14}\left[\left\{\left(2^{2}\right)^{m}\right\}^{\frac{1}{2}}+\left(5^{-m}\right)^{-1}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{14}\left\{2^{2 x m \times \frac{1}{2}}+5^{-m \times(-1)}\right\}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{14}{/tex} {2m + 5+m}
= {tex}\frac{1}{14}{/tex} {21 + 51}
= {tex}\frac{1}{14} \times{/tex} 7 = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}

questions.question: If {tex}\frac{3^{5 x} \times 81^{2} \times 6561}{3^{2 x}}{/tex} = 37, then x =
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{3^{5 x} \times 81^{2} \times 6561}{3^{2 x}}{/tex} = 37
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 35x {tex}\times{/tex} 812 {tex}\times{/tex} 6561 = 37 {tex}\times{/tex} 32x

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 35x {tex}\times{/tex} (34)2 {tex}\times{/tex} 38 = 37 {tex}\times{/tex} 32x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 35x+8+8 = 37+2x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5x + 16 = 7 + 2x

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x = -9
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}  x = -3

{tex}\therefore \sqrt{9+2 x}-\sqrt{2 x}=\frac{5}{\sqrt{9+2 x}}{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow(\sqrt{9+2 x})^{2}-\sqrt{2 x(9+2 x)}{/tex} = 5
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 9 + 2x - 5 = {tex}\sqrt{2 x(9+2 x)}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4 + 2x = {tex}\sqrt{2 x(9+2 x)}{/tex}
Squaring both sides,
(4 + 2x)2 = {tex}(\sqrt{2 x(9+2 x)})^{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 16 + 4x2 + 16x = 18x + 4x2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 16 = 2x

  3. There are infinite irrational numbers

questions.question: Which of the following is an irrational number?
The value of{tex}\frac{a+\sqrt{a^2-b^2}}{a-\sqrt{a^2-b^2}}+\frac{a-\sqrt{a^2-b^2}}{a+\sqrt{a^2-b^2}}{/tex}is ________.
questions.explanation:
We have,{tex}\frac{a+\sqrt{a^2-b^2}}{a-\sqrt{a^2-b^2}}+\frac{a-\sqrt{a^2-b^2}}{a+\sqrt{a^2-b^2}}{/tex}
Rationalising both terms, we get
{tex}\frac{\left(a+\sqrt{a^2-b^2}\right)^2}{a^2-a^2+b^2}+\frac{\left(a-\sqrt{a^2-b^2}\right)^2}{a^2-a^2+b^2}{/tex}
={tex}\frac{1}{b^2}{/tex}{tex}\left[a^2+a^2-b^2+2 a \sqrt{a^2-b^2}\right.{/tex}{tex}\left.+a^2+a^2-b^2-2 a \sqrt{a^2-b^2}\right]{/tex}
={tex}\frac{2}{b^2}{/tex}(2a2 - b2)

questions.question: The rationalising factor of{tex}\sqrt[7]{x^3 y^5 z^2}{/tex}is ________.
questions.explanation: {tex}\sqrt[7]{z^5 y^2 x^4}{/tex}

questions.question: The value of x, if 52x - 1 = 25x - 1 + 100 is ________.
questions.explanation:
We have, 52x - 1 = 25x - 1 + 100
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 52x - 1 - 52(x - 1) = 100
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{5^{2 x}}{5}-\frac{5^{2 x}}{25}=100{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 5^{2 \times}\left(\frac{1}{5}-\frac{1}{25}\right)=100{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 5^{2 x}=\frac{100 \times 25}{4}{/tex} = 625 = 54
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x= 4 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 2

questions.question: If {tex}\frac{3+2 \sqrt{5}}{3-2 \sqrt{5}}=(p+q \sqrt{5}){/tex}, then find the value of 11(p + q).
questions.explanation:
We have, p + q{tex}\sqrt{5}=\frac{3+2 \sqrt{5}}{3-2 \sqrt{5}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{3+2 \sqrt{5}}{3-2 \sqrt{5}} \times \frac{3+2 \sqrt{5}}{3+2 \sqrt{5}}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{9+20+12 \sqrt{5}}{9-20}=-\frac{29}{11}-\frac{12}{11} \sqrt{5}{/tex}
On comparing, we get p = {tex}\frac{-29}{11} {/tex} and q = {tex}\frac{-12}{11}{/tex}
Now, 11 (p + q) = 11{tex}\left(\frac{-29}{11}-\frac{12}{11}\right){/tex} = -41

questions.question: The number x = 1.242424....... can be expressed in the form x = {tex}\frac{p}{q}{/tex}, where p and q are positive integers having no common factors. Then p + q equals
questions.explanation:
We have, x = 1.2424... ...(i)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 100x= 124.2424... ...(ii)
Subtracting (i) from (ii), we get
100x - x = 123
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 99x = 123 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{123}{99}=\frac{41}{33}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} p = 41, q = 33 and p + q = 41 + 33 = 74

questions.question: If x = 1 - {tex}\sqrt2{/tex}, then find the value of {tex}\left(x-\frac{1}{x}\right)^2{/tex}
questions.explanation:
We have, x = 1 - {tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \frac{1}{x}=\frac{1}{1-\sqrt{2}} \times \frac{1+\sqrt{2}}{1+\sqrt{2}}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1+\sqrt{2}}{1-2}=-(1+\sqrt{2}){/tex}
Now, {tex}\left(x-\frac{1}{x}\right)^2=(1-\sqrt{2}+1+\sqrt{2})^2{/tex} = (2)2 = 4

questions.question: The value of {tex}\frac{1}{1+\sqrt{2}}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}+\sqrt{3}}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}+\sqrt{4}}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{4}+\sqrt{5}}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{5}+\sqrt{6}}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{6}+\sqrt{7}}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{7}+\sqrt{8}}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{8}+\sqrt{9}}{/tex} is ________.
questions.explanation:
We have,
{tex}\frac{1}{1+\sqrt{2}}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}+\sqrt{3}}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}+\sqrt{4}}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{4}+\sqrt{5}}{/tex} {tex}+ \frac{1}{\sqrt{5}+\sqrt{6}}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{6}+\sqrt{7}}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{7}+\sqrt{8}}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{8}+\sqrt{9}}{/tex}
On rationalising each of the above number separately, we get
{tex}\frac{1-\sqrt{2}}{1-2}+\frac{\sqrt{2}-\sqrt{3}}{2-3}+\frac{\sqrt{3}-\sqrt{4}}{3-4}+\frac{\sqrt{4}-\sqrt{5}}{4-5}{/tex} {tex}+ \frac{\sqrt{5}-\sqrt{6}}{5-6}+\frac{\sqrt{6}-\sqrt{7}}{6-7}+\frac{\sqrt{7}-\sqrt{8}}{7-8}+\frac{\sqrt{8}-\sqrt{9}}{8-9}{/tex}
{tex}=-(1-\sqrt{2})-(\sqrt{2}-\sqrt{3})-(\sqrt{3}-\sqrt{4})-(\sqrt{4}-\sqrt{5})-(\sqrt{5}-\sqrt{6}){/tex} {tex}-(\sqrt{6}-\sqrt{7})-(\sqrt{7}-\sqrt{8})-(\sqrt{8}-\sqrt{9}){/tex}
{tex}=-1+\sqrt{9}{/tex} = -1 + 3 = 2

questions.question:
Read the statements carefully.
Statement-1: The product of a rational and an irrational number is an irrational number.
Statement 2: Reciprocal of every rational number is a rational number.

options.option: Statement-1 is false but Statement-2 is true.
Statement-1 is true but Statement-2 is false.

questions.explanation: Statement-1 is true and statement-2 is false as 0 is a rational number but {tex}\frac{1}{0}{/tex} is not defined.

questions.question:
Match the following.

Column-I Column-ll
(a) If {tex}\frac{3}{x+8}=\frac{4}{6-x}{/tex}, then x is ________. (i) 3
(b) If {tex}\frac{2^{x-1} \cdot 4^{2 x+1}}{8^{x-1}}=64{/tex}, then x is ________. (ii) 55/2
(c) If 4x - 4 x-1 = 24, then (2x)x is ________. (iii) -2
(d) If 4x + 1 = 256, then x is ________. (iv) 1
questions.explanation:
1. (a) We have, {tex}\frac{3}{x+8}=\frac{4}{6-x}{/tex}
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 18 - 3x = 4x + 32 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 7x = -14 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = -2
  2. We have, {tex}\frac{2^{x-1} \cdot 4^{2 x+1}}{8^{x-1}}{/tex} = 64
    {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{2^x \cdot 2^{-1} \cdot 4^{2 x} \cdot 4}{8^x \cdot 8^{-1}}=64{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{1}{4^x} \cdot 4^{2 x} \cdot 16=64 \Rightarrow 4^x=\frac{64}{16}=4{/tex}
    On comparing, we get x = 1
  3. We have, 4x - 4x-1 = 24
    {tex}\Rightarrow 4^x-\frac{4^x}{4}=24 \Rightarrow 4^x\left(1-\frac{1}{4}\right)=24 {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\left(2^2\right)^x=\frac{24 \times 4}{3}=32{/tex}
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 22x = 25
    On comparing, we get 2x = 5 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{5}{2}{/tex}
    Now, (2x)x = (5)5/2
  4. We have, 4x+1 = 256
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4x+1 = (4)4
    On comparing, we get x + 1 = 4 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 3

questions.question: If, {tex}x = \frac{a-b}{a+b}, {/tex} {tex}y=\frac{b-c}{b+c}, {/tex} {tex}z=\frac{c-a}{c+a}{/tex} then the value of {tex}\frac{(1+x)(1+y)(1+z)}{(1-x)(1-y)(1-z)}{/tex} is ________.
questions.explanation:
We have,
{tex}x=\frac{a-b}{a+b}, y=\frac{b-c}{b+c}, z=\frac{c-a}{c+a}{/tex}
Now, 1 + x {tex}=1+\frac{a-b}{a+b}=\frac{a+b+a-b}{a+b}=\frac{2 a}{a+b}{/tex}
1 + x {tex}=1+\frac{a-b}{a+b}=\frac{a+b+a-b}{a+b}=\frac{2 a}{a+b}{/tex}
Similarly, 1 + y = {tex}\frac{2 b}{b+c}, 1+z=\frac{2 c}{a+c}, {/tex} {tex}1-y=\frac{2 c}{b+c}, 1-z=\frac{2 a}{a+c}{/tex}
Now, {tex}\frac{(1+x)(1+y)(1+z)}{(1-x)(1-y)(1-z)}=\frac{\left(\frac{2 a}{a+b}\right)\left(\frac{2 b}{b+c}\right)\left(\frac{2 c}{a+c}\right)}{\left(\frac{2 b}{a+b}\right)\left(\frac{2 c}{b+c}\right)\left(\frac{2 a}{a+c}\right)}{/tex} = 1

questions.question: Value of R, if {tex}\frac{a^2-19 a-25}{a-7}=a-12+\frac{R}{a-7}{/tex} is ________.
questions.explanation:
We have, {tex}\frac{a^2-19 a-25}{a-7}=a-12+\frac{R}{a-7}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{a^2-19 a-25}{a-7}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{(a-12)(a-7)+R}{a-7}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a2 -19a - 25 = a2 - 19a + 84 + R
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} - 84 - 25 = R {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} R = -109

questions.question: The ascending order of the surds {tex}\sqrt[3]{2}, \sqrt[6]{3}, \sqrt[9]{4}{/tex} is ________.
questions.explanation:
LCM of 3, 6, 9 = 18
Now, reduce the given surds to surds of order 18.
{tex}\therefore \sqrt[3]{2}=(2)^{\frac{1}{3} \times \frac{6}{6}}=\left(2^6\right)^{\frac{1}{18}}{/tex} {tex}=(64)^{\frac{1}{18}}=\sqrt[18]{64}{/tex}
{tex}\sqrt[6]{3}=(3)^{\frac{1}{6} \times \frac{3}{3}}=\left(3^3\right)^{\frac{1}{18}}=(27)^{\frac{1}{18}}=\sqrt[18]{27}{/tex}
{tex}\sqrt[9]{4}=(4)^{\frac{1}{9} \times \frac{2}{2}}=\left(4^2\right)^{\frac{1}{18}}=(16)^{\frac{1}{18}}=\sqrt[18]{16}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \sqrt[9]{4}<\sqrt[6]{3}<\sqrt[3]{2}{/tex}

questions.question: The greater number among {tex}\sqrt{17}-\sqrt{12}{/tex} and {tex}\sqrt{11}-\sqrt{6}{/tex} is ________.

and {tex}\sqrt{17}-\sqrt{12}{/tex} = 4.1 - 3.5 = 0.6
So, {tex}\sqrt{11}-\sqrt{6}{/tex} is greater.

questions.question: A real number k is said to be a zero of a polynomial p(x), if p(k) =
questions.explanation:
A real number ‘k’ is said to be a zero of a polynomial p(x), if p(k) equals to 0.
Explanation: if P(x) is a polynomial in x and k is any real number, then the value of P(k) at x = k is denoted by P(k) is found by replacing x by k in P(x).
e.g., In the polynomial x2 - 3x + 2,
Replacing x by 1 gives,
P(1) = 1 - 3 + 2 = 0
Similarly, replacing x by 2 gives,
P(2) = 4 - 6 + 2 = 0
For a polynomial P(x), real number k is said to be zero of polynomial P(x), if P(k) = 0.

c2 - 2 b2 - bc = 0   ..... 3

  =  (x -2 y + 2 ) (x -2 y - 4)   OR   (x -2 y - 4) (x -2 y + 2 )

questions.question: If x2 - 1 is a factor of ax4 + bx3 + cx2 + dx + e, then
questions.explanation:
As (x2 - 1) is a factor of polynomial
f(x2) = ax4 + bx3 + cx2 + dx + e
Therefore,
f(x) = 0
And
f(1) = 0
a(1)4 + b(1)3 + c(1)2 + d(1) + e = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a + b + c + d + e = 0
And
f(-1) = 0
a(-1)4 + b(-1)3 + c(-1)2 + d(-1) + e = 0
a - b + c - d + e = 0
Hence, a + c + e = b + d

questions.question: One factor of x4 + x2 - 20 is x2 + 5. The other factor is
questions.explanation:
It is given that x2 + 5 is a factor of the polynomial
f(x)2 = x4 + x2 - 20

Here, the remainder is zero. Therefore, x2 - 4 is a factor of the polynomial.

Now,

questions.explanation:
As (x - 2) is a factor of f(x) = x2 + 3ax - 2a
i.e. f(2) = 0

questions.question: If x + 2 and x -1 are the factors of x3 + 10x2 + mx + n, then the values of m and n are respectively.
questions.explanation:
It is given (x + 2) and (x - 1) are the factors of the polynomial
f(x) = x3 + 10x2 + mx + n
i.e., f(-2) = 0 and f(1) = 0
Now
f(-2) = (-2)3 + 10(-2)2 + m(-2) + n = 0
-8 + 40 - 2m + n = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -2m + n = -32
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2m - n = 32 ..... (i)
f(1) = (1)3 + 10(1)2 + m(1) + n = 0
1 + 10 + m + n = 0
m + n = -11 ..... (ii)
Solving equation (i) and (ii) we get
m = 7 and n = -18

questions.question: Let f(x) be a polynomial such that {tex}f\left(-\frac{1}{2}\right)=0{/tex}, then a factor of f(x) is
questions.explanation:
Let f(x) be a polynomial such that {tex}f\left(-\frac{1}{2}\right)=0{/tex}
i.e., {tex}x+\frac{1}{2}=0{/tex} is a factor.
On rearranging {tex}x+\frac{1}{2}=0{/tex} can be written as (2x + 1) = 0
Thus, (2x + 1) is a factor of f(x).

questions.question: When x3 - 2x2 + ax- b is divided by x2 - 2x - 3 , the remainder is x - 6 . The values of a and b are respectively.
questions.explanation:
If the remainder (x - 6) is subtracted from the given polynomial f(x) = x3 - 2x2 + ax - b,
then rest part of this polynomial is exactly divisible by x2 - 2x - 3.
Therefore, p(x) = x3 - 2x2 + ax - b - (x - 6)
Now,
x2 - 2x - 3 = x2 - 3x + x - 3
x2 - 2x - 3 = (x + 1)(x - 3)
Therefore, (x + 1)(x - 3) are factors of polynomial p(x).
Now,
p(-1) = 0
And
p(3) = 0
p(-1) = (-1)2 + a(-1) - b - (-1 - 6) = 0
= -1 - 2 - a - b + 1 + 6 = 0
= -a - b + 4 = 0
a + b = 4 ..... (i)
and
p(3) = (3)3 - 2(3)3 + a(3) - b(3 - 6) = 0
= 27 - 18 + 3a - b + 3 = 0
3a - b = -12 ..... (ii)
Solving (i) and (ii) we get
a = -2, b = 6

questions.question: (x + 1) is a factor of xn +1 only if
questions.explanation:
The linear polynomial (x - 1) is a factor of xn + 1, only if
f(-1) = (-1)n + 1 = 0
If n is odd integer, then f(-1) = -1 + 1 = 0

questions.question: The factors of x3 - 7x - 6, are
questions.explanation:
The given expression to be factorized is x3 - 7x + 6
This can be written in the form
x3 - 7x + 6 = x3 - (1 + 6)x + 6
= x3 - x - 6x + 6
Take common x from the first two terms and -6 from the last two terms. Then we have
x3 + 7x + 6 = x(x2 - 1) - 6(x - 1)
= x{(x)2 - (1)2} - 6{x - 1}
= x(x + 1)(x - 1) - 6(x - 1)
Finally, take common (x - 1) from the above expression,
x3 - 7x + 6 = (x - 1){(x + 1) - 6}
= (x - 1)(x2 + x - 6)
= (x - 1) (x2 + 3x - 2x - 6)
= (x - 1){x(x + 3) - 2(x + 3)}
= (x - 1)(x + 3)(x - 2)

questions.question: The factors of x4 + x2 + 25, are
questions.explanation:
The given expression to be factorized is x4 + x2 + 25
This can be written in the form
x4 + x2 + 25 = (x2)2 {tex}+\text { 2. } x^{2} .5+(5)^{2}-9 x^{2}{/tex}
{tex}=\left\{\left(x^{2}\right)^{2}+2 x^{2} .5+(5)^{2}\right\}-(3 x)^{2}{/tex}
= (x2 + 5)2 - (3x)2
= (x2 + 5)2 - (3x)2
= (x2 + 5 + 3x) (x2 + 5 - 3x)

questions.question: The factors of x3 -1 + y3 + 3xy are
questions.explanation:
The given expression to be factorized is x3 - 1 + y3 + 3xy
This can be written in the form
x3 - 1 + y3 + 3xy = (x)2 + (-1)3 + (y)3 - 3(x).(-1).(y)
Recall the formula a3 + b3 + c3 - 3abc = (a + b + c) (a2 + b2 + c2 - ab - bc - ca)
Using the above formula, we have,
x3 - 1 + y3 + 3xy
= (x + (-1) + y} {(x)2 + (-1)2 + (y)2 - (x).(-1) - (-1).(y) - (y).(x)}
= (x - 1 + y)(x2 + 1 + y2 + x + y - xy)

questions.question: If (x + y)3 - ( x - y )3 - 6y (x2 - y2) = ky3, then k =
questions.explanation:
We have,
= (x + y)3 - (x - y)3 - 6y(x2 - y2) = ky3
= (x + y - x + y)3 + 3(x + y)(x - y) (x + y - x + y) - 6y(x2 - y2) = ky3
= 2y3 + 6y(x2 - y2) - 6y(x2 - y2) = ky3
= 8y3 = ky3
= k = 8

questions.question: The factors of x3 - x2y - xy2 + y3 , are
questions.explanation:
The given expression to be factorized is x3 - x2y - xy2 + y3
Take common x2 from the first two terms and -y2 from the last two terms. That is
x3 - x2y - xy2 + y3 = x2(x - y) - y2(x - y)
Finally, take common (x - y) from the two terms. That is
x3 - x2y - xy2 + y3 = (x - y)(x2 - y2)
= (x - y){(x2 - y2)}
= (x - y)(x + y(x - y)
= (x - y)2(x + y)

questions.question: The value of {tex}\frac{(2.3)^{3}-0.027}{(2.3)^{2}+0.69+0.09}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
The given expression is
{tex}\frac{(2.3)^{3}-0.027}{(2.3)^{2}+0.69+0.09}{/tex}
This can be written in the form
{tex}\frac{(2.3)^{3}-(0.3)^{3}}{(2.3)^{2}+2.3 \times 0.3+(0.3)^{2}}{/tex}
Assume a = 2.3 and b = 0.3. Then the given expression can be rewritten as
{tex}\frac{a^{3}-b^{3}}{a^{2}+a b+b^{2}}{/tex}
Recall the formula for difference of two cubes
a3 - b3 = (a - b)(a2 + ab + b2)
Using the above formula, the expression becomes
{tex}\frac{(a-b)\left(a^{2}+a b+b^{2}\right)}{a^{2}+a b+b^{2}}{/tex}
Note that both a and b are positive, unequal. So, neither a3 -b3 nor any factor of it can be zero.
Therefore we can cancel the term (a2 + ab + b2) from both numerator and denominator. Then the expression becomes
{tex}\frac{(a-b)\left(a^{2}+a b+b^{2}\right)}{a^{2}+a b+b^{2}}=a-b{/tex}
= 2.3 - 0.3
= 2

questions.question: (x + y)3 - (x - y)3 can be factorized as
questions.explanation:
put a=x+y and b=x-y,then
{tex}(x+y)^3-(x-y)^3=a^3-b^3{/tex}
{tex}=(a-b)(a^2+b^2+ab){/tex}
{tex}=(x+y-x+y)[(x+y)^2+(x-y)^2+(x-y)(x+y)]{/tex} 
{tex}=2y[2(x^2+y^2)+(x^2-y^2)]{/tex}
{tex}=2y[3x^2+y^2]{/tex}

questions.question: The value of {tex}\frac{(0.013)^{3}+(0.007)^{3}}{(0.013)^{2}-0.013 \times 0.007+(0.007)^{2}}, \text { is }{/tex}
questions.explanation:
Assume a = 0.013 and b = 0.007. Then the given expression can be rewritten as
{tex}\frac{a^3 + b^{3}}{a^{2}-a b+b^{2}}{/tex}
Recall the formula for sum of two cubes
a3 + b3 = (a + b)(a2 - ab + b2)
Using the above formula, the expression becomes
{tex}\frac{(a+b)\left(a^{2}-a b+b^{2}\right)}{a^{2}-a b+b^{2}}{/tex}
Note that both a and b are positive. So, neither a3 + b3 nor any factor of it can be zero.
Therefore we can cancel the term (a2 - ab + b2) from both numerator and denominator. Then the expression becomes
{tex}\frac{(a+b)\left(a^{2}-a b+b^{2}\right)}{a^{2}-a b+b^{2}}=a+b{/tex}
= 0.013 + 0.007
= 0.02

questions.question: The factors of a2 - 1 - 2x - x2, are
questions.explanation:
The given expression to be factorized is a2 - 1 - 2x - x2
Take common -1 from the last three terms and then we have
a2 - 1 - 2x - x2 = a2 - (1 + 2x + x3)
= a2 - {(1)2 + 2.1.x + (x)2}
= a2 - (1 + x)2
= (a)2 - (1 + x)2
= {a + (1 + x)}{a - (1 + x)}
= (a + 1 + x)(a - 1 - x)
= (a + x + 1)(a - x - 1)

questions.question: The expression x4 + 4 can be factorized as
questions.explanation:
The given expression to be factorized is x4 + 4
This can be written in the form
x4 + 4 = (x2)2 + (2)2 + 4x2 - 4x2
{tex}=\left\{\left(x^{2}\right)^{2}+2 . x^{2} .2+(2)^{2}\right\}-(2 x)^{2}{/tex}
= (x2 + 2)2 - (2x)2
= (x2 + 2 + 2x) (x2 + 2 - 2x)

Therefore, x = a

a3 - 3a3 + 2a3 + b = 0
-3a3 + 3a3 + b = 0
b = 0

questions.question: If {tex}\left(3 x+\frac{1}{2}\right)\left(3 x-\frac{1}{2}\right){/tex} = 9x2 - p then the value of p is
questions.explanation:

{tex}\Rightarrow 9 x^{2}-\frac{1}{4}{/tex} = 9x2 - p
{tex}\Rightarrow p=\frac{1}{4}{/tex}

questions.question: The zeros of the polynomial p(x) = 3x2 - 1 are
questions.explanation:

To find the zeroes of p(x), we have:

questions.explanation:

questions.question: For what value of k is the polynomial p(x) = 2x3 - kx2 + 3x +10 exactly divisible by (x + 2)?
questions.explanation:
Let: p(x) = 2x3 − kx2 + 3x + 10
Now, x + 2 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = -2
p(x) is completely divisible by (x + 2)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} p(-2) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} (-2)3 - k {tex}\times{/tex} (-2)2 + 3 {tex}\times{/tex} (-2) + 10 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -16 - 4k - 6 + 10 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -12 - 4k = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4k = -12
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} k = {tex}\frac{-12}{4}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} k = −3

questions.question: If (x100 + 2x99 + k) is divisible by (x + 1) then the value of k is
questions.explanation:

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1 - 2 + k = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -1 + k = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} k = 1

questions.question: The zeros of the polynomial p(x) = 2x2 + 7x - 4 are
questions.explanation:
The given polynomial is p(x) = 2x2 + 7x - 4
Putting x = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} in p(x), we get
p{tex}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right){/tex} = 2 {tex}\times\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{2}{/tex} + 7 {tex}\times \frac{1}{2}{/tex} - 4 = {tex}\frac{1}{2}+\frac{7}{2}{/tex} - 4 = 4 - 4 = 0
Therefore, x = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} is a zero of the polynomial p(x)
Putting x = -4 in p(x), we get
p(-4) = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} (-4)2 + 7 {tex}\times{/tex} (-4) - 4 = 32 - 28 - 4 = 32 - 32 = 0
Therefore, x = -4 is a zero of the polynomial p(x)
Thus, {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} and -4 are the zeros of the given polynomial p(x).

questions.question: The zeros of the polynomial p(x) = 2x2 + 5x - 3 are
questions.explanation:
The given polynomial is p(x) = 2x2 + 5x - 3
Putting x = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} in p(x), we get
p{tex}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right){/tex} = 2 {tex}\times\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{2}{/tex} + 5 {tex}\times\frac{1}{2}{/tex} - 3 = {tex}\frac{1}{2}+\frac{5}{2}{/tex} - 3 = 3 - 3 = 0
Therefore, x = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} is a zero of the polynomial p(x)
Putting x = -3 in p(x), we get
p(-3) = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} (-3)2 + 5 {tex}\times{/tex} (-3) - 3 = 18 - 15 - 3 = 0
Therefore, x = -3 is a zero of the polynomial p(x)
Thus, {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} and -3 are the zeros of the given polynomial p(x).

questions.question: The zeros of the polynomial p(x) = x2 + x - 6 are
questions.explanation:
The given polynomial is p(x) = x2 + x - 6
Putting x = 2 in p(x), we get
p(2) = 22 + 2 - 6 = 4 + 2 - 6 = 0
Therefore, x = 2 is a zero of the polynomial p(x).
Putting x = -3 in p(x), we get
p(-3) = (-3)2 - 3 - 6 = 9 - 9 = 0
Therefore, x = -3 is a zero of the polynomial p(x)
Thus, 2 and -3 are the zeroes of the given polynomial p(x).

questions.explanation:

Putting x = a in p(x), we get
p(a) = a3 - a {tex}\times{/tex} a2 + a = a3 - a3 + a = a

questions.question: When p(x) = 4x3 - 12x2 + 11x - 5 is divided by (2x - 1), the remainder is
questions.explanation:
2x  - 1 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}
By the remainder theorem, we know that when p(x) is divided by (2x - 1), the remainder is p{tex}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right){/tex}
Now, we have:
p{tex}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right){/tex} = 4 {tex}\times\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{3}{/tex} - 12 {tex}\times\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{2}{/tex} + 11 {tex}\times \frac{1}{2}{/tex} - 5
= {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} - 3 + {tex}\frac{11}{2}{/tex} - 5
= -2

questions.question: When p(x) = x3 - 3x2 + 4x + 32 is divided by (x + 2), the remainder is
questions.explanation:
x + 2 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = -2
By the remainder theorem, we know that when p(x) is divided by (x +2), the remainder is p(-2).
Now, we have:
p(-2) = (-2)3 - 3 {tex}\times{/tex} (-2)2 + 4 {tex}\times{/tex} (-2) + 32
= -8 - 12 - 8 + 32
= 4

questions.question: If p(x) = x + 4 then p(x) + p(-x) = ?
questions.explanation:
Let: p(x) = (x + 4)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} p(-x) = (x + 4)
= -x + 4
Thus, we have: p(x) + p(-x) = {(x + 4) + (-x + 4)}
= 4 + 4
= 8

questions.question: If a1/3 + b1/3 + c1/3 = 0, then

questions.question: If a2 + b2 + c2 - ab -bc - ca = 0, then
questions.explanation:
Given:  a2 + b2 + c2 - ab - bc- ca = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2(a2 + b2 + c2- ab - bc - ca) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (2a2 + 2b2 + 2c2 - 2ab - 2bc - 2ca) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} ({a2 + b2 - 2ab} + {b2 + c2 - 2bc} + {c2 + a2 - 2ca}) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (a - b)2 + (b - c)2 + (c - a)2 = 0
Now, since the sum of all squares is zero
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a - b = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = b
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} b - c = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} b = c
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}c - a = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} c = a
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = b = c

questions.question: If {tex}3 x+\frac{2}{x}=7{/tex}, then {tex}\left(9 x^{2}-\frac{4}{x^{2}}\right){/tex}=

questions.question: If {tex}x-\frac{1}{x}=\frac{15}{4}{/tex}, then {tex}x+\frac{1}{x}{/tex} =
questions.explanation:

 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left(x^{2}\right)+\left(\frac{1}{x^{2}}\right)+2{/tex}{tex}\times x \times \frac{1}{x}{/tex}{tex}=\frac{257}{16}+2{/tex}{tex}\times x \times \frac{1}{x}{/tex}

questions.question: If {tex}x^{4}+\frac{1}{x^{4}}=194{/tex}, then {tex}x^{3}+\frac{1}{x^{3}}{/tex} =
questions.explanation:
{tex}\left(x^{4 }+\frac{1}{x^{4}}\right)=194{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left(x^{2}\right)^{2}+\left(\frac{1}{x^{2}}\right){/tex}{tex}+2 \times x^{2} \times \frac{1}{x^{2}}=194{/tex}{tex}+2 \times x^{2} \times \frac{1}{x^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}\left(x^{2}+\frac{1}{x^{2}}\right)^{2}=196{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}x^{2}+\frac{1}{x^{2}}{/tex}{tex}=\sqrt{196}=14{/tex}
Now,
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}\left(x^{2}\right)+\left(\frac{1}{x^{2}}\right)+2{/tex}{tex}\times x \times \frac{1}{x}=14{/tex}{tex}+2 \times x \times \frac{1}{x}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}\left(x-\frac{1}{x}\right)^{2}=16{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}x+\frac{1}{x}{/tex}{tex}=\sqrt{16}=4{/tex}
Now, {tex}\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)^{3}=(4)^{3}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}(x)^{3}+\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)^{3}+3{/tex}{tex}\times x \times \frac{1}{x}{/tex}{tex}\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)=64{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}\left(x^{3}\right)+\left(\frac{1}{x^{3}}\right)+3(4)=64{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}\left(x^{3}\right)+\left(\frac{1}{x^{3}}\right)=64{/tex} - 12 = 52

questions.question: If {tex}x^{4}+\frac{1}{x^{4}}=623{/tex}, then {tex}x+\frac{1}{x}{/tex} =

questions.question: If {tex}x^{3}-\frac{1}{x^{3}}=14{/tex}, then {tex}x-\frac{1}{x}{/tex} =
questions.explanation:
Given : {tex}x^{3}-\left(\frac{1}{x^{3}}\right)=14{/tex}
Let x = a and {tex}\frac{1}{x}=b{/tex}
Say, {tex}x-\frac{1}{x}=A{/tex}
Then, a3 - b3 = 14
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (a - b)(a2 + ab + b2) = 14
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (a - b)( {(a - b)2 + 2ab} + 2ab) = 14
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (a - b){(a-b)2 + 3ab} = 14
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (a - b){(a - b)2 + 3} = 14
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A(A2 + 3) = 14
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A(A2 + 3) = 14
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A3 + 3A - 14 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A3 - 2A2 + 2A2 - 4A + 7 A - 14 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A2(A - 2) + 2Y(Y - 2) + 7(Y - 2) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (A-2)(A2+ 2A + 7) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A - 2 = 0,   {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = 2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}x-\frac{1}{x}=2{/tex}

questions.question: If {tex}x^{3}+\frac{1}{x^{3}}=110{/tex}, then {tex}x+\frac{1}{x}{/tex} =

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)^{3}{/tex} {tex}=110+3\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)^{3}{/tex}{tex}-3\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)-110=0{/tex}
Let {tex}x+\frac{1}{x}=a{/tex}

questions.question: If {tex}x+\frac{1}{x}=4{/tex}, then {tex}x^{4}+\frac{1}{x^{4}}{/tex}=
questions.explanation:

questions.question: If x3 - 3x2 + 3x - 7 = (x + 1)(ax2 +bx + c), then a + b + c =
questions.explanation:
First multiply
(x + 1)(ax² + bx + c)
= ax³ + bx² + cx + ax² + bx + c
= ax³ + bx² + ax² + cx + bx + c
= ax³ + (b+a)x² + (c + b)x + c
Comparing it with
x³ - 3x² + 3x - 7
a = 1
b + a = -3 => b + 1 = -3 => b = -4
c + b = 3 = > c - 4 = 3 => c = 7
c = -7 should be 7
as if we put x = -1 in
x³ - 3x² + 3x - 7
-1 - 3 - 3 - 7 = 14 so x + 1 can not be factor so x +1 will be factor if
x³ - 3x² + 3x - 7 is actually
x³ - 3x² + 3x + 7
then -1 - 3 - 3 + 7 = 0 
hence we can say that
a = 1
b = -4
c = 7
so a + b + c = 4

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3xy and (x + y) are factors of given polynomial.

questions.question: If x = 2 -{tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex} then the value of {tex}x^2+\frac{1}{x^2}{/tex} and {tex}x^2-\frac{1}{x^2}{/tex} respectively, are ________.
questions.explanation:
We have, {tex}x=2-\sqrt{3}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \frac{1}{x}=\frac{1}{2-\sqrt{3}} \times \frac{2+\sqrt{3}}{2+\sqrt{3}}=\frac{2+\sqrt{3}}{4-3}=2+\sqrt{3}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)=2-\sqrt{3}+2+\sqrt{3}=4{/tex}
Also, {tex}\left(x-\frac{1}{x}\right)=2-\sqrt{3}-2-\sqrt{3}=-2 \sqrt{3}{/tex}
Now, {tex}\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)^2=x^2+\frac{1}{x^2}+2{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow (4)^2=x^2+\frac{1}{x^2}+2 \Rightarrow x^2+\frac{1}{x^2}=14{/tex}
and {tex}x^2-\frac{1}{x^2}=\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)\left(x-\frac{1}{x}\right)=4 \times(-2 \sqrt{3})=-8 \sqrt{3}{/tex}

questions.question: The value of k for which (x - 1) is a factor of 9x2 + kx - 18 is ________.
questions.explanation:
Let f(x) = 9x2 + kx - 18
Since, (x - 1) is a factor of f(x). Therefore, by factor theorem, f(1) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 9(1)2 + k(1) - 18 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 9 + k - 18 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} k = 9

questions.question: The remainder when x4 - y4 is divided by x - y is ______.
questions.explanation:
x4 - y4 = (x2)2 - (y2)2 = (x2 - y2) (x2 + y2)
= (x - y)(x + y)(x2+ y2)
Since, (x - y) is a factor of x4- y4.
Using factor theorem, the remainder will be 0.

questions.question: If x21 + 101 is divided by x + 1, then the remainder is
questions.explanation:
If x21 + 101
is divided by x + 1, then the remainder is
P(-1) = (-1)21 + 101 = -1 + 101
{tex}\therefore{/tex} P(-1)= 100
Hence, the remainder is 100.

questions.question: Find the remainder when the expression x3 + x2 + x + 1 is divided by x + 1.
questions.explanation:
Let f(x) = x3 + x2 + x + 1
We know that
when f(x) is divided by x + 1, the remainder is f(-1).
Now, f(-1) = (-1)3 + (-1)2 + (-1) + 1
= -1 + 1 - 1 + 1 = 0

questions.question: A rectangular field has an area (14x2 - 11x - 15) m2. What could be the possible expression for length and breadth of the field?
questions.explanation:
Area of rectangular field = (14x2 - 11x - 15) m2
= 14x2 - 21x + 10x - 15
= 7x(2x - 3) + 5(2x - 3)
= (7x + 5)(2x - 3)
So, possible expression for length and breadth of the field are (7x + 5) m and (2x - 3) m.

questions.question: Vikas has ₹(x3 + 2ax) with this money he can buy exactly (x - 1) jeans or (x + 1) shirts with no money left. How much money Vikas has, if x = 4?
questions.explanation:
Amount of money Vikas has
= ₹(x3 + 2ax + b)
Now, he can buy exactly (x - 1) jeans or (x + 1) shirts.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} (x - 1) and (x + 1) are factors of x3 + 2ax + b.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} (1)3 + 2a(1) + b = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2a + b = -1 ...(i)
and (-1)3 + 2a(- 1) + b = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2 a - b = -1 ...(ii)
Adding (i) and (ii), we get
4a = -2 {tex} \Rightarrow a=\frac{-1}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} -1 + b = -1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} b = 0
So, amount of money he has = ₹(x3 - x)
= ₹(43 - 4) = ₹(64 - 4) = ₹60

questions.question: If x = {tex}(5-2 \sqrt{6}){/tex}, then {tex}\left(x^2+\frac{1}{x^2}\right){/tex} = ________.
questions.explanation:
We have, x = 5 - 2{tex}\sqrt{6}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x2 = 25 + 24 - 20{tex}\sqrt{6}{/tex} = 49 - 20{tex}\sqrt{6}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{1}{x}=\frac{1}{5-2 \sqrt{6}} \times \frac{5+2 \sqrt{6}}{5+2 \sqrt{6}}=\frac{5+2 \sqrt{6}}{25-24}=5+2 \sqrt{6}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{x^2}{/tex} = 25 + 24 + 20{tex}\sqrt{6}{/tex} = 49 + 20{tex}\sqrt{6}{/tex}
So, {tex}\left(x^2+\frac{1}{x^2}\right)=49-20 \sqrt{6}+49+20 \sqrt{6}=98{/tex}

questions.question: The zeroes of the polynomial 3x2 + 11x - 4 are
questions.explanation:

f(x) = 3x(x + 4) - 1 (x + 4)
f(x) = (x + 4)(3x - 1)
Put both the factors equal to zero.
x + 4 = 0, x = -4
3x - 1 = 0, x = {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex}
The zeroes of the polynomial 3x2 + 11x - 4 are {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} and - 4.

{tex}\therefore {/tex} The distance of the given point from the origin = {tex}\sqrt {{{\left( {{x_2} - {x_1}} \right)}^2} + {{\left( {{y_2} - {y_1}} \right)}^2}} {/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt {{{\left( {0 + 3} \right)}^2} + {{\left( {0 - 4} \right)}^2}} {/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt {9 + 16}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt {25} {/tex} = 5 units

questions.question: The point whose abscissa and ordinate have different signs will lie in
questions.explanation:
A coordinate is an ordered pair of numbers in which the first number is the abscissa i.e. the measurement along the X axis.
The signs of the coordinates in the first quadrant is (+,+),
the second quadrant is (−,+),
the third quadrant is (−,−),
fourth quadrant is (+,−).
So, the points whose abscissa and ordinate have different signs are 2nd and 4th quadrants.

questions.question: The difference between two complementary angles is {tex}{40^0}{/tex}.The angles are
questions.explanation:
We know that the sum of two complementary angles is {tex}{90^0}{/tex}

Let the two angles be x and y

{tex}x + y = 90^0{/tex} (1)

We also know that the difference of the angles is {tex}40^0{/tex}

Therefore  {tex}x - y = 40^0{/tex}  (2)

Combining (1) and (2)

We have 

{tex}x + y = 90^0{/tex}

{tex}x - y = 40^0{/tex}

Solving as simultaneous equations we get

{tex}2x = 130^0{/tex}

Hence {tex}x = 65^0 {/tex}

Substituting this value of x in either one of the equations (1) or (2)

We get {tex}y = 25^0{/tex}

questions.question: An angle is 4 time its complement. Find measure.
questions.explanation:
If x is the angle, its complement will be (90o - x),
because they have to add up to 90, and here x + (90o - x) = 90o
x is equal to 4 times of its complement, so we have
x = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} (90o - x)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 360o - 4x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5x = 360o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{360^o}{5}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 72o

{tex}​​\angle{/tex}5 = {tex}​​\angle{/tex}6 = y
Now,
{tex}​​\angle{/tex}4 + {tex}​​\angle{/tex}5 + {tex}​​\angle{/tex}6 = 180o (Consecutive interior angle)
y = {tex}90-\frac{x}{2}{/tex}

questions.question: In Fig. if lines l and m are parallel, then x =
questions.explanation:
l ‖ m
Let transversal be n and {tex}​​\angle{/tex}1 = 65o
{tex}​​\angle{/tex}2 = 20o
{tex}​​\angle{/tex}3 = x
Since,
l ‖ m and n cuts them so,
{tex}​​\angle{/tex}1 + {tex}​​\angle{/tex}4 = 180o (Co. interior angle)
65o + ∠4 = 180o
{tex}​​\angle{/tex}4 = 115o (i)
{tex}​​\angle{/tex}4 = {tex}​​\angle{/tex}5 = 115o (Vertically opposite angle)
{tex}​​\angle{/tex}2 + {tex}​​\angle{/tex}5 + {tex}​​\angle{/tex}3 = 180o
20o + 1150 + x = 180o
x = 45o

questions.question: In Fig. ,if lines l and m are parallel lines, then x =
questions.explanation:
Given that,
l ‖ m
Let, l ‖ m and transversal cuts them and
{tex}​​\angle{/tex}1 = 70o
{tex}​​\angle{/tex}3 = 20o
{tex}​​\angle{/tex}4 = 30o
{tex}​​\angle{/tex}1 +{tex}​​\angle{/tex}2 = 180o (Interior angle)
{tex}​​\angle{/tex}2 = 110o (i)
{tex}​​\angle{/tex}2 ={tex}​​\angle{/tex}5 (Vertically opposite angle)
{tex}​​\angle{/tex}5 = 110o (ii)

questions.question: Two complementary angles are such that two times the measure of one is equal to three times the measure of the other. The measure of the smaller angle is
questions.explanation:
Let x and (90o – x) be two complimentary angles
According to question,
2x = 3 (90o – x)
2x = 270o – 3x
x = 54o
The angles are:
54o and 90o – 54o = 36o
Thus, smallest angle is 36o

questions.question: An exterior angle of a triangle is 105° and its two interior opposite angles are equal. Each of these equal angles is
questions.explanation:
Let one of interior angle be xo.
Sum of two opposite interior angles = Exterior angle
{tex}\therefore{/tex}  xo + xo = 105o [{tex}\therefore{/tex} Exterior angle = 105o (given)]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2xo = 105o
{tex}\therefore{/tex} xo = {tex}\frac{105^{\circ}}{2}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} xo = {tex}52 \frac{1}{2}{/tex} 
Hence, each angle of a triangle is {tex}52 \frac{1}{2}{/tex}

questions.question: Angles of a triangle are in the ratio 2 : 4 : 3. The smallest angle of the triangle is
questions.explanation:
Given, the ratio of angles of a triangle is 2 : 4 : 3.
Let the angles of a triangle be {tex}\angle{/tex}A, {tex}\angle{/tex}B and {tex}\angle{/tex}C.
{tex}\angle{/tex}A = 2x, {tex}\angle{/tex}B = 4x
{tex}\angle{/tex}C = 3x, {tex}\angle{/tex}A + {tex}\angle{/tex}B+ {tex}\angle{/tex}C = 180°
[sum of all the angles of a triangle is 180°]
2x + 4x + 3x = 180°
9x = 180°
x = {tex}\frac{180^o}{9}{/tex}= 20°
{tex}\angle{/tex}A = 2x = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 20° = 40°
{tex}\angle{/tex}B = 4x = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 20° = 80°
{tex}\angle{/tex}C = 3x = 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 20° = 60°
Hence, the smallest angle of a triangle is 40°.

questions.question: Two complementary angles are such that twice the measure of the one is equal to three times the measure of the other. The larger of the two measures.
questions.explanation:
Let the measure of the required angle be x°
Then, the measure of its complement will be(90 − x)°
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 2x = 3 (90 - x)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x = 270 - 3x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5x = 270
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 54°

questions.question: The measure of an angle is five times its complement. The angle measures
questions.explanation:
Let the measure of the required angle be x°
Then, the measure of its complement will be (90 − x)°
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 5 (90 - x)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 450 - 5x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 6x = 450
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 75°

questions.question: AB and CD are two parallel lines. PQ cuts AB and CD at E and F respectively. EL is the bisector of {tex}\angle{/tex}FEB. If {tex}\angle{/tex}LEB = 35°, then {tex}\angle{/tex}CFQ will be
questions.explanation:
It is given that, AB||CD with PQ as transversal.
Also, EL is the bisector {tex}\angle BEF{/tex} and {tex}\angle LEB{/tex} = 35o
We need to find {tex}\angle CFQ{/tex}
Therefore,
{tex}\angle B E F=2(\angle L E B){/tex}
{tex}\angle \mathrm{BEF}=2\left(35^{\circ}\right){/tex}
{tex}\angle B E F=70^{\circ}{/tex} .... (i)
We have {tex}\mathrm{AB} \| \mathrm{CD}{/tex}, {tex}\angle BEF{/tex} and {tex}\angle DFE{/tex} are consecutive interior angles, which must be supplementary.
{tex}\angle B E F+\angle D F E=180^{\circ}{/tex}
From equation (i), we get:
{tex}70^{\circ}+\angle D F E=180^{\circ}{/tex}
{tex}\angle D F E=180^{\circ}-70^{\circ}{/tex}
{tex}\angle D F E=110^{\circ}{/tex} .... (ii)
We have {tex}\angle \mathrm{CFQ} {/tex} and {tex}\angle \mathrm{DFE}{/tex} as vertically opposite angles.
Therefore,
{tex}\angle \mathrm{CFQ}=\angle \mathrm{DFE}{/tex}
{tex}\angle C F Q=110^{\circ}{/tex}

questions.question: If two angles are complements of each other then each angle is
questions.explanation:
an acute angle
If two angles are complements of each other, that is, the sum of their measures is 90°, then each angle is an acute angle.

questions.question: The angles of a triangle are in the ratio 2:3:4. The largest angle of the triangle is
questions.explanation:
Suppose {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC such that {tex}\angle A: \angle B: \angle C{/tex} = 2 : 3 : 4
Let {tex}\angle{/tex}A = 2k, {tex}\angle{/tex}B = 3k and {tex}\angle{/tex}C = 4k, where k is some constant
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC,
{tex}\angle A+\angle B+\angle C=180^{\circ}{/tex} (Angle sum property)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2k + 3k + 4k = 180°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 9k = 180°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} k = 20°
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Measure of the largest angle = 4k {tex}=4 \times 20^{\circ}=80^{\circ}{/tex}

questions.question: An exterior angle of a triangle is 110° and its two interior opposite angles are equal. Each of these equal angles is
questions.explanation:
Let the measure of each of the two equal interior opposite angles of the triangle be x.
In a triangle, the exterior angle is equal to the sum of the two interior opposite angles.
{tex}\therefore x+x=110^{\circ}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x = 110°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 55°
Thus, the measure of each of these equal angles is 55°.

questions.question: If one of the angles of a triangle is 130°, then the angle between the bisectors of the other two angles can be
questions.explanation:
Let angles of a triangle be {tex}\angle{/tex}A, {tex}\angle{/tex}B and {tex}\angle{/tex}C. 

In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC,
{tex}\angle A+\angle B+\angle C=180^{\circ}{/tex} [sum of all interior angles of a triangle is 180o] 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{2} \angle A+\frac{1}{2} \angle B+\frac{1}{2} \angle C=\frac{180^{\circ}}{2}=90^{\circ}{/tex} [dividing both sides by 2]
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{2} \angle B+\frac{1}{2} \angle C=90^{\circ}-\frac{1}{2} \angle A{/tex}  [{tex}\because{/tex} In {tex}\triangle{/tex}OBC, {tex}\angle{/tex}OBC +{tex}\angle{/tex}BCO + {tex}\angle{/tex}COB = 180o]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Since, {tex}\frac{\angle B}{2}+\frac{\angle C}{2}+\angle B O C=180^{\circ}{/tex} as BO and OC are the angle bisectors of {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC and {tex}\angle{/tex}BCA, respectively
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}180^{\circ}-\angle B O C=90^{\circ}-\frac{1}{2} \angle A{/tex} 
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}BOC = 180o - 90o + {tex}\frac{1}{2}\angle A{/tex} 
= {tex}90^{\circ}+\frac{1}{2} \times 130^{\circ}{/tex} = 90o + 65o [{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}A = 130o (given)]
= 155o 
Hence, the required angle is 155o.

questions.question: If {tex}\angle{/tex}BAC = 30o, find {tex}\angle{/tex}ACD.
questions.explanation: p||q abd AC is a transversal. Thus, {tex}\triangle{/tex}BAC and {tex}\angle{/tex}ACD are alternate interior angles. Therefore, {tex}\angle{/tex}BAC = {tex}\angle{/tex}ACD = 30o (alternate interior angles are equal).

questions.question: If cow at C and cow at D is 2 km apart, then what is the distance between cow at A and cow at B?

So, AB = CD
Given, distance between cow at C and cow at D = CD = 2 km
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} AB = 2 km
Hence, distance cow at A and cow at B is 2 km.

questions.question: If {tex}\angle{/tex}B = 45o, then {tex}\angle{/tex}D = ________.
questions.explanation:

questions.explanation:

questions.question: {tex}\angle{/tex}ACO =

In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ACO, AO = AC (given)
{tex}\therefore \angle{/tex}ACO = {tex}\angle{/tex}AOC
But in {tex}\triangle{/tex}AOC.
{tex}\angle{/tex}AOC + {tex}\angle{/tex}ACO + {tex}\angle{/tex}CAO = 180o
