questions.explanation: Rectangle

questions.question: Two adjacent angles of a parallelogram are in the ratio 4 : 5. The angles are
questions.explanation:
Let the adjacent angles of a parallelogram be 4x and 5x and sum of adjacent angles of parallelogram is 180°.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 4x + 5x = 180°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 9x = 180° {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 20°
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Angles are 80° and 100°.

questions.question: The diagonals of a rectangle PQRS intersect at O. If {tex}\angle{/tex}ROQ = 60°, then find {tex}\angle{/tex}OSP.
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle{/tex}ROQ = {tex}\angle{/tex}SOP = 60° ...(i) [Vertically opposite angles]

{tex}\therefore{/tex} PR = SQ {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} PO= SO (Diagonals of a rectangle are equal and bisect each other)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}OPS = {tex}\angle{/tex}OSP ...(ii) [{tex}\because{/tex} In a triangle, angles opposite to equal sides are equal]
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}POS, by angle sum property
{tex}\angle{/tex}OSP + {tex}\angle{/tex}OPS + {tex}\angle{/tex}SOP = 180°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2{tex}\angle{/tex}OSP = 180° - 60° [Using (i) & (ii)]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}OSP = 60°

questions.question: ABCD is a parallelogram. If is produced to E such that ED bisects BC at O. Then which of the following is correct?
questions.explanation:
In the figure, {tex}\triangle{/tex}BCD is a parallelogram, where AB is produced to E such that OC = OB
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}OBE and {tex}\triangle{/tex}OCD,
{tex}\angle{/tex}1 = {tex}\angle{/tex}2 (Vertically opposite angles)
{tex}\angle{/tex}3 = {tex}\angle{/tex}4 (Alternate interior angles)
OB = OC (given)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\triangle{/tex}OBE {tex}\cong{/tex} {tex}\triangle{/tex}OCD (By ASA congruency)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} BE = CD (By CPCT)
Also, AB = CD (y ABCD is parallelogram)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} AB = BE

questions.question: D and E are the mid-points of the sides AB and AC, respectively of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC. DE is produced to F. To prove that CF is equal and parallel to DA, we need an additional information which is
questions.explanation:
We have produced DE to F such that DE = EF ...(i)

In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ADE and {tex}\triangle{/tex}CFE,
AE = CE [{tex}\because{/tex} E is the mid-point of AC]
{tex}\angle{/tex}AED = {tex}\angle{/tex}CEF [Vertically opposite angles]
DE = FE [By (i)]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\triangle{/tex}ADE = {tex}\triangle{/tex}CFE [By SAS congruency]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} AD = CF and {tex}\angle{/tex}ADE = {tex}\angle{/tex}CFE [By CPCT]
This shows that alternate interior angles are equal.
Thus, AD || CF
Therefore, the additional information which we need is DE = EF

questions.question: X, Y are the mid-points of opposite sides AB and DC of a parallelogram ABCD. AY and DX are joined intersecting at S; CX and BY are joined intersecting at R. Then SXRY is a
questions.explanation:
In quadrilateral AXCY,
AX || CY ({tex}\because{/tex} AB || CD) ...(i)
AX = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} AB and CY = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} CD ({tex}\because{/tex} X and Y are midpoint of AB and CD)
Also, AB = CD (Opposite sides of parallelogram)
So, AX = CY ...(ii)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} AXCY is a parallelogram (from (i) and (ii))
Similarly, quadrilateral DXBY is a parallelogram.
In quadrilateral SXRY, SX || YR ({tex}\because{/tex} SX is a part of DX and YR is a part of YB)
Similarly, SY || XR
So, SXRY is a parallelogram.

questions.question: The triangle formed by joining the mid-points of the sides of a right angled triangle is
questions.explanation:
Let ABC be right angled triangle and {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = 90°.
Let D, E, F are mid-points of sides BC,
AC and AB respectively.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} EF || BD and BF || DE (By mid-point theorem)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} BDEF is a parallelogram.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}FED = {tex}\angle{/tex}FBD = 90° ({tex}\because{/tex} Opposite angles of a parallelogram are equal)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} DEF is right angled triangle.

questions.question: ABCD is a parallelogram in which {tex}\angle{/tex}ADC = 85° and side AB is produced to point E as shown in the figure. Find the value of (x + y).
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle{/tex}ADC + {tex}\angle{/tex}DCB = 180° (Sum of adjacent angles of a parallelogram is 180°)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 85° + x = 180° {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 95°
Now, DC || AE and CB is a transversal.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} y - x - 95° (Alternate interior angles)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x + y = 95° + 95° = 190°

questions.question:
By using a given figure of quadrilateral ABCD, match the following:

Column-I Column-II
(a) If ABCD is a parallelogram, then sum of the angles x, y and z is (i) 25°
(b) If ABCD is a rhombus, where {tex}\angle{/tex}D = 130°, then the value of x is (ii) 180°
(c) If ABCD is a rhombus, then value of w is (iii) 50°
(d) If ABCD is a parallelogram, where x + y = 130°, then the value of {tex}\angle{/tex}B is (iv) 90°
questions.explanation:
1. In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC, by angle sum property
    x + y + {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = 180°
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = 180° - (x + y) ...(i)
    {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = {tex}\angle{/tex}ADC [{tex}\because{/tex} Opposite angles of a parallelogram are equal]
    {tex}\therefore{/tex} z = 1 8 0 ° - ( x + y) [using (i)]
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x + y + z = 180°
  2. {tex}\angle{/tex}C = 2x [Since, diagonals bisects the angles in rhombus]
    Now, we have
    {tex}\angle{/tex}D + {tex}\angle{/tex}C = 180° (Co-interior angles)
    130° + 2x = 180° ({tex}\angle{/tex}D = 130°)
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x = 180° - 130° = 50° {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 25°
  3. since, in a rhombus, diagonals bisect each other at right angle.
    {tex}\therefore{/tex} w = 90°
  4. {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = 180° - (x + y) [Using (i)]
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = 180° - 130° [x + y = 130° (Given)]
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = 50°

chapters.name: Areas of Parallelograms and Triangles (FA Only)

questions.question: A triangle has an area of 45 square foot. Base of the triangle is 9 foot. What is corresponding height of triangle
questions.explanation:
Area of triangle = 45 square feet. Base = 9 feet
We know that
Area of triangle = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} Base {tex}\times{/tex} Height
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 45 = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 9 {tex}\times{/tex} Height
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Height = {tex}{{45 \times 2} \over 9}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Height = 10 feet

questions.question: What is the area of a parallelogram whose base is 8 and the corresponding altitude is 5?
questions.explanation:
Base = 8 and Corresponding altitude = 5
Area of parallelogram = Base {tex}\times{/tex} Corresponding altitude
= 8 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 = 40

questions.question: Parallelograms on the same base and between the same parallels have equal
questions.explanation: The theorem says that parallelograms on the same base and between the same parallels have equal area.

questions.question: Find the area of parallelogram, if Base = 3 and altitude is 4
questions.explanation:
Base = 3 and Corresponding altitude is 4
Area of parallelogram = Base {tex}\times{/tex} Corresponding altitude
= 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 = 12

questions.question: Find the area of ||gm, if base = 8 cm and altitude = 10 cm,
questions.explanation:
Base = 8 cm and Corresponding altitude = 10 cm
Area of parallelogram = Base {tex}\times{/tex} Corresponding altitude
= 8 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 = 80 sq.cm

questions.question: If Base = 9 and corresponding altitude = 4. Find area of ||gram
questions.explanation:
Area of parallelogram = Base {tex}\times{/tex} Corresponding altitude
= 9 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 = 36

questions.question: A parallelogram has an area of 36 square cm and base of the parallelogram is 9 cm. what is the corresponding altitude of the parallelogram?
questions.explanation:
Area of parallelogram = Base {tex}\times{/tex} Corresponding altitude
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 36 = 9 {tex}\times{/tex} Corresponding altitude
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Corresponding Altitude = {tex}{{36} \over 9} = 4{/tex} cm

questions.question:
Given below are the measurements of a parallelogram. Find the missing measurement.
Area = 90 square cm, Base = 5 cm, Height =?
questions.explanation:
Area of parallelogram = Base {tex}\times{/tex} Height
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Height =  {tex}{{{\rm{Area\ \ of\ \ Parallelogram}}} \over {{\rm{Base}}}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Height = {tex}\frac{90}{5}{/tex} = 18
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Height = 18 cm

questions.question: Measure of each side of an equilateral triangle is 12cm. Its area is given by
questions.explanation:
The area of equilateral triangle A = {tex}{{\sqrt 3 } \over 4}{/tex}a2
A = {tex}{{\sqrt 3 } \over 4}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 12 {tex}\times{/tex} 12
= {tex}36\sqrt{3}{/tex} sq cm

questions.question: The area of quadrilateral ABCD whose diagonals are perpendicular and of lengths 12 cm, 8 cm is
questions.explanation:
Since in the quadrilateral ABCD, the diagonals are perpendicular.
Area of quadrilateral = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} Product of diagonals
= {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 12 {tex}\times{/tex} 8 = 48 sq. cm

questions.question: The area of a parallelogram whose base is 32 m and the corresponding altitude is 6 m is
questions.explanation:
Area of parallelogram = Base {tex}\times{/tex} Corresponding altitude
= 32 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 = 192 sq. m

questions.question: The adjacent sides of a parallelogram are 20 cm and 15 cm in length. Then the ratio between the corresponding altitudes is
questions.explanation:
Since the adjacent sides and corresponding altitudes of a parallelogram are in proportion.
Therefore, {tex}{{15} \over {20}} = {3 \over 4}{/tex} 
Thus, the required ratio is 3 : 4.

questions.question: In a four-sided field, the length of the longer diagonal is 128 m. The lengths of the perpendiculars from the opposite vertices upon this diagonal are 22.7 m and 17.3 m. Then area of the field is
questions.explanation:
According to the question,
Area of the field = {tex}{1 \over 2} \times 128 \times 17.3 + {1 \over 2} \times 128 \times 22.7{/tex} 
= {tex}{1 \over 2} \times 128\left( {17.3 + 22.7} \right){/tex} 
= 2560 sq. m

questions.question: The diagonals of a rhombus measure 4 cm and 6 cm respectively. Its area in sq. cm is
questions.explanation:
Area of Rhombus = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} Product of diagonals
= {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 
= 12 sq. cm

questions.question: Let ABC be a triangle of area 24 sq. units and PQR be the triangle formed by the midpoints of sides of {tex}\Delta{/tex}ABC. Then the area of {tex}\Delta{/tex}PQR is
questions.explanation:
Since Q and R are the midpoints of BC and AC respectively.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} PQ || BA {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} PQ|| BP
Similarly, RQ || BP. So BQRP is a parallelogram.
Similarly, APRQ and PQCR are parallelograms.
We know that diagonal of a parallelogram bisect the parallelogram into two triangles of equal area.
Now, PR is a diagonal of ||gm APQR.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}APR = Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}PQR  .....(i)
Similarly,
PQ is a digonal of ||gm PBQR
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}PQR = Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}PBQ .....(ii)
QR is the diagonal of ||gm PQCR
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}PQR = Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}RCQ .....(iii)
From (i), (ii), and (iii) we have
Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}APR = Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}PQR = Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}PBQ = {tex}\triangle{/tex}Area of RCQ
But
Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}APR + Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}PQR + Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}PBQ + Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}RCQ = Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC
4(Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}PBQ) = Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC
= {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex} Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex} PBQ = {tex}\frac{1}{4} (24){/tex} 
= 6 sq units.

questions.question: ABCD is a quadrilateral whose diagonal AC divides it into two parts, equal in area, then ABCD is
questions.explanation: In all the mentioned quadrilaterals, a diagonal divides them into two triangles of equal areas.

questions.question: Two parallel sides of a trapezium are 12 cm and 8 cm long and the distance between them is 6.5 cm. The area of the trapezium is
questions.explanation:
Area of the trapezium = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} (sum of parallel sides) {tex}\times{/tex} distance between them
=  {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex}​ (12 + 8) {tex}\times{/tex} 6.5
= 65 cm2

questions.question: The lengths of the diagonals of a rhombus are 12 cm and 16 cm. The area of the rhombus is
questions.explanation: Area of the rhombus = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} product of diagonals = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex}​ 12 {tex}\times{/tex} 16 = 96 cm2

questions.question: The median of  triangle divides it into two
questions.explanation:
Let AD be one of the medians of {tex}\triangle ABC{/tex}.
To prove: ar(ABD) = ar(ADC)
Draw AL {tex}\perp{/tex} BC
Proof: ar (ABD) {tex}=\frac{1}{2} B D \times A L{/tex}
ar (ADC) {tex}=\frac{1}{2} DC \times A L{/tex}
But BD = DC
{tex}\therefore {/tex} ar(ABD) = ar (ADC)

questions.question: In which of the following figures, you find two polygons on the same base and between the same parallels?
questions.explanation: In the given figure, the parallelograms, PQRA and BQRS are on the same base QR and between the same parallels QR and PS.

questions.question: Out of the following given figures which are on the same base but not between the same parallels?
questions.explanation: In this figure, both the triangles are on the same base (QR) but not on the same parallels.

questions.question: An angle at the circumference in the semicircle is
questions.explanation: The angle at the circumference in a semicircle is always 90o.

questions.question: If the angles subtended by two chords of a circle at the centre are equal then the chords are
questions.explanation: If the angles subtended by two chords of a circle at the centre are equal then the chords are equal. It's a theorem.

questions.question: How many circle can pass through three non-collinear points?
questions.explanation: Only one circle can be drawn from three distinct points. Join any two sets of points. Make their respective perpendicular bisectors. The point where the two bisectors meet act as the centre of such a circle. Taking the distance between the centre and any of the given points as radius, we can draw the circle. Such a circle would definitely pass through the remaining two points as well.

questions.question: The constant distance of a point on a circle from the centre of the circle is called
questions.explanation:
Radius is the fixed distance of a fixed point from a point on the circle. 
Also more precisely, a circle is the loci or the path of a point that moves to maintain a fixed distance from a given point.

questions.question: PS and RS are two chord's of a circle such that PQ = 10cm and RS = 24cm and PQ||RS. The distance between PQ and RS is 17cm. Find the radius of circle.
questions.explanation:
Let L and M be the midpoints of RS and PQ respectively.
Let OM = x thus, OL = 17 - x
Now in triangle RLO, RL= 12 and
RL2 + OL2 = r2
122 + (17- x)2 = r2 ........ (1)
Similarly,
In triangle OMP, PM = 5 and
PM2 + OM2 = OP2
(x)2 + 52 = r2 .........................(2)
Equating (1) and (2), we get :-
122 +(17-x)2  = x2 + 52
144 + 289 - 34x + x2 = x2  +25 { using, (a-b)2 }
On solving, we get:
{tex}x = {408\over34} = 12{/tex} 
so, 17 - x =17 - 12 = 5
Thus,from (1),
RL2 + OL2 = r2
122 +  52 = r2
144 + 25 = r²
r2 =169
so, radius = {tex} \sqrt{169} {/tex} 
Hence, the radius is 13 cm

questions.question: A circle is drawn. It divides the plane into
questions.explanation: A circle divides the plane into 3 parts namely, the points outside the circle, the points inside the circle and the points on the circle.

questions.question: The relation between diameter and radius of a circle is
questions.explanation: Radius is half the length of the diameter, thus diameter is twice the length of the radius.

questions.question: If P and Q are any two points on a circle then PQ is called a
questions.explanation: A chord is a line formed by any two points on a circle.

questions.question: The whole arc of a circle is called
questions.explanation: Circumference is the total length of the circle or in other words its a perimeter of the circle.

questions.question: One half of the whole arc of a circle
questions.explanation: A semi-circle is half the circle. In other words, half of the total length of the circle makes the semicircle.

questions.question: Circles having same centre are said to be
questions.explanation: Concentric circles are those circle that is drawn with the same point as a centre but different radii.

questions.question: The line which meet a circle in two points is called a
questions.explanation: A line that meets a circle at any two points is called secant of that circle and secant intercepted between these two points is chord of that circle.

questions.question: The sum of either pair of opposite angle of cyclic quadrilateral is
questions.explanation:
As per theorem,
The sum of either pair of opposite angles of a cyclic quadrilateral is {tex}180^\circ {/tex}.
Here, {tex}​​\angle 1 +\angle 2 = 180°{/tex}

questions.question: Which of the following pair of lines in a circle cannot be parallel?
questions.explanation: Two diameters cannot be parallel.

questions.question: If the length of a chord of a circle is 16 cm and is at a distance of 15 cm from the centre of the circle, then the radius of the circle is
questions.explanation:
We will represent the given data in the figure

In the diagram, AB is the given chord of 16 cm length and OM is the perpendicular distance from the centre to AB.
We know that perpendicular from the centre to any chord divides it into two equal parts.
So, AM = MB = {tex}\frac{16}{2}{/tex} = 8 cm.
Now consider right triangle {tex}\triangle{/tex}OMA and by using Pythagoras theorem {tex}\angle{/tex}OMA =90{tex}^\circ{/tex}
AO2 = AM2 + OM2    
AO2= 82 + 152
AO2= 64 + 225 = 289
AO= {tex}\sqrt{289}{/tex} = 17 cm

questions.question: The radius of a circle is 6 cm. The perpendicular distance from the centre of the circle to the chord which is 8 cm in length, is
questions.explanation:
We will represent the given data in the figure.

We know that perpendicular drawn from the centre to the chord divides the chord into two equal parts.
So, AM = MB = {tex}\frac{A B}{2}=\frac{8}{2}{/tex} = 4 cm.
Using Pythagoras theorem in the {tex}\triangle{/tex}AMO, {tex}\angle{/tex}OMA = 90{tex}^\circ{/tex}
OM2 = AO2 - AM2
OM2= 62 - 42
OM2= 36 - 16
OM= {tex}\sqrt{20}{/tex} 
OM= 2{tex}\sqrt{5}{/tex} cm

questions.question: If O is the centre of a circle of radius r and AB is a chord of the circle at a distance {tex}\frac{r}{2}{/tex} from O, then {tex}\angle{/tex}BAO =
questions.explanation:
Let OD = r
OC = {tex}\frac{r}{2}{/tex} 
In {tex}\angle{/tex}OAC and {tex}\angle{/tex}DAC
SAS - {tex}\angle{/tex}OAC {tex}\cong{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}DAC
Now, in {tex}\angle{/tex}OAD equilateral
{tex}\angle{/tex}AOD = 60o 
{tex}\angle{/tex}CAO = {tex}\angle{/tex}BAO = 30o 
=> sin {tex}\theta{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\frac{r}{2}}{r}=\frac{1}{2}{/tex} 
=> {tex}\theta{/tex} = 30o

questions.question: ABCD is a cyclic quadrilateral such that {tex}\angle{/tex}ADB = 30° and {tex}\angle{/tex}DCA = 80°, then {tex}\angle{/tex}DAB =
questions.explanation:
It is given that ABCD is cyclic quadrilateral {tex}\angle{/tex}ADB = 90o and {tex}\angle{/tex}DCA = 80o. We have to find {tex}\angle{/tex}DAB.
We have the following figure regarding the given information

{tex}\angle{/tex}BDA = {tex}\angle{/tex}BCA = 30o  (Angle in the same segment are equal)
Now, since ABCD is a cyclic quadrilateral 
So, {tex}\angle{/tex}DAB + {tex}\angle{/tex}BCD = 180o 
{tex}\angle{/tex}DAB + {tex}\angle{/tex}BCA + {tex}\angle{/tex}DCA = 180o   [ {tex}\angle{/tex}BCD =  {tex}\angle{/tex}BCA + {tex}\angle{/tex}DCA]
{tex}\angle{/tex}DAB + 30o + 80o = 180o
{tex}\angle{/tex}DAB = 180o - 110o 
{tex}\angle{/tex}DAB = 70o

questions.question: A chord of length 14 cm is at a distance of 6 cm from the centre of a circle. The length of another chord at a distance of 2 cm from the centre of the circle is
questions.explanation:
We are given the chord of length 14 cm and perpendicular distance from the centre to the chord is 6 cm. We are asked to find the length of another chord at a distance of 2 cm from the centre.
We have the following figure

We are given AB = 14 cm, OD = 6 cm, MO = 2 cm, PQ = ?
Since, perpendicular from centre to the chord divide the chord into two equal parts
Therefore
AO2 = AD2 + OD2 [using paythagoras theorem]
= 72 + 62
= 49 + 36
AO= {tex}\sqrt{85}{/tex} 
Now consider the {tex}\triangle{/tex}OPQ in which OM = 2 cm
So using Pythagoras theorem in {tex}\triangle{/tex}OPM
PM2 = OP2 - OM2
= {tex}(\sqrt{85})^{2}{/tex} - 22 ({tex}\because{/tex} OP = AO = radius)
PM2= 81
PM= 9 cm
Hence PQ = 2PM
= 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 9
PQ= 18 cm

questions.question: One chord of a circle is known to be 10 cm. The radius of this circle must be
questions.explanation:
We are given length of a chord to be 10 cm and we have to give information about the radius of the circle.
Since in any circle, the diameter of the circle is greater than any chord.
So diameter > 10
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2r > 10
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r > 5 cm

Radius is greater than 5 cm.

questions.question: ABC is a triangle with B as right angle, AC = 5 cm and AB = 4 cm. A circle is drawn with A as centre and AC as radius. The length of the chord of this circle passing through C and B is
questions.explanation:
In the circle produce CB to P. Here PC is the required chord.
We know that perpendicular drawn from the centre to the chord divide the chord into two equal parts.
So, PC = 2BC
Now in {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC apply Pythagoras theorem

AB2 + BC2 = AC2
==>BC2 = AC2 - AB2
==>BC2= 52 - 42
==>BC2= 25 - 16
==>BC2= 9
==>BC = 3 cm
So, PC = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} BC
= 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3
PC= 6 cm

questions.question: If AB, BC and CD are equal chords of a circle with O as centre and AD diameter, than {tex}\angle{/tex}AOB =
questions.explanation:
As we know that equal chords make an equal angle at the center.
Therefore,
{tex}\angle{/tex}AOB = {tex}\angle{/tex}BOC = {tex}\angle{/tex}COD
{tex}\angle{/tex}AOB + {tex}\angle{/tex}BOC + {tex}\angle{/tex}COD = 180o [Sum of Linear pair of angle is 180o]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3{tex}\angle{/tex}AOB = 180o 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}AOB = 60o

questions.question: Let C be the mid-point of an arc AB of a circle such that m{tex}\widehat{A B}{/tex} = 183°. If the region bounded by the arc ACB and line segment AB is denoted by S, then the centre O of the circle lies
questions.explanation:
Given: m{tex}\widehat{A B}{/tex}  = 183o and C is mid-point of arc ABO is the centre.

With the given information the corresponding figure will look like the following,
 
From the figure, so the centre of the circle O lies inside the shaded region S.

questions.question: In a circle, the major arc is 3 times the minor arc. The corresponding central angles and the degree measures of two arcs are
questions.explanation:
We are given the major arc is 3 times the minor arc. We are asked to find the corresponding central angle.
See the corresponding figure.

We know that the angle formed by the circumference at the centre is 360o.
Since the circumference of the circle is divided into two parts such that the angle formed by major and minor arcs at the centre are 3x and x respectively.
So 3x + x = 360
4x = 360
x = 90
So m{tex}\widehat {AB}{/tex} = 90o and m{tex}\widehat {AB}{/tex} = 3x = 270o 
the degree measures of two arcs are 90o  and  270o

questions.question: If A and B are two points on a circle such that m ({tex}\widehat{AB}{/tex}) = 260°. A possible value for the angle subtended by arc BA at a point on the circle is
questions.explanation:
We are given m({tex}\widehat{AB}{/tex}) = 260o 

Suppose point P is on the circle.
Since m({tex}\widehat {AB}{/tex}) = 260o 
So, angle AOB = 360o - 260o = 100o 
We know that angle subtended by chord AB at the centre is twice that of subtended at the point P
So, {tex}\angle{/tex}APB = {tex}\frac{\angle A O B}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{100}{2}{/tex} = 50o 

==>  {tex}\angle{/tex}APB =  50o

questions.question: An equilateral triangle ABC is inscribed in a circle with centre O. The measures of {tex}\angle{/tex}BOC is
questions.explanation:
We are given that an equilateral {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC is inscribed in a circle with centre O. We need to find {tex}\angle{/tex}BOC.
We have the following corresponding figure.

We are given AB = BC = AC
Since the sides, AB, BC, and AC are these equal chords of the circle.
Hence,
{tex}\angle{/tex}AOB + {tex}\angle{/tex}BOC + {tex}\angle{/tex}AOC = 360
==> {tex}\angle{/tex}BOC + {tex}\angle{/tex}BOC + {tex}\angle{/tex}BOC = 360
==> 3{tex}\angle{/tex}BOC = 360
==> {tex}\angle{/tex}BOC = {tex}\frac{360}{3}{/tex}
==> {tex}\angle{/tex}BOC = 120o

questions.question: If two diameters of a circle intersect each other at right angles, then quadrilateral formed by joining their end points is a
questions.explanation:
Let AB and CD be the diagonals of a circle such that AB {tex}\perp{/tex} CD.
Joining points A, B, C, D in the order we see that AB and CD are the equal diagonals of quad. ACBD which intersect at a right angle. every angle is equal to 90{tex}^\circ{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} ACBD is a square.

questions.question: If ABC is an arc of a circle and {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = 135°, then the ratio of arc {tex}\widehat{ABC}{/tex}  to the circumference, is
questions.explanation:
The length of an arc subtending an angle {tex}\theta{/tex} in a circle of radius r is given by the formula,
Length of the arc = {tex}\frac{\theta}{360^{\circ}} 2 \pi r{/tex} 
Here, it is given that the are subtends an angle of 135o with its centre. So the length of the given arc in a circle with radius r is given as
Length of the arc = {tex}\frac{135^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}} 2 \pi r{/tex}     ......(1)
The circumference of the same circle with radius r =  2{tex}\pi{/tex}r.   ....(2)
The ratio between the lengths of the arc and the circumference of the circle will be
{tex}\frac{\text { Lenght of the arc }}{\text { Cirrumference of the circle }}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{135^{\circ}(2 \pi r)}{360^{\circ}(2 \pi r)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{135^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3}{8}{/tex}   [FROM (1) and (2)]

RATIO = 3 : 8

questions.question: The chord of a circle is equal to its radius. The angle subtended by this chord at the minor arc of the circle, is
questions.explanation:
We are given that the chord is equal to its radius.
We have to find the angle subtended by this chord at the minor arc.
We have the corresponding figure as follows:

We are given that
AO = OB = AB
So,
{tex}\triangle{/tex}AOB is an equilateral triangle.
Therefore, we have
{tex}\angle{/tex} AOB = 60o 
since the angle subtended by any chord at the centre is twice the angle subtended at any point on the circle.
So {tex}\angle{/tex}AQB = {tex}\frac{\angle A O B}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{60}{2}{/tex} = 30o    ==> {tex}\angle{/tex}AQB = 30o
Take a point P on the minor arc
Since APBQ is a cyclic quadrilateral
So, opposite angles are supplementary. That is
{tex}\angle{/tex}APB + {tex}\angle{/tex}AQB = 180o 
{tex}\angle{/tex}APB + 30o = 180o  [ {tex}\angle{/tex}AQB = 30o]
{tex}\angle{/tex}APB = 180o - 30o = 150o

questions.question: PQRS is a cyclic quadrilateral such that PR is a diameter of the circle. If {tex}\angle{/tex}QPR = 67° and {tex}\angle{/tex}SPR = 72°, then {tex}\angle{/tex}QRS =
questions.explanation:
Here we have a cyclic quadrilateral PQRS with PR being a diameter of the circle. Let the centre of this circle be O.
We are given that {tex}\angle{/tex}QPR = 67o and {tex}\angle{/tex}SPR = 72o. 

So, we see that,
{tex}\angle{/tex}QPS = {tex}\angle{/tex}QPR + {tex}\angle{/tex}RPS
= 67o + 72o 
= 139o 
In a cyclic quadrilateral, it is known that the opposite angles as supplementary.
 {tex}\angle{/tex}QPS + {tex}\angle{/tex}QRS = 180o 
{tex}\angle{/tex}QRS = 180o  - {tex}\angle{/tex}QPS

{tex}\angle{/tex}QRS= 180o - 139o = 41o 
{tex}\angle{/tex}QRS = 41o

questions.question: If A, B, C are three points on a circle with centre O such that {tex}\angle{/tex}AOB = 90° and {tex}\angle{/tex}BOC = 120°, then {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC =
questions.explanation:
To solve this problem we need to know that the angle subtended by an arc at the centre of the circle is double the angle subtended by the arc in the remaining part of the circle.

Here we are given that A, B, C, are three points on a circle with centre O such that {tex}\angle{/tex}AOB = 90o and {tex}\angle{/tex}BOC = 120o.
From
{tex}\angle{/tex}AOC = 360o - {tex}\angle{/tex}AOB - {tex}\angle{/tex}BOC
==>{tex}\angle{/tex}AOC= 360o - 90o - 120o 
==>{tex}\angle{/tex}AOC= 360o - 210o 
==>{tex}\angle{/tex}AOC= 150o 
Now, as seen earlier, the angle made by the arc AC with the centre of the circle will be twice the angle it makes in any point in the remaining part of the circle.
Since the point C lies on the remaining part of the circle, the angle the arc AC makes with this point has to be half of the angle 'AC' makes with the centre. Therefore we have,
{tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = {tex}\frac{\angle A O C}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{150^{\circ}}{2}{/tex} = 75o 
==> {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = 75o

questions.question: In a circle with centre O, AB and CD are two diameters perpendicular to each other. The length of chord AC, is
questions.explanation:
We are given a circle with centre at O and two perpendicular diameters AB and CD.
We need to find the length of AC.
We have the following corresponding figure:

Since, AB = CD  (Diameter of the same circle)
Also, {tex}\angle{/tex}AOC = 90o 
And, AO = {tex}\frac{A B}{2}{/tex} 
Here, AO = OC (radius)
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}AOC is right angled triangle,
AC2 = AO2 + OC2 = AO2 + AO2
= {tex}\left(\frac{A B}{2}\right)^{2}+\left(\frac{A B}{2}\right)^{2}{/tex} 
AC2 = {tex}\frac{A B^{2}}{2}{/tex} 
AC = {tex}\frac{A B}{\sqrt{2}}{/tex}

questions.question: Two equal circles of radius r intersect such that each passes through the centre of the other. The length of the common chord of the circles, is
questions.explanation:
We need to find a common chord.
We have the corresponding figure as follows:

AO = AO' = r (radius)
And OO' = r
So, {tex}\triangle{/tex} OAO' is an equilateral triangle.
We know that the attitude of an equilateral triangle with side r is given by {tex}\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}{/tex}r
That is AM = {tex}\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} r{/tex} 
We know that the line joining centre of the circles divides the common chord into two equal parts.
So we have
AB = 2AM= {tex}\text { 2. } \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} r{/tex} 
AB = {tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex}r

questions.question: If AB is a chord of a circle, P and Q are the two points on the circle different from A and B, then
questions.explanation:
We are given AB is a chord of the circle; P and Q are two points on the circle different from A and B.
We have the following figure.
Case 1: Consider P and Q are on the same side of AB.

We know that angles in the same segment are equal.
Hence, {tex}\angle{/tex}APB = {tex}\angle{/tex}AQB
Case 2: Now consider P and Q are on the opposite sides of AB.
In this case, we have the following figure.

Since quadrilateral APBQ is a cyclic quadrilateral.
Therefore,
{tex}\angle{/tex}APB + {tex}\angle{/tex}AQB = 180o (Sum of the pair of opposite angles of a cyclic quadrilateral is 180o.)
Therefore,from case 1 and case 2, {tex}\angle{/tex}APB = {tex}\angle{/tex}AQB or {tex}\angle{/tex}APB + {tex}\angle{/tex}AQB = 180o

questions.question: AB and CD are two parallel chords of a circle with centre O such that AB = 6 cm and CD = 12 cm. The chords are on the same side of the centre and the distance between them is 3 cm. The radius of the circle, is
questions.explanation:
Let the distance between the center and the chord CD be x cm and the radius of the circle is r cm.
We have to find the radius of the following circle:

In right angled triangle, OND,
x2 + 36 = r2 .....(i)
Now, in right angled triangle AOM,
r2 = 9 + (x + 3)2 ......(ii)
From (i) and (ii), we have,
r2 = 9 + ({tex}(\sqrt{r})^2{/tex} - 36 + 3)2 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r2 = 9 + r2 - 36 + 9 + {tex}6 \sqrt{r^{2}-36}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3 = {tex}\sqrt{r^{2}-36}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 9 = r2 - 36 [squaring both the sides]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r2 = 45 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 3{tex}\sqrt{5}{/tex} cm

questions.question: In a circle of radius 17 cm, two parallel chords are drawn on opposite side of a diameter. The distance between the chords is 23 cm. If the length of one chord is 16 cm, then the length of the other is
questions.explanation:
Given that: Radius of the circle is 17 cm, distance between two parallel chords AB and CD is 23 cm, where AB = 16 cm. We have to find the length of CD.

We know that the perpendicular drawn from the centre of the circle to any chord divides it into two equal parts.
AM = MB = 8 cm
Let OM = x cm ==> ON = 23 - x 
In right angled triangle OMB,
x = {tex}\sqrt{17^{2}-8^{2}=15}{/tex} 
Now, in triangle OND, ON = (23 - x) cm = (23 - 15) cm = 8 cm
ND = {tex}\sqrt{O D^{2}-O N^{2}}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} ND = {tex}\sqrt{17^{2}-8^{2}=15}{/tex} 
Therefore, the length of the other chord is
CD = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 15 = 30 cm

questions.question: A chord is at a distance of 8 cm from the centre of a circle of radius 17 cm. The length of the chord is
questions.explanation:
Here OA = 17 cm and OL = 8 cm.
AL2 = OA2 - OL2 = (17)2 - (8)2 = 289 - 64 = 225.   [using paythagoras theorem]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} AL= {tex}\sqrt{225}{/tex} = 15
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} AB= (2 {tex}\times{/tex} AL) = (2 {tex}\times{/tex} 15) cm = 30 cm.

questions.explanation: The angle in a semicircle measures 90o.

questions.question: AD is a diameter of a circle and AB is a chord. If AD = 34 cm, AB = 30 cm the distance of AB from the center of the circle is:
questions.explanation:
Given, AD = 34 cm and AB = 30 cm In Figure, draw OL {tex}\perp{/tex} AB.
Since, the perpendicular from the centre of a circle to a chord bisects the chord.

{tex}\therefore{/tex} AL = LB {tex}=\frac{1}{2} A B{/tex} = 15cm
In right angled {tex}\triangle{/tex}OLA, OA2 = OL2 + AL2 [by Pythagoras theorem]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} (17)2 = OL2 + (15)2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 289 = OL2 + 225
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} OL2 = 289 - 225 = 64
{tex}\therefore{/tex} OL = 8cm
[taking positive square root, because length is always positive] Hence, the distance of the chord from the centre is 8 cm.

questions.question: Find the area of sector ODCO.

questions.question: Find the radius of the outer most circle.
questions.explanation:

{tex}\Rightarrow 2 \pi{/tex} R = 308 {tex}\Rightarrow 2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times{/tex} R = 308
{tex}\Rightarrow R=\frac{308 \times 7}{2 \times 22}{/tex} = 49 m

questions.question: Find the radius of the inner most circle.

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2nr = 264
{tex}\Rightarrow 2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times{/tex} r = 264 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = {tex}\frac{264 \times 7}{2 \times 22}{/tex} = 42 m

= 49 - 42 = 7 m

questions.question: Find the area of the race track.

= {tex}\pi{/tex}(R2 - r2) = {tex}\pi{/tex} [(49)2 - (42)2]
= {tex}\frac {22}7{/tex} [2401 - 1764] = 2002 m2

questions.question: If the cost of painting on the race track is ₹ 6 per m2, then find the total cost for painting the whole race track.
questions.explanation: Cost of painting the whole race track = ₹ (6 {tex}\times{/tex} 2002) = ₹ 12012.

questions.question: In the given figure, if chords and CD of the circle intersect each other at right angles, then x + y = ________.
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle{/tex}CDB = {tex}\angle{/tex}CAB (Angles in same segment are equal)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}CDB = x
Given, {tex}\angle{/tex}CPB = 90°
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}DPB, {tex}\angle{/tex}PDB + {tex}\angle{/tex}PBD = {tex}\angle{/tex}CPB (exterior angle property)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x + y = 90°

questions.question: In the given figure, angles subtended by chords AC and BC at the centre O of the circle are 55° and 155° respectively. Find {tex}\angle{/tex}ACB.
questions.explanation:
Given, {tex}\angle{/tex}AOC = 55°, {tex}\angle{/tex}BOC = 155°
reflex {tex}\angle{/tex}AOB = {tex}\angle{/tex}AOC + {tex}\angle{/tex}BOC = 55° + 155° = 210°
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}AOB = 360° -210 ° = 150°
Since angle subtended by an arc at centre is double the angle subtended by it on the remaining part of the circle.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}ACB = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}AOB = 75°

questions.question: In the given figure, if PQRS is a cyclic quadrilateral with respective angles. Then, the ratio of x and y is ________.
questions.explanation:
Given, PQRS is a cyclic quadrilateral.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}PSR + {tex}\angle{/tex}PQR = {tex}\angle{/tex}QPS + {tex}\angle{/tex}QRS = 180°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2y + 3y = 2x + 4x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5y = 6x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{x}{y}=\frac{5}{6}{/tex} i.e., 5 : 6

questions.question: O is the centre of the circle. If {tex}\angle{/tex}POQ = 98°, then {tex}\angle{/tex}PRQ is ________.
questions.explanation:
Since angle subtended by an arc at centre is double the angle subtended by it on the remaining part of the circle.
{tex}\therefore \quad \angle P R Q=\frac{1}{2} \angle P O Q=\frac{1}{2} \times 98^{\circ}=49^{\circ}{/tex}

questions.question: In the given figure, AB = 8 cm, OM = ON = 4 cm. Then CD is ________.
questions.explanation:
Given, AB = 8 cm and OM = ON = 4 cm
Since equal chords are equidistant from the centre.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} CD = 8 cm

questions.question: In the given figure, O is the centre of the circle and {tex}\angle{/tex}BAC = 56°. The measure of {tex}\angle{/tex}BDC is ________.
questions.explanation:
Given, {tex}\angle{/tex}BAC = 56°
Since, angles in same segment are equal.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}BAC = {tex}\angle{/tex}BDC = 56°

questions.question: In the given figure, {tex}\angle{/tex}ABD = 70°, {tex}\angle{/tex}ADB = 30°. Then, {tex}\angle{/tex}BCD is ________.
questions.explanation:
Given, {tex}\angle{/tex}ABD = 70°, {tex}\angle{/tex}ADB = 30°
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ADB, {tex}\angle{/tex}DAB + {tex}\angle{/tex}ADB + {tex}\angle{/tex}ABD = 180°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}DAB + 30° + 70° = 180°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}DAB = 180° - 100° = 80°
Since ABCD is a cyclic quadrilateral.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}DAB + {tex}\angle{/tex}BCD = 180°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 80° + {tex}\angle{/tex}BCD =180° {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}BCD = 100°

questions.question: In the given figure, if {tex}\angle{/tex}DAB = 62° and {tex}\angle{/tex}ABD=58°, then {tex}\angle{/tex}ACB is equal to______ .
questions.explanation:
Given, {tex}\angle{/tex}DAB = 62°, {tex}\angle{/tex}ABD = 58°
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ADB, {tex}\angle{/tex}DAB + {tex}\angle{/tex}ABD + {tex}\angle{/tex}ADB = 180°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 62° + 58° + {tex}\angle{/tex}ADB = 180°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}ADB = 180° - 120° = 60°
Now, {tex}\angle{/tex}ACB = {tex}\angle{/tex}ADB = 60° (Angles in same segment are equal)

questions.question: AD is a diameter of a circle and AB is a chord. If AD = 34 cm, AB = 30 cm, then the distance of AB from the centre of the circle is _______.
questions.explanation:
Given, AD = 34 cm, AB = 30 cm
{tex}\therefore \quad A O=\frac{1}{2} A D=\frac{1}{2}(34)=17 \mathrm{~cm}{/tex}
Draw OP {tex}\perp{/tex} AB.
Since, perpendicular drawn from centre to the chord bisects the chord.
{tex}\therefore \quad A P=\frac{1}{2} A B=15 \mathrm{~cm}{/tex}

Now, in right angled {tex}\triangle{/tex}APO
(OP)2 = (17)2 - (15)2 = 289 - 225 = 64
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} OP = 8 cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Distance of AB from the centre of the circle is 8 cm.

questions.question: In the given figure, {tex}\angle{/tex}PQR = 150°, where P, Q and R are points on a circle with centre O. Then {tex}\angle{/tex}OPR is ________.
questions.explanation:
Given, {tex}\angle{/tex}PQR = 150°
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Reflex {tex}\angle{/tex}POR = 2{tex}\angle{/tex}PQR = 2(150°) = 300°
Now, {tex}\angle{/tex}POR = 360° - Reflex {tex}\angle{/tex}POR = 360° - 300° = 60° ...(i)
Also, OP = OR {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}OPR = {tex}\angle{/tex}ORP ...(ii) (Angles opposite to equal sides of a triangle are equal)
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}OPR, {tex}\angle{/tex}OPR + {tex}\angle{/tex}ORP + {tex}\angle{/tex}POR = 180°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2{tex}\angle{/tex}OPR + 60° = 180° [From (i) & (ii)]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2{tex}\angle{/tex}OPR = 120° {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}OPR = 60°

questions.question: PQRS is a cyclic quadrilateral such that PR is a diameter of the circle. If {tex}\angle{/tex}QPR = 67° and {tex}\angle{/tex}SPR = 72°, then {tex}\angle{/tex}QRS =______.
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle{/tex}QPS = {tex}\angle{/tex}QPR + {tex}\angle{/tex}SPR = 67° + 72° = 139°
PQRS is a cyclic quadrilateral.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}QRS + {tex}\angle{/tex}QPS = 180°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}QRS= 180°-139° = 41°

questions.question: In the given figure, PQRS is a cyclic quadrilateral in which PS = RS, {tex}\angle{/tex}SQR = x and {tex}\angle{/tex}PQS = 60°. The value of x is_______.
questions.explanation:
: Given, PS = RS, {tex}\angle{/tex}PQS = 60° and ZSQR = x
Since, angles in the same segment are equal.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}SPR = {tex}\angle{/tex}SQR = x
Also, ZSPR = ZSRP ({tex}\because{/tex} PS = RS)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}SRP = x
{tex}\angle{/tex}SRP = {tex}\angle{/tex}PQS (Angles in the same segment are equal)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 60°

questions.question: A, B, C and D are four points on a circle. AC and BD intersect at a point E such that {tex}\angle{/tex}BEC = 130° and {tex}\angle{/tex}ECD = 20°, then {tex}\angle{/tex}BAC is ________.
questions.explanation:
Given, {tex}\angle{/tex}BEC = 130° and {tex}\angle{/tex}ECD = 20°
Now, {tex}\angle{/tex}ABD = {tex}\angle{/tex}ACD (Angles in the same segment)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}ABD = 20° Now, in {tex}\triangle{/tex}AEB
{tex}\angle{/tex}EBA + {tex}\angle{/tex}BAE = {tex}\angle{/tex}BEC (exterior angle property)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 20° + ZBAC = 130° {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} ZBAC = 110°

questions.question: In the given figure, O is the centre of the circle. Find the values of x, y and z.
questions.explanation:
Since ABCD is a cyclic quadrilateral
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x + 3x = 180° {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4x = 180°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 45°
Now, {tex}\angle{/tex}BOD = 2{tex}\angle{/tex}BAD ({tex}\because{/tex} Angle subtended by an arc at the centre is double the angle subtended by it at any point on the remaining part of the circle)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} y = 2x = 2 x 45° = 90°
Similarly, z = 2(3x) = 6 x 45° = 270°.

questions.question: In the given figure, AEDF is a cyclic quadrilateral. The values of x and y respectively are
questions.explanation:
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}AEB, {tex}\angle{/tex}ABE + {tex}\angle{/tex}BEA + {tex}\angle{/tex}BAE = 180°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 35° + {tex}\angle{/tex}BEA + 54° = 180°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}BEA = 91°
Now, {tex}\angle{/tex}AFD + {tex}\angle{/tex}DEA = 180° (opposite angles of cyclic quadrilateral)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x + 91° = 180° => x = 89°
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}AFC, {tex}\angle{/tex}AFC + {tex}\angle{/tex}FCA + {tex}\angle{/tex}CAF = 180°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 89° + y + 54° = 180° {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = 37°

questions.question: O is the centre of the circle having radius 5 cm. AB and AC are two chords such that AB = 6 cm. If OA meets BC at P, then OP = ________.
questions.explanation:
We have, AB = AC = 6 cm
OB = OC = 5 cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} ABOC is a kite.
Since, diagonals of kite are perpendicular to one another.
{tex}\angle{/tex}OPB = {tex}\angle{/tex}OPC = 90°
Let OP = x {tex}\therefore{/tex} AP = O A - OP = 5 - x ...(i)
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}OPB, (OB)2 = (OP)2 + (PB)2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (5)2 = x2 + (PB)2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (PB)2 = 25 -x2 ...(ii)
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}APB, (AB)2 = (AP)2 + (PB)2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (6)2 = (5 - x)2 + (PB)2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (PB)2 = (6)2 - (5 - x)2 ...(iii)
From (ii) and (iii), we have
25 - x2 = (6)2 - (5 - x)2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 25 - x2 = 36 - 25 - x2 + 10x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 14 = 10x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 1.4 cm

questions.question: In the given figure, if O is the centre of the circle, then x = ________.
questions.explanation:
Since, angle subtended by an arc at centre is double the angle subtended by it on the remaining part of the circle.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}AOB = 2{tex}\angle{/tex}ACB = 2 x 29° = 58° {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 58°

questions.question: In the given figure, AOB is the diameter of a circle and CD || AB. If {tex}\angle{/tex}BAD = 30°, then {tex}\angle{/tex}CAD =_____.
questions.explanation:
Given that, CD || AB and {tex}\angle{/tex}BAD = 30°
{tex}\because{/tex} AOB is the diameter.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}ADB = 90° (Angle in semicircle)
Now, In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ADB, {tex}\angle{/tex}DAB + {tex}\angle{/tex}ADB + {tex}\angle{/tex}ABD = 180°
30° + 90° + {tex}\angle{/tex}ABD = 180° {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}ABD = 60°
In cyclic quadrilateral ABDC, we have
{tex}\angle{/tex}ABD + {tex}\angle{/tex}ACD = 180°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 60° + {tex}\angle{/tex}ACD = 180° {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}ACD = 120°
Also, {tex}\angle{/tex}CDA = 30° = {tex}\angle{/tex}DAB (Alternate angles)
In A CAD, {tex}\angle{/tex}CAD + {tex}\angle{/tex}CDA + {tex}\angle{/tex}ACD = 180°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}CAD + 30° + 120° = 180° {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}CAD = 30°

questions.question: In the given figure, a circle touches all the four sides of a quadrilateral ABCD whose three sides are AB = 6 cm, BC = 7 cm, CD = 4 cm, then AD equals ________.
questions.explanation:
x + y = 6 cm ...(i)
y + z = 7 cm ...(ii)
z + w = 4 cm ...(iii)
Using (ii) and (iii),
y - w = 3 cm
Now using (i);
x + (3 + w) = 6 cm
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x + w = 3 cm
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} AD = 3 cm

questions.question: In the given figure, AB and PQ intersect at M. If A and B are centres of circles, then ________.
questions.explanation:
In the given figure, PQ is the common chord of the two circles.
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} AB bisects the common chord PQ at M.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} PM = MQ
Moreover, PQ is perpendicular to AB.

questions.question: In the given figure, O is the centre of the circle, then {tex}\angle{/tex}XOZ is ________.
questions.explanation:
OX = OY = OZ = r (radii of circle)
Taking {tex}\angle{/tex}OXY = {tex}\theta{/tex} and {tex}\angle{/tex}OZY = {tex}\alpha{/tex}
Then in {tex}\triangle{/tex}YXO, OX = OY {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}OXY = {tex}\angle{/tex}OYX = {tex}\theta{/tex}
Now, in {tex}\triangle{/tex}OZY, OZ = OY {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}OZY = {tex}\angle{/tex}OYZ = {tex}\alpha{/tex}
Now, XOZY is a quadrilateral.
{tex}\Rightarrow \angle O X Y+\angle X Y Z+\angle O Z Y+\angle X O Z=360^{\circ}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \theta+\theta+\alpha+\alpha+\angle X O Z=360^{\circ}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \angle X O Z=360^{\circ}-2(\alpha+\theta){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \angle X O Z=360^{\circ}-2(\angle O Z X{/tex}{tex}+\angle X Z Y+\angle O X Z+\angle Z X Y){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \angle X O Z=360^{\circ}-2(\angle O Z X+\angle O X Z){/tex}{tex}-2(\angle X Z Y+\angle Z X Y){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \angle X O Z=360^{\circ}-2{/tex}{tex}\left(180^{\circ}-\angle X O Z\right)-2(\angle X Z Y+\angle Z X Y){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \angle X O Z=360^{\circ}-360^{\circ}{/tex}{tex}+2 \angle X O Z-2(\angle X Z Y+\angle Z X Y){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \angle X O Z=2(\angle X Z Y+\angle Z X Y){/tex}

questions.question: In the given figure, A and B are the centres of two circles that intersect at X and Y. PXQ is a straight line. If reflex angle QBY = 210o, then find obtuse angle PAY.
questions.explanation:
In quadrilateral YBQX

{tex}\angle{/tex}YXQ = {tex}\frac 12{/tex} reflex {tex}\angle{/tex}QBY = {tex}\frac 12{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 210o = 105o
PXQ is straight line 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}PXY + {tex}\angle{/tex}YXQ = 180o (linear pair)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}PXY = 180o - 105o = 75o
Now, in quadrilateral PAYX
{tex}\angle{/tex}PZY = {tex}\frac 12{/tex}{tex}\angle{/tex}PAY
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}PAY = 2{tex}\angle{/tex}PZY = 150o

questions.question: OACB is a quadrant of a circle with centre O and radius 7 cm where ACB is the arc. Then the perimeter of the quadrant is:
The length of an arc of a circle with radius 12 cm is 10{tex}\pi{/tex} cm. The central angle subtended by this arc at the centre, is:
questions.explanation:
Radius of circle = 12 cm
Arc length = 10 {tex}\pi{/tex} cm
And
Arc length = {tex}\frac{\theta}{360} \times 2 \pi r{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 10 \pi =\frac{\theta}{360} \times 2 \times \pi \times 12{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \theta=150^{\circ}{/tex}

questions.question: What should be known by the architect to justify the construction:
questions.explanation: Properties and theorems

questions.question: Will the architect the able to draw an angle of 75o with ruler and compass only:
questions.explanation: Yes

questions.question: What will be the measure of each angle of 75o angle is bisected once:
questions.explanation: Half of 75o is 37o or {tex}37 \frac{1^{\circ}}{2}{/tex}.

questions.question: If the other base angle is 60o, what will be the measure of the third angle:
questions.explanation:
Third angle = x
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 60o + 75o + x = 180o (Angle sum property of triangle)
x = 180 - 135
x = 45o

questions.question: Which of the following sets of angles can be angle of triangle:
questions.explanation:
The sum of all 3 angles of a {tex}\triangle{/tex} = 180o
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 50o + 30o + 100o = 180

questions.question: Area of triangle ABC is
questions.explanation: Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC = {tex}\frac {1}{2}{/tex}(Base) {tex}\times{/tex} height = {tex}\frac {1}{2}{/tex}(10) {tex}\times{/tex} 12 = 60 cm2

questions.question: Length of the side PQ
questions.explanation:
Since PQ||BC and P is mid-point of AB
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Q is mid-point of AC
{tex}\therefore{/tex} PQ = {tex}\frac {1}{2}{/tex}BC = {tex}\frac {1}{2}{/tex}(10) = 5 cm

questions.question: Length of AQ is
questions.explanation: Since Q is mid-point of AC in AQ = {tex}\frac{1}{2}({AC})=\frac{11}{2}{/tex}= 5.5 cm

questions.question: Length of PB is
questions.explanation:
Since P is mid-point of AB
{tex}\therefore{/tex}PB = AP = 7 cm

questions.question: Length of QC is
questions.explanation:
QC = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}(AC) [{tex}\because{/tex} Q is mid-point of AC]
{tex}=\frac{1}{2}{/tex}(11) = 5.5 cm

questions.question: {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABE {tex}\cong \triangle{/tex}EFC
questions.explanation: SAS [DE = EF, {tex}\angle{/tex}AED = {tex}\angle{/tex}CEF]

questions.question: {tex}\angle{/tex}EFC =
questions.explanation: {tex}\angle{/tex}ECF = {tex}\angle{/tex}ADE [{tex}\therefore \triangle ADE \cong \triangle EFC{/tex}]

questions.question: {tex}\angle{/tex}ECF is equal to which angle?
questions.explanation: {tex}\angle{/tex}ECF = {tex}\angle{/tex}DAE [{tex}\therefore \triangle ADE \cong \triangle EFC{/tex}]

questions.question: CF =
questions.explanation: {tex}\triangle A B E \cong \triangle E F C \therefore{/tex} CF = AD = BD [{tex}\because{/tex} D is mid-point of AB]

questions.question: CF is || to which of the following?
questions.explanation:
Since {tex}\angle{/tex}EFC = {tex}\angle{/tex}ADE [Alternate angles]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} AD || CF i.e. BD || CF

questions.question: The measure of each side of an equilateral triangle whose area is {tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex} cm2 is
questions.explanation:
Area of equilateral triangle = {tex}\frac{\sqrt{3}a^2}{4}{/tex} where a = side of the triangle
{tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\sqrt{3}a^2}{4}{/tex}
Solving
a2 = 4
a = 2 cm

questions.question: The perimeter of a triangle is 60cm. If its sides are in the ratio 1:3:2, then its smallest side is
questions.explanation:
Given: Ratio of sides: 1 : 3 : 2
Let the sides of triangle be x, 3x and 2x cm
Perimeter = 60 cm
x + 3x + 2x = 60
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 6x = 60
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 10 
So, sides are
a = 1 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 = 10 cm
b = 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 = 30 cm
c = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 = 20 cm
Therefore, Length of smallest side = 10 cm.

questions.question: The side of a triangle is 12 cm, 16 cm, and 20 cm. Its area is
questions.explanation:
{tex}s = {{12 + 16 + 20} \over 2} = {{48} \over 2}{/tex} = 24 cm
Area of triangle  = {tex}\sqrt {s\left( {s - a} \right)\left( {s - b} \right)\left( {s - c} \right)} {/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt {24\left( {24 - 16} \right)\left( {24 - 12} \right)\left( {24 - 20} \right)} {/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt {24 \times 8 \times 12 \times 4} {/tex} = 12 {tex}\times{/tex} 8 = 96 sq cm

questions.question: If side of equilateral triangle is 25 m. Its area is
questions.explanation:
Area of equilateral triangle = {tex}{{\sqrt 3 } \over 4}{\left( {{\rm{Side}}} \right)^2}{/tex} 
= {tex}{{\sqrt 3 } \over 4}{\left( {{\rm{25}}} \right)^2}{/tex}
= {tex}{{625\sqrt 3 } \over 4}{/tex}  sq.cm

questions.question: The perimeter of an equilateral triangle is 48 cm. Its area is
questions.explanation:
Side = {tex}{{48} \over 3}{/tex} = 16 cm
Area ={tex}{{\sqrt 3 } \over 4} \times 16 \times 16{/tex} = {tex}64\sqrt 3 {/tex} sq cm

questions.question: The sides of a triangle are 35 cm, 54 cm and 61 cm, respectively. The length of its longest altitude
questions.explanation:
Let ABC be a triangle in which sides AB = 35cm, BC = 54 cm and CA = 61 cm

Now, semi-perimeter of a triangle,
{tex}s=\frac{a+b+c}{2}{/tex}{tex}=\frac{35+54+61}{2}=\frac{150}{2}=75 \mathrm{cm}{/tex}
[{tex}\because{/tex} semiperimeter, s = {tex}\frac{a+b+c}{2}{/tex}]
{tex}\because{/tex} Area of {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC {tex}=\sqrt{s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)}{/tex} [by Heron's formula]
{tex}=\sqrt{75(75-35)(75-54)(75-61)}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{75 \times 40 \times 21 \times 14}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{25 \times 3 \times 4 \times 2 \times 5 \times 7 \times 3 \times 7 \times 2}{/tex}
{tex}=5 \times 2 \times 2 \times 3 \times 7 \sqrt{5}{/tex}
{tex}=420 \sqrt{5} \mathrm{cm}^{2}{/tex}
Also, Area of {tex}\triangle A B C=\frac{1}{2} \times A B \times \text { Altitude }{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{2} \times 35 \times C D{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow C D=\frac{420 \times 2 \sqrt{5}}{35}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} CD {tex}=24 \sqrt{5}{/tex}
Hence, the length of altitude is {tex}24 \sqrt{5} \mathrm{cm}{/tex}.

questions.question: The sides of a triangle are 16 cm, 30 cm, 34 cm. Its area is
questions.explanation:
Let a = 16 cm, b = 30 cm, c = 34 cm
Semi-perimeter of a triangle = {tex}\frac{a+b+c}{2}=\frac{16+30+34}{2}{/tex} = 40
Now, s - a = 24 cm, s - b = 10 cm and s - c = 6 cm
By Heron's formula. Area of a triangle = {tex}\sqrt{s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{40 \times 24 \times 10 \times 6}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{4 \times 10 \times 4 \times 6 \times 10 \times 6} {/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{4^{2} \times 10^{2} \times 6^{2}} {/tex}
= 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 {tex}\times{/tex} 6
= 240 cm2

questions.question: The base of an isosceles right triangle is 30 cm. Its area is
questions.explanation:
Since triangle ABC is an isosceles triangle.
Base = 30
Hence, height = 30 cm
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Area of triangle ABC {tex}=\frac{1}{2} \times A B \times B C=\frac{1}{2} \times 30 \times 30=450 \mathrm{cm}^{2}{/tex}

questions.question: The sides of a triangle are 7 cm, 9 cm and 14 cm. Its area is
questions.explanation:
Let a = 7 cm, b = 9 cm, c = 14 cm
Semi-perimeter = s = {tex}\frac{a+b+c}{2}=\frac{7+9+14}{2}{/tex} = 15 cm
s - a = 15 - 7 = 8cm, s - b = 15 - 9 = 6 cm and s - c = 15 - 14 = 1 cm
Are of a triangle = {tex}\sqrt{s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{15 \times 8 \times 6 \times 1}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{5 \times 3 \times 4 \times 2 \times 3 \times 2}{/tex}
= {tex}12 \sqrt{5} \mathrm{cm}^{2}{/tex}

questions.question: The sides of a triangle are 325 m, 300 m and 125 m. Its area is
questions.explanation:
a = 325 m, b = 300 m, c = 125 m
s = {tex}\frac{a+b+c}{2}=\frac{325+300+125}{2}{/tex} = 375 m
s - a = 50 m, s - b = 75 m , s - c = 250 m
Area = {tex}\sqrt{s}(s-a)(s-b)(s-c){/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{375 \times 50 \times 75 \times 250}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{15 \times 25 \times 25 \times 2 \times 3 \times 25 \times 25 \times 10}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{25 \times 25 \times 25 \times 25 \times 30 \times 30}{/tex}
= 25 {tex}\times{/tex} 25 {tex}\times{/tex} 30
= 18750 m2

questions.question: The sides of a triangle are 11 m, 60 m and 61 m. The altitude to the smallest side is
questions.explanation:
Area of {tex}\Delta=\frac{1}{2} \text { Base } \times \text { Height }{/tex}
The smallest side is 11 m
Area = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \times 11 \times{/tex} Height .. (i)
Area by Heron's Formula = {tex}\sqrt{s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)}{/tex}
s {tex}\frac{11+60+61}{2}{/tex} = 66 m
Area = {tex}\sqrt{66 \times 55 \times 6 \times 5}{/tex} = 330 m2
From eq (i)
330 = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \times 11 \times{/tex} height
Height = {tex}\frac{2 \times 330}{11}{/tex} = 60 m

questions.question: The sides of a triangle are 11 cm, 15 cm and 16 cm. The altitude to the largest side is
questions.explanation:
s = {tex}\frac{11+15+16}{2}{/tex} = 21 cm
Area of {tex}=\Delta=\sqrt{s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{21 \times 10 \times 6 \times 5}=30 \sqrt{7} \mathrm{cm}^{2}{/tex}
Also if we choose largest side and its Altitude, the area would be
A = {tex}\frac{1}{2} {/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} largest side {tex}\times{/tex} h
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{2} \times 16 \times h=30 \sqrt{7}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow h=\frac{30 \sqrt{7}}{8}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{15 \sqrt{7}}{4}{/tex}cm

questions.question: The base and hypotenuse of a right triangle are respectively 5 cm and 13 cm long. it is area is:
questions.explanation:
AB = {tex}\sqrt{(13)^{2}-(5)^{2}}={/tex} 12 cm
Area = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \times \mathrm{BC} \times \mathrm{AB}=\frac{1}{2} \times 5 \times 12{/tex}
= 30 cm2

questions.question: The lengths of the sides of {tex}\Delta{/tex}ABCare consecutive integers. It {tex}\Delta{/tex}ABC has perimeter as an equilateral triangle triangle with a side of length 9 cm, what is the length of the shortest side of {tex}\Delta{/tex}ABC?
questions.explanation:
Let the sides of {tex}\Delta{/tex}ABC be n, n + 1, n + 2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Perimeter = n + n + 1 + n + 2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}(9 + 9 + 9) = 3n + 3
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 27 = 3n + 3
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3n = 24
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n = 8 cm
Thus, the shortest side is 8 cm

questions.question: If the length of a median of an equilateral triangle is x cm, then its area, is
questions.explanation:
Let the side of equilateral {tex}\Delta{/tex}ABC be a cm
The median of equilateral triangle is its altitude drawn from A to BC. (i.e. the height of {tex}\Delta{/tex} over Base BC)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} AD = a sin 60o
{tex}\Rightarrow x=\frac{a \sqrt{3}}{2}{/tex} [AD = x (given)
{tex}\Rightarrow a=\frac{2 x}{\sqrt{3}}{/tex}
Area of equilateral {tex}\Delta{/tex} of side a = {tex}\frac{\sqrt{3} a^{2}}{4}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4}\left(\frac{2 x}{\sqrt{3}}\right)^{2}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{\sqrt{3}}{/tex}

questions.question: A square and an equilateral triangle have equal perimeters. If the diagonal of the square is 12{tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex} cm, then area of the triangle is
questions.explanation:
If side of a square is a cm then, its diagonal = {tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex} a cm
But diagonal = 12{tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex} cm
{tex}\Rightarrow \sqrt{2}{/tex} a = 12{tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = 12 cm
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Perimeter of a square = 4a = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 12= 48cm
Now, perimeter of an equilateral triangle with side x = 3x cm
But, perimeter of equilateral triangle = Perimeter of square
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x = 48
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 16 cm
Now, Area of equilateral {tex}\Delta=\frac{\sqrt{3} x^{2}}{4}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 16 {tex}\times{/tex} 16 = 64{tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex} cm2

questions.question: The sides of a triangle are 50 cm, 78 cm and 112 cm. The smallest altitude is
questions.explanation:
The smallest altitude is {tex}\perp{/tex} drawn to the largest side of a {tex}\Delta{/tex} from opposite point.
i.e. BD Area of {tex}\Delta=\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} Ac {tex}\times{/tex} BD = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 112 {tex}\times{/tex} BD = 56 {tex}\times{/tex}BD
s = {tex}\frac{50+78+112}{2}{/tex} = 120cm
s - AB = 70 cm, s - BC = 42 cm, s - AC = 8 cm
Area = {tex}\sqrt{s(s-A B)-(s-B C)(s-A C)}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{120 \times 70 \times 42 \times 8}{/tex}
= 1680 cm2
Now, 56{tex}\times{/tex}BD = 1680 cm2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} BD= {tex}\frac{1680}{56}{/tex} = 30 cm

questions.question: In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC, it is given that base = 12 cm and height = 5 cm. Its area is
questions.explanation:
30 cm2
Area of triangle {tex}=\frac{1}{2} \times{/tex} Base {tex}\times{/tex} Height
Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC {tex}=\frac{1}{2} \times 12 \times 5{/tex}
= 30 cm2

questions.question: Each side of an equilateral triangle measures 8 cm. The area of the triangle is
questions.explanation:
Area of equilateral triangle  {tex}=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} \times(\text { Side })^{2}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} \times(8)^{2}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} \times 64{/tex}
{tex}=16 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{cm}^{2}{/tex}

questions.question: The base of an isosceles triangle is 8 cm long and each of its equal sides measures 6 cm. The area of the triangle is
questions.explanation:
Area of isosceles triangle {tex}=\frac{b}{4} \sqrt{4 a^{2}-b^{2}}{/tex}
Here,
a = 6 cm and b = 8 cm
Thus, we have
{tex}\frac{8}{4} \times \sqrt{4(6)^{2}-8^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{8}{4} \times \sqrt{144-64}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{8}{4} \times \sqrt{80}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{8}{4} \times 4 \sqrt{5}{/tex}
{tex}=8 \sqrt{5} \mathrm{cm}^{2}{/tex}

questions.question: The base of an isosceles triangle is 6 cm and each of its equal sides is 5 cm. The height of the triangle is
questions.explanation:
Height of isosceles triangle {tex}=\frac{1}{2} \sqrt{4 a^{2}-b^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{2} \sqrt{4(5)^{2}-6^{2}}{/tex} (a = 5 cm and b = 6 cm)
{tex}=\frac{1}{2} \times \sqrt{100-36}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{2} \times \sqrt{64}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{2} \times 8{/tex}
= 4 cm

questions.question: Each of the two equal sides of an isosceles right triangle is 10 cm long. Its area is
questions.explanation:
Here, the base and height of the triangle are 10 cm and 10 cm, respectively.
Thus, we have
Area of triangle {tex}=\frac{1}{2} \times{/tex} Base {tex}\times{/tex} Height
{tex}=\frac{1}{2} \times 10 \times 10{/tex}
= 50 cm2

questions.question: Each side of an equilateral triangle is 10 cm long. The height of the triangle is
questions.explanation:
Height of equilateral triangle {tex}=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \times{/tex} Side
{tex}=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \times 10{/tex}
{tex}=5 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{cm}{/tex}

questions.question: The lengths of the three sides of a triangular field are 40 m, 24 m and 32 m respectively. The area of the triangle is
questions.explanation:
Let:
a = 40 m, b = 24 m and c = 32 m
 S {tex}=\frac{40+24+32}{2}{/tex} = 48 m
By Heron's formula, we have:
Area of triangle {tex}=\sqrt{s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{48(48-40)(48-24)(48-32)}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{48 \times 8 \times 24 \times 16}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{24 \times 2 \times 8 \times 24 \times 8 \times 2}{/tex}
{tex}=24 \times 8 \times 2{/tex}
= 384 m2

questions.question: The sides of a triangle are in the ratio 5 : 12 : 13 and its perimeter is 150 cm. The area of the triangle is
questions.explanation:
Let the sides of the triangle be 5x cm, 12x cm and 13x cm.
Perimeter = Sum of all sides
or, 150 = 5x + 12x + 13x
or, 30x = 150
or, x = 5
Thus, the sides of the triangle are 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 cm, 12 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 cm and 13 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 cm, i.e., 25 cm, 60 cm and 65 cm.
Now,
Let:
a = 25 cm, b = 60 cm and c = 65 cm
{tex}s=\frac{150}{2}=75 \mathrm{cm}{/tex}
By Heron's formula, we have:
Area of triangle {tex}=\sqrt{s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{75(75-25)(75-60)(75-65)}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{75 \times 50 \times 15 \times 10}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{15 \times 5 \times 5 \times 10 \times 15 \times 10}{/tex}
{tex}=15 \times 5 \times 10{/tex}
= 750 cm2

questions.question: The lengths of the three sides of a triangle are 30 cm, 24 cm and 18 cm respectively. The length of the altitude of the triangle corresponding to the smallest side is
questions.explanation:
Let:
a = 30 cm, b = 24 cm and c = 18 cm
{tex}s=\frac{a+b+c}{2}{/tex}{tex}=\frac{30+24+18}{2}=36 \mathrm{cm}{/tex}
On applying Heron's formula, we get:
Area of triangle {tex}=\sqrt{s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{36(36-30)(36-24)(36-18)}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{36 \times 6 \times 12 \times 18}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{12 \times 3 \times 12 \times 6 \times 3}{/tex}
{tex}=12 \times 3 \times 6{/tex}
= 216 cm2
The smallest side is 18 cm.
Hence, the altitude of the triangle corresponding to 18 cm is given by:
Area of triangle = 216 cm2
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{2} \times{/tex} Base {tex}\times{/tex} Height = 216 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Height {tex}=\frac{216 \times 2}{18}{/tex} = 24 cm

questions.question: The area of an equilateral triangle is {tex}81 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{cm}^{2}{/tex}. Its height is
questions.explanation:
Area of equilateral triangle {tex}=81 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{cm}^{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} \times(\text { Side })^{2}=81 \sqrt{3}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (Side)2 {tex}=81 \times 4{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (Side)2 = 324
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Side = 18 cm
Now,
Height {tex}=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \times Side{/tex}{tex}=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \times 18=9 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{cm}{/tex}

questions.question: The base of an isosceles triangle is 16 cm and its area is 48 cm2. The perimeter of the triangle is
questions.explanation:
Let {tex}\triangle{/tex}PQR be an isosceles triangle and PX {tex}\perp{/tex} QR
Now,
Area of triangle = 48 cm2
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{2} \times Q R \times P X{/tex} = 48
{tex}\Rightarrow h=\frac{96}{16}=6 \mathrm{cm}{/tex}
Also,
{tex}Q X=\frac{1}{2} \times 24{/tex} = 12 cm and PX = 12 cm
{tex}P Q=\sqrt{Q X^{2}+P X^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}a=\sqrt{8^{2}+6^{2}}=\sqrt{64+36}{/tex}{tex}=\sqrt{100}=10 \mathrm{cm}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Perimeter  = (10 + 10 + 16) cm = 36 cm

questions.question: The base of a triangle is 12cm and height is 8cm then area of triangle is
questions.explanation:
Area of triangle =  {tex}\frac {1}{2} \times base \times height{/tex}
{tex}= \frac {1}{2} \times 12 \times 8 = 48{/tex}

questions.question: An isosceles right triangle has area 8 cm2 The length of its hypotenuse is
questions.explanation:
Given, area of an isosceles right triangle = 8cm2
Area of an isosceles triangle {tex}= \frac {1}{2} (Base \times Height){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 8 = \frac {1}{2} (Base \times Base){/tex}
[{tex}\therefore{/tex} Base = height, as triangle is an isosceles triangle]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (Base)2 = 16 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Base = 4 cm

In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC, using Pythagoras theorem
Ac2 = AB2 + BC2 = 42 + 42 = 16 + 16
{tex}\Rightarrow A C^{2}=32 \Rightarrow A C=\sqrt{32} \mathrm{cm}{/tex}
[taking positive square root because length is always positive]
Hence, the length of its hypotenuse is {tex}\sqrt{32} \mathrm{cm}{/tex}.

questions.question: What is the length of equal sides?
questions.explanation:
Let x cm be the length of equal sides of the isosceles triangle.
So, x + x + 4 = 20
2x + 4 = 20
2x = 20 - 4
2x = 16
x = {tex}\frac{16}{2}{/tex} = 8 cm

= {tex}\sqrt{10(2)(2)(6)}{/tex}
= {tex}4 \sqrt{15}{/tex} cm2

questions.question: If the sides of a triangle are in the ratio 3 : 5 : 7 and its perimeter is 300 m. Find its area.
questions.explanation:
Let the sides of a triangle are
a = 3x, b = 5x, c = 7x
then a + b + c = 300
3x + 5x + 7x = 300
15x = 300
x = 20
So, a = 60, b = 100, c = 140
s = {tex}\frac{a+b+c}{2}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{300}{2}{/tex}
= 150
Area of triangle = {tex}\sqrt{s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{150(150-60)(150-100)(150-140)}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{150 \times 90 \times 50 \times 10}{/tex}
= {tex}1500 \sqrt{3}{/tex} m2

questions.question: What is the semi-perimeter of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC?
{tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} of AB =
questions.explanation: QP

questions.question: If colourful rope is to be placed along the sides of small {tex}\triangle{/tex}PQR. What is the length of the rope?

Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}PQR =
where s is the semi-perimeter of {tex}\triangle{/tex}PQR.

questions.question: Area of {tex}\triangle ABC{/tex} is

questions.question: Perimeter of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ACD is

questions.question: Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ACD is

questions.question: Area of quadrilateral ABCD is

= 180 + 126 = 306 m2

questions.question: Base of red triangle is
questions.explanation: Base of red triangle = {tex}\frac{1}{2}(3)=\frac{3}{2} \mathrm{~cm}{/tex}

questions.question: Area of one grey and red triangle is
questions.explanation:
For area of one grey and red triangle, sides are 4cm, 4 cm, 3 cm.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 2s = 4 + 4 + 3 = 11 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} s = {tex}\frac{11}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} area of one grey and red triangle
{tex}=\sqrt{\frac{11}{2}\left(\frac{11}{2}-4\right)\left(\frac{11}{2}-4\right)\left(\frac{11}{2}-3\right)}{/tex} {tex}=\sqrt{\frac{11}{2} \cdot \frac{3}{2} \cdot \frac{3}{2} \cdot \frac{5}{2}}=\frac{3}{4} \sqrt{55} \mathrm{~cm}^{2}{/tex}

questions.question: Area of one red triangle is
questions.explanation:
For area of one red triangle, sides are 2 cm, 2 cm, {tex}\frac{3}{2} \mathrm{~cm}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 2s = {tex}2+2+\frac{3}{2}=\frac{11}{2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow s=\frac{11}{4}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} area {tex}=\sqrt{\frac{11}{4}\left(\frac{11}{4}-2\right)\left(\frac{11}{4}-2\right)\left(\frac{11}{4}-\frac{3}{2}\right)}{/tex} {tex}=\sqrt{\frac{11}{4} \cdot \frac{3}{4} \cdot \frac{3}{4} \cdot \frac{5}{4}}=\frac{3}{16} \sqrt{55} \mathrm{~cm}^{2}{/tex}

questions.question: Area of grey part is

area of grey part = 4(area of one grey part) {tex}=4\left(\frac{3}{4}-\frac{3}{16}\right) \sqrt{55} \mathrm{~cm}^{2}{/tex}
{tex}=\left(3-\frac{3}{4}\right) \sqrt{55}=\frac{9}{4} \sqrt{55} \mathrm{~cm}^{2}{/tex}

questions.question: Ratio of area of red part : area of grey part is
questions.explanation:
Ratio of red part : area of grey part
{tex}=\frac{3}{16} \sqrt{55}: \frac{9}{4} \sqrt{55}=\frac{3}{16}: \frac{9}{4}{/tex} = 3 : 36 = 1 : 12

questions.question: Area of portion I is
questions.explanation:
Portion I is an isosceles triangle with sides 1, 5, 5 [Base = 1]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 2s = 1 + 5 + 5 = 11 {tex}\Rightarrow s=\frac{11}{2}{/tex}
Area of {tex}I=\sqrt{\frac{11}{2}\left(\frac{11}{2}-1\right)\left(\frac{11}{2}-5\right)\left(\frac{11}{2}-5\right)}{/tex} {tex}=\sqrt{\frac{11}{2} \times \frac{9}{2} \times \frac{1}{2} \times \frac{1}{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{5.5 \times 4.5 \times 0.5 \times 0.5}=\sqrt{6.1875}{/tex}
= 2.5 cm2 (nearly)

questions.question: Area of portion II is

questions.question: Area of portion III is

{tex}=\frac{3}{2}\left(\sqrt{1^{2}-(0.5)^{2}}\right)=\frac{3}{2} \sqrt{\frac{3}{4}}{/tex}{tex}=\frac{3 \sqrt{3}}{4}=\frac{3}{4}{/tex}(1.73) = 1.3 cm2 nearly

questions.question: Area of portion IV is
questions.explanation: Area of portion IV = {tex}\frac {1}{2}{/tex}(6)(1.5) = 4.5 cm2

questions.question: Area of portion V is
questions.explanation: Area of portion V = 4.5 cm2

questions.question: Area of wheat triangle is
questions.explanation:
For wheat, sides of the triangle ABC are 250 m, 350 m, 300 m
2s = 250 + 350 + 300 m = 900 m2
{tex}\therefore {/tex} s = 450 m
{tex}\therefore {/tex} area of this triangle = {tex}\sqrt{s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{450(450-250)(450-350)(450-300)}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{450(200)(100)(150)}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{1350000000}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{1350}(1000) \mathrm{m}^{2}{/tex} {tex}=15000 \sqrt{6} \mathrm{~m}^{2}{/tex}

questions.question: Area of potatoes and onions field is
questions.explanation:
For potatoes and onions, sides of triangle ADC are 440 m, 250 m, 330 m
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 2s = 440 + 250 + 330 = 1020
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} s = 510
{tex}\therefore{/tex} required area = {tex}\sqrt{510(510-440)(510-250)(510-330)}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{510(70)(260)(180)}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{4641} \ 600 \mathrm{~m}^{2}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} area = {tex}\sqrt{1 s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)}{/tex}

questions.question: Area of field of potatoes is

{tex}=\sqrt{4641}(300) \mathrm{m}^{2}{/tex}

questions.question: Area of field of onions is
