questions.question: Ratio of areas of potatoes and onions
questions.explanation: Required ratio = 1 : 1

questions.question: The difference between the semi-perimeter and the sides of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC are 7 cm, 5 cm and 3 cm respectively. The perimeter of the triangle is ________.
questions.explanation:
Let the sides of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC be a, b, c
Then, (s - a) = 7 cm, (s - b) = 5 cm and (s - c) = 3 cm
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (s - a) + (s - b) + (s - c) = 15
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3s - (a + b + c) = 15
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3s - 2s = 15 {tex}\left[\because s=\frac{a+b+c}{2}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} s = 15 cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Perimeter = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 15 = 30 cm

questions.question: The perimeter of a triangle is 300 m and its sides are in the ratio 3 : 5 : 7. Find its area.
questions.explanation:
Let the sides of the triangle be
a = 3x, b = 5x, c = 7x
Perimeter of triangle = 300 m
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x + 5x + 7x = 300 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 15x = 300 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 20
{tex}\therefore{/tex} a = 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 20 = 60 m, b = 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 20 = 100 m,
c = 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 20 = 140 m
Now, {tex}s=\frac{300}{2}=150 \mathrm{~m}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of triangle {tex}\sqrt{150(150-60)(150-100)(150-140)}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{150 \times 90 \times 50 \times 10} \mathrm{~m}^2=1500 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{~m}^2 .{/tex}

questions.question: In the given figure, the area of the {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC is
questions.explanation:
Here a = 11 cm, b = 4 cm, c = 8 cm
{tex}\therefore s=\frac{11+4+8}{2}=\frac{23}{2}=11.5 \mathrm{~cm}{/tex}
Area
{tex}=\sqrt{11.5 \times(11.5-11) \times(11.5-4) \times(11.5-8)}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{11.5 \times 0.5 \times 7.5 \times 3.5}=\sqrt{150.94}{/tex} = 12.29 cm2

questions.question: A triangle and a parallelogram have the same base and the same area. If the sides of the triangle are 26 cm, 28 cm and 30 cm and the parallelogram stands on the base 28 cm, then find the height of the parallelogram.
questions.explanation:
For the given triangle, we have
a = 28 cm, b = 30 cm, c = 26 cm

So, {tex}s=\frac{a+b+c}{2}=\frac{28+30+26}{2}=\frac{84}{2}{/tex} = 42 cm
Area of the triangle {tex}\sqrt{42(42-28)(42-30)(42-26)} \mathrm{cm}^2{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{42 \times 14 \times 12 \times 16} \mathrm{~cm}^2{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{112896} \mathrm{~cm}^2=336 \mathrm{~cm}^2{/tex}
As, Area of the parallelogram = Area of the triangle
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of the parallelogram = 336 cm2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Base x height = 336 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 28 x h = 336
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}h=\frac{336}{28}=12 \mathrm{~cm}{/tex}
Thus, the height of the parallelogram = 12 cm

questions.question: In the given parallelogram, find the length of the altitude from vertex A on the side DC.
questions.explanation:
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}BCD, let a = 12cm, b = 17cm and c = 25 cm.
{tex}\therefore s=\left(\frac{12+17+25}{2}\right) \mathrm{cm}=\frac{54}{2} \mathrm{~cm}{/tex} = 27 cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}BCD {tex}=\sqrt{27(27-12)(27-17)(27-25)} \mathrm{cm}^2{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{27 \times 15 \times 10 \times 2} \mathrm{~cm}^2=90 \mathrm{~cm}^2{/tex}
Now, area of parallelogram ABCD
= 2 {tex}\times{/tex} Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}BCD
= (2 {tex}\times{/tex} 90) cm2 = 180 cm2 ...(i)
Let length of altitude of parallelogram ABCD from vertex A be h cm.
Also, area of parallelogram = Base {tex}\times{/tex} Altitude
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 180 = DC {tex}\times{/tex} h [From (i)]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 180 = 12 {tex}\times{/tex} h
{tex}\Rightarrow h=\frac{180}{12}{/tex} = 15 cm
Required length of the altitude is 15 cm.

questions.question: A rhombus shaped sheet with perimeter 40 cm and one diagonal 12 cm, is painted on both sides at the rate of ₹ 5 per cm2. Find the cost of painting.
questions.explanation:
Let ABCD be a rhombus having sides AB = BC = CD = DA = x cm
Perimeter of rhombus = 40 cm [Given]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x + x + x + x = 40
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4x = 40
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 10
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC, let a = 10 cm, b = 12 cm and c = 10 cm
Now, semi-perimeter of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC, s {tex}=\frac{a+b+c}{2}{/tex}
{tex}=\left(\frac{10+10+12}{2}\right) \mathrm{cm}=\frac{32}{2} \mathrm{~cm}=16 \mathrm{~cm}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC {tex}=\sqrt{16(16-10)(16-12)(16-10)} \mathrm{cm}^2{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{16 \times 6 \times 4 \times 6} \mathrm{~cm}^2=48 \mathrm{~cm}^2{/tex}
Now, area of the rhombus ABCD
= 2(Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC) = (2 {tex}\times{/tex} 48) cm2 = 96 cm2
Cost of painting the sheet of area 1 cm2 = ₹5
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Cost of painting the sheet of area 96 cm2 = f (96 {tex}\times{/tex} 5) = ₹ 480
Thus, the cost of painting the sheet on both sides
= ₹(2 {tex}\times{/tex} 480) = ₹ 960

questions.question: The area of a triangle, two sides of which are 8 cm and 11 cm and the perimeter is 32 cm is {tex}k \sqrt{30} \mathrm{~cm}^2{/tex}. Find the value of k.
questions.explanation:
Here we have perimeter of the triangle = 32 cm
Let a = 8 cm and b = 11 cm
Third side, c = 32 - (8 + 11) = 13 cm
{tex}\therefore s=\frac{32}{2}=16 \mathrm{~cm}{/tex}
Therefore, area of the triangle
{tex}=\sqrt{s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)}=\sqrt{16 \times 8 \times 5 \times 3} \mathrm{~cm}^2{/tex}
{tex}=8 \sqrt{30} \mathrm{~cm}^2=k \sqrt{30} \mathrm{~cm}^2{/tex} (given)
{tex}\therefore k \sqrt{30}=8 \sqrt{30} \Rightarrow k=8{/tex}

questions.question: In the given figure, {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC has sides AB = 7.5 cm, AC = 6.5 cm and BC = 7 cm. On base BC a parallelogram DBCE of same area as that of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC is constructed. Find the height DF of the parallelogram.
questions.explanation:
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC, a = 7 cm, b = 6.5 cm and c = 7.5 cm
{tex}\therefore s=\left(\frac{7.5+7+6.5}{2}\right) \mathrm{cm}=\frac{21}{2} \mathrm{~cm}{/tex} = 10.5 cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC {tex}=\sqrt{10.5(10.5-7.5)(10.5-7)(10.5-6.5)} \mathrm{cm}^2{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{10.5 \times 3 \times 3.5 \times 4} \mathrm{~cm}^2=\sqrt{441} \mathrm{~cm}^2=21 \mathrm{~cm}^2{/tex}
Since, Area of AABC = Area of parallelogram BCED
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 21 = BC {tex}\times{/tex} DF {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 21 = 7 {tex}\times{/tex} DF
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}D F=\frac{21}{7}{/tex} = 3 cm

questions.question: The sides of a triangle are 11 cm, 15 cm and 16 cm. The altitude to the largest side is ________.
questions.explanation:
We have, sides of triangle 11 cm, 15 cm and 16 cm.
{tex}\therefore s=\frac{11+15+16}{2}=21 \mathrm{~cm}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of triangle {tex}=\sqrt{21(21-11)(21-15)(21-16)}{/tex}
{tex}=30 \sqrt{7} \mathrm{~cm}^2{/tex}
Let altitude to the largest side be h cm
{tex}\therefore \frac{1}{2} \times 16 \times h=30 \sqrt{7} \Rightarrow 8 h=30 \sqrt{7}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow h=\frac{15 \sqrt{7}}{4} \mathrm{~cm}{/tex}

questions.question: A field is in the shape of a trapezium whose parallel sides are 77 cm and 60 cm. The non-parallel sides are 25 cm and 26 cm. Find the area of the field.
questions.explanation:
Let ABCD be the trapezium with sides AB = 77 cm, CD = 60 cm, AD = 25 cm, BC = 26 cm.

We draw CE || AD
{tex} \therefore{/tex} Area of trapezium ABCD
= area of parallelogram {tex}\triangle{/tex}ECD + area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ECB
Now, In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ECB
{tex}s=\frac{25+17+26}{2}=34{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}EBC {tex}=\sqrt{34(34-25)(34-17)(34-26)}=204 \mathrm{~cm}^2{/tex}
Also, Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}EBC = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \times B E \times h \Rightarrow \frac{1}{2} \times 17 \times h=204{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} h = 24 cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of parallelogram AECD = AE {tex}\times{/tex} h = 60 {tex}\times{/tex} 24 = 1440 cm2
Hence, area of trapezium ABCD
= (1440 + 204) cm2 = 1644 cm2

questions.question: If the sides of a triangular field measure 51 m, 37 m and 20 m, then find the cost of levelling it at ₹ 7 per m2.
questions.explanation:
We have, {tex}s=\frac{1}{2}(51+37+20) \mathrm{m}=54 \mathrm{~m}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area {tex}=\sqrt{54(54-51)(54-37)(54-20)}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{54 \times 3 \times 17 \times 34} \mathrm{~m}^2=306 \mathrm{~m}^2{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Cost of levelling the field = ₹(306 {tex}\times{/tex} 7) = ₹ 2142

questions.question: The base of an isosceles triangle measures 24 cm and its area is 192 cm2. Find its perimeter.
questions.explanation:
Let the each of the other two equal sides of an isosceles triangle be a cm.
Then, {tex}s=\frac{a+a+24}{2}=(a+12) \mathrm{cm}{/tex}
Area of triangle = 192 cm2
{tex}\Rightarrow \sqrt{(a+12)(a+12-a)(a+12-a)(a+12-24)}{/tex} = 192
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 144 (a2 - 144) = (192)2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a2 = 400 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = 20 cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Perimeter = 20 + 20 + 24 = 64 cm

questions.question: A conical tent is made by stitching 12 triangular pieces of cloth of two different colours as shown in the given figure. Each piece measuring 11 m, 11 m and 6 m. How much cloth of each colour is required for the conical tent?
questions.explanation:
We have, a = 11 m, 5 = 11 m and c = 6 m
{tex}s=\frac{11+11+6}{2}{/tex} = 14 m
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of one triangular piece
{tex}=\sqrt{14 \times 3 \times 3 \times 8}=31.75 \mathrm{~cm}^2{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of 6 identical triangular pieces = 31.75 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 = 190.5 cm2
Similarly, area of another 6 triangular pieces = 190.5 cm2

questions.question: The perimeter of a field in the form of an equilateral triangle is 36 cm, then its area is given by
questions.explanation:
Since all the sides are equal in an equilateral triangle.
So, perimeter = a + a + a, where a is the side of equilateral triangle.
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3a = 36 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = 12 cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of equilateral triangle {tex}=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} a^2=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4}(12)^2=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} \times 144{/tex}
{tex}=36 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{~cm}^2{/tex}

questions.question: Tanya joined four triangles of cardboard to create a mask of Joker as shown in the given figure. Find the total area of the mask. {tex}\text { [Given } \sqrt{2}=1.41, \sqrt{3}=1.73 \text { ] }{/tex}
questions.explanation:
Total area of I & II part
{tex}=2 \times \frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} \times(2)^2=2 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{~cm}^2=3.46 \mathrm{~cm}^2{/tex}
In fig.(lll), {tex}s=\frac{6+11+15}{2}{/tex} = 16 cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Total area of III & IV part {tex}2 \times \sqrt{16 \times 10 \times 5 \times 1}=56.56 \mathrm{~cm}^2{/tex}
Hence, total area of the mask = (3.46 + 56.56) cm2 = 60.02 cm2

questions.question: Suman made a picture with some white paper and a single coloured paper as shown in figure. White paper is available at her home and free of cost. The cost of coloured paper used is at the rate of 10p per cm2. Find the total cost of the coloured paper used. (Take {tex}\sqrt3{/tex} = 1.732 and {tex}\sqrt{11}{/tex} = 3.32)
questions.explanation:
Area of I {tex}=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} \times(10)^2=25 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{~cm}^2=43.3 \mathrm{~cm}^2{/tex}
(II) {tex}s=\frac{10+9+3}{2}=11 \mathrm{~cm}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of II {tex}=2 \sqrt{11 \times 1 \times 2 \times 8}=8 \sqrt{11} \mathrm{~cm}^2=26.56 \mathrm{~cm}^2{/tex}
(IV) {tex}s=\frac{20+20+4}{2}=22 \mathrm{~cm}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of IV {tex}=2 \sqrt{22 \times 2 \times 2 \times 18}{/tex}
{tex}=24 \sqrt{11} \mathrm{~cm}^2=79.68 \mathrm{~cm}^2{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Total area of coloured paper used
= (43.3 + 26.56 + 79.68) cm2 = 149.54 cm2
Cost of coloured paper used = {tex}\frac{10}{100} \times 149.54=₹ 14.95{/tex}

questions.question: A design is made on a rectangular tile of dimensions 50 cm {tex}\times{/tex} 70 cm as shown in figure. The design shows 8 triangles, each of sides 26 cm, 17 cm and 25 cm. Find the total area of the design and the remaining area of the tile respectively.
questions.explanation:
Area of rectangular tile = (50 {tex}\times{/tex} 70) cm2 = 3500 cm2
We have,
a = 25 cm, b = 17 cm and c = 26 cm
{tex}\therefore s=\frac{a+b+c}{2}=\left(\frac{25+17+26}{2}\right) \mathrm{cm}=34 \mathrm{~cm}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of 1 triangle {tex}=\sqrt{s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{34(34-25)(34-17)(34-26)} \mathrm{cm}^2{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{34 \times 9 \times 17 \times 8} \mathrm{~cm}^2=204 \mathrm{~cm}^2{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Total area of 8 triangles = (204 {tex}\times{/tex} 8) cm2 = 1632 cm2
So, area of the design = 1632 cm2
Also, remaining area of the tile = (3500 - 1632) cm2 = 1868 cm2

questions.question: Find the area of quadrilateral ABCD in which AB = 9 cm, BC = 40 cm, CD = 28 cm, DA = 15 cm and {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = 90°.
questions.explanation:
In right angled {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC, using Pythagoras theorem (AC)2 = (9)2 + (40)2 = 1681
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} AC = 41 cm

{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ADC {tex}=\frac{1}{2} \times 9 \times 40{/tex} = 180 cm2
Hence, area of quadrilateral ABCD
= (180 + 126) cm2 = 306 cm2

questions.question: Each edge of a cube is increased by 40%. The % increase in the surface area is.
questions.explanation: If edge length a increases by {tex}40 \%{/tex}, new edge {tex}=1.4{/tex} a. Surface area scales with square of edge: new area {tex}=6(1.4 a)^2=6(1.96) a^ 2=1.96 \times{/tex} original. Percentage increase {tex}=(1.96-1) \times 100 \%=96 \%{/tex}.

questions.question: Surface area of sphere of diameter 14 cm is
questions.explanation:
Surface area of sphere = 4{tex}\pi{/tex}r2
Given that Diametre = 14 cm
Radius = {tex}\frac{14}{2}{/tex} = 7 cm
The surface area of sphere = 4{tex}\pi{/tex}r2
= 4 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{22}{7}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 72
= 616 cm2

questions.question: Surface area of bowl of radius r cm is
questions.explanation:
CSA of hemisphere = 2{tex}\pi{/tex}r2 
But Given that there is a bowl which is considered to be a hollow hemisphere.
So, TSA of hollow hemisphere = 2{tex}\pi{/tex}r2 + {tex}\pi{/tex}r2  (area of the brim, which is a circle)
= 3{tex}\pi{/tex}r2

questions.question: Volume of a sphere whose radius is 7 cm
questions.explanation:
Volume of sphere = {tex}{4 \over 3}{/tex}{tex}\pi{/tex}r3 ( Given r = 7cm)
= {tex}{4 \over 3}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}{22 \over 7}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 73
= {tex} {88×343 \over7×3}{/tex} 
= 1437{tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} cm3

questions.question: A conical tent is 10 m high and the radius of its base is 24 m then slant height of the tent is
questions.explanation:
Slant height of cone = {tex}\left.\sqrt{( r^{2}+h^{2}}\right){/tex} (given r = 24 m, h = 10 m)
= {tex}\sqrt{( 24)^{2}+(10)^{2}}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{576 + 100}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{676}{/tex} = 26 m

questions.question: The cost of painting a cubical box of side 3m at the rate of Rs.2 per sq.m is
questions.explanation:
Given: Side of the cube (a) = 3 m
{tex}\therefore {/tex} Surface Area of Cube = 6a2 = 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 = 54 sq. cm
Now, Cost of painting the cubical box of 1 sq. m = Rs. 2
{tex}\therefore {/tex} Cost of painting the cubical box of 54 sq. m = 54 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = Rs. 108

questions.question: Two identical solid cubes of side a are joined end-to-end. The total surface area of the resulting cuboid is:
questions.explanation:
Length of resulting cuboid = {tex}a + a = 2a{/tex}
Breadth of resulting cuboid = a
And Height of the resulting cuboid = a
{tex}\therefore {/tex} TSA of cuboid = {tex}2\left( {lb + bh + hl} \right){/tex}
= {tex}2\left( {2a \times a + a \times a + a \times 2a} \right){/tex} 
= {tex}2\left( {5{a^2}} \right) = 10{a^2}{/tex}

questions.question: If two solid-hemispheres of same base radius r are joined together along their bases, then curved surface area of this new solid is
questions.explanation:
Because curved surface area of a hemisphere is {tex}2 \pi r^{2}{/tex} and here, we join two solid hemisphere along their bases of radius r, from which we get a solid sphere.
Hence, the curved surface area of new solid = {tex}2 \pi r^{2}+2 \pi r^{2}=4 \pi r^{2}{/tex}

questions.question: If the radius of the sphere is 2cm, then its curved surface area is
questions.explanation:
Here the radius of the sphere = 2 cm
{tex}\therefore {/tex} CSA of sphere = 4{tex}\pi{/tex}r2 = 4{tex}\pi{/tex}(2)3
= 16{tex}\pi{/tex}cm2

questions.question: The TSA of a solid hemisphere of diameter 2cm is
questions.explanation:
Given: Diameter of hemisphere = 2 cm, then Radius = {tex}\frac{2}{2}{/tex} = 1 cm
{tex}\therefore {/tex} 3{tex}\pi{/tex}r2 = 3{tex}\pi{/tex}(1)2 = 3{tex}\pi{/tex} cm2

questions.question: If the volume of a sphere is {tex}\frac{9}{{16}}\pi {\text{ }}{/tex} cm3, then its radius is
questions.explanation:
Given: Volume of sphere = {tex}\frac{9}{{16}}\pi {/tex}{tex}c{m^3}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}\frac{4}{3}\pi {r^3} = \frac{9}{{16}}\pi {/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} r3 = {tex}\frac{9}{{16}} \times \frac{3}{4}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} r3 = {tex}\frac{{3 \times 3}}{{4 \times 4}} \times \frac{3}{4}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} r = {tex}\frac{3}{4}{/tex} cm

questions.question: If the surface area of a sphere is100{tex}\pi{/tex} sq.cm, then its radius is
questions.explanation:
Given: Surface Area = {tex}100\pi {/tex} sq. cm
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} 4{tex}\pi{/tex}r2 = 100{tex}\pi{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} r2 = 25
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} r  = 5 cm

questions.question: If the TSA of a solid hemisphere is 12{tex}\pi{/tex} sq. cm, then its CSA is
questions.explanation:
Given: TSA of solid hemisphere = 12{tex}\pi{/tex} sq. cm
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} 3{tex}\pi{/tex}r2 = 12{tex}\pi{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} r2 = 4
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} r = 2 cm
{tex}\therefore {/tex} CSA of hemisphere = 2{tex}\pi{/tex}r2 = 2{tex}\pi{/tex}(2)3 = 8{tex}\pi{/tex} sq. cm

questions.question: Two right circular cones have equal radii. If their slant heights are in the ratio 4 : 3, then their respective curved surface areas are in the ratio
questions.explanation:
Let {tex}{l_1}{/tex} and {tex}{l_2}{/tex} be the slant heights of two cones respectively.
Given: {tex}\frac{{{l_1}}}{{{l_2}}} = \frac{4}{3}{/tex}
Now, required ratio = {tex}\frac{{\pi r{l_1}}}{{\pi r{l_2}}} = \frac{{{l_1}}}{{{l_2}}} = \frac{4}{3}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} CSA1:CSA2 = 4 : 3

questions.question: The volumes of two spheres are in the ratio 125: 64. The ratio of their surface areas is
questions.explanation:
Let r1 and r2 be the radius of the two spheres respectively. Therefore, the ratio of their surface areas,
{tex}\frac{{\frac{4}{3}\pi r_1^3}}{{\frac{4}{3}\pi r_2^3}} = \frac{{125}}{{64}}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}\frac{{r_1^3}}{{r_2^3}} = \frac{{125}}{{64}}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}{\left( {\frac{{{r_1}}}{{{r_2}}}} \right)^3} = {\left( {\frac{5}{4}} \right)^3}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}\frac{{{r_1}}}{{{r_2}}} = \frac{5}{4}{/tex}
Now, Ratio of their surface area,
{tex}\frac{{4\pi r_1^2}}{{4\pi r_2^2}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{{r_1^2}}{{r_2^2}}{/tex} = {tex}{\left( {\frac{{{r_1}}}{{{r_2}}}} \right)^2}{/tex} = {tex}{\left( {\frac{5}{4}} \right)^2} = \frac{{25}}{{16}}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore {/tex} SA1:SA2 = 25 : 16

questions.question: The maximum volume of a cone that can be carved out of a solid hemisphere of radius ‘r’ is
questions.explanation:
Volume of cone = {tex}\frac{1}{3}\pi {r^2}h{/tex}
Here height of the carved out cone = Radius of the hemisphere
{tex}\therefore {/tex} Volume of cone = {tex}\frac{1}{3}\pi {r^2} \times r{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{3}\pi {r^3}{/tex}

questions.question: The surface areas of two spheres are in the ratio 16: 9. The ratio of their volumes is
questions.explanation:
Let {tex}{r_1}{/tex} and {tex}{r_2}{/tex} be the radius of the two spheres respectively.
Therefore, the ratio of their surface Areas,
{tex}\frac{{4\pi r_1^2}}{{4\pi r_2^2}} = \frac{{16}}{9}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}\frac{{r_1^2}}{{r_2^2}} = \frac{{{{\left( 4 \right)}^2}}}{{{{\left( 3 \right)}^2}}}{/tex} {tex}\frac{{{r_1}}}{{{r_2}}} = \frac{4}{3}{/tex}
Now, ratio of their volumes,
{tex}\frac{{\frac{4}{3}\pi r_1^3}}{{\frac{4}{3}\pi r_2^3}}{/tex} {tex} = \frac{{r_1^3}}{{r_2^3}}{/tex} = {tex}{\left( {\frac{{{r_1}}}{{{r_2}}}} \right)^3} = {\left( {\frac{4}{3}} \right)^3}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{{64}}{{27}}{/tex} = 64 : 27

questions.question: The ratio of the volumes of two cones with equal heights and ratio of their radii as 2 : 5 is
questions.explanation:
Let r1, r2 be the radii of the two cones respectively, then Required ratio,
{tex}\frac{{\frac{1}{3}\pi r_1^2h}}{{\frac{1}{3}\pi r_2^2h}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{{r_1^2}}{{r_2^2}}{/tex}
= {tex}{\left( {\frac{{{r_1}}}{{{r_2}}}} \right)^2}{/tex}
= {tex}{\left( {\frac{2}{5}} \right)^2} = \frac{4}{{25}}{/tex}
= 4 : 25

questions.question: The base radii of two circular cones of the same height are in the ratio 3: 5. The ratio of their volumes is
questions.explanation:
Let base radii of two circular cones be r1 and r2 and height be h cm.
Then, the required ratio is {tex}\frac{{\pi r_1^2h}}{{\pi r_2^2h}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{{r_1^2}}{{r_2^2}} = {\left( {\frac{{{r_1}}}{{{r_2}}}} \right)^2} = {\left( {\frac{3}{5}} \right)^2} = \frac{9}{{25}}{/tex} = 9 : 25

questions.question: The volume of a spherical shell is given by
questions.explanation: The volume of a spherical shell is given by {tex}\frac{4}{3}\pi \left( {{{\text{R}}^3} - {r^3}} \right){/tex} where R = Larger radius and r = smaller radius

questions.question: The surface area of a cube whose volume is 64 cm3 is
questions.explanation:
Given: Volume of cube = 64 cm3
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} a3 = 64
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} a3 = (4)3
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} a = 4 cm
{tex}\therefore {/tex} Surface Area of cube = 6a2 = 6(4)2 = 96 sq. cm

questions.question: The height of a cone whose base area and volume are equal numerically is
questions.explanation:
According to the question,
The base area of cone = Volume of a cone
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}\pi{/tex}r2 = {tex}\frac{1}{3}\pi {r^2}h{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} h = 3 units

questions.question: If the surface area of a sphere is 36{tex}\pi{/tex} sq. cm, then its volume is
questions.explanation:
Given: Surface area of sphere = 36{tex}\pi{/tex} sq. cm
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} 4{tex}\pi{/tex}r2 = 36{tex}\pi{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} r2 = 9 
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} r = 3
{tex}\therefore {/tex} The volume of sphere = {tex}\frac{4}{3}\pi {r^3}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{4}{3}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\pi{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 = 36{tex}\pi{/tex} cu. cm

questions.question: Six solid spheres of the same size are made by melting a solid metallic cylinder of base diameter 4cm and height 16cm. The diameter of each sphere is
questions.explanation:
According to the question,
The volume of 6 solid spheres = Volume of a solid metallic cylinder
 {tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} 6 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{4}{3}\pi {r^3} = \pi {{\text{R}}^2}h{/tex} [Radius of sphere = r and Radius of cylinder = R]
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} 6 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{4}{3}{r^3}{/tex} = (2)2 {tex}\times{/tex} 16
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} r3 = {tex}\frac{{4 \times 16 \times 3}}{{6 \times 4}}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} r3 = 8
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} r = 2 cm
{tex}\therefore {/tex} Diameter of the sphere = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = 4 cm

questions.question: If three metallic spheres of radii 6cm, 8cm and 10cm are melted to form a single sphere, the diameter of the sphere is
questions.explanation:
Let {tex}{r_1},{r_2}{/tex} and {tex}{r_3}{/tex} be the radii of three metallic spheres and {tex}r{/tex} be the radius of a single sphere.
Therefore, according to the question,
{tex}\frac{4}{3}\pi r_1^3 + \frac{4}{3}\pi r_2^3 + \frac{4}{3}\pi r_3^3 = \frac{4}{3}\pi {r^3}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}\frac{4}{3}\pi \left( {r_1^3 + r_2^3 + r_3^3} \right) = \frac{4}{3}\pi {r^3}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}r_1^3 + r_2^3 + r_3^3 = {r^3}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}{\left( 6 \right)^3} + {\left( 8 \right)^3} + {\left( {10} \right)^3} = {r^3}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}216 + 512 + 1000 = {r^3}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}{r^3} = 1728{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}r = 12{/tex} cm
{tex}\therefore {/tex} Diameter of the sphere = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 12 = 24 cm

questions.question: A solid sphere of radius 3cm is melted and cast into the shape of a solid cylinder of height 1cm. The radius of the base of the cylinder is
questions.explanation:
Let the radius of the solid sphere be {tex}r{/tex} cm
and radius of the solid cylinder is R cm.
Then according to question,
{tex}\frac{4}{3}\pi {r^3}{/tex} = {tex}\pi{/tex}R2h
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}\frac{4}{3}{r^3}{/tex} = R2h
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}\frac{4}{3}{\left( 3 \right)^3}{/tex} = R2 {tex}\times{/tex} 1
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} R2 = {tex}\frac{4}{3}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} R = 6 cm

questions.question: The number of spherical balls each of radius 1cm can be made from a solid sphere of lead of radius 6cm is
questions.explanation:
Let the radius of the smaller sphere be r cm
and the radius of the bigger sphere is R cm.
Then according to question,
No. of spherical balls = {tex}\frac{{{\text{Volume of a solid sphere}}}}{{{\text{Volume of a spherical ball}}}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{{\frac{4}{3}\pi {{\text{R}}^3}}}{{\frac{4}{3}\pi {r^3}}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{{{{\text{R}}^3}}}{{{r^3}}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{{{6^3}}}{{{1^3}}}{/tex} = 216

questions.question: Two cubes of volume 125cm3 are joined end to end to form a cuboid. The surface area of the resulting cuboid is
questions.explanation:
Let the side of each cube be {tex}a{/tex} a cm.
then {tex}a = \sqrt[3]{{125}}{/tex} = 5 cm
Now, length of new cuboid = {tex}a + a = 2a{/tex} 
breadth of new cuboid = {tex}a{/tex} a
and height of the new cuboid = {tex}a{/tex} a

{tex}\therefore {/tex}Surface Area of new cuboid = {tex}2\left( {2a \times a + a \times a + a \times 2a} \right){/tex}
= {tex}2\left( {2{a^2} + {a^2} + 2{a^2}} \right){/tex}
= {tex}10{a^2}{/tex}
= {tex}10 \times {5^2}{/tex}
= 250 sq. cm

questions.question: A metallic cylinder of radius 6cm and height 8cm is melted and recast into the shape of a sphere. The radius of the sphere is
questions.explanation:
Let the radius of the cylinder be r cm
and the radius of the sphere is R cm.
Then according to the question,
The volume of cylinder = Volume of a sphere
{tex}\pi{/tex}r2h = {tex}\frac{4}{3}\pi {{\text{R}}^3}{/tex}
r2h = {tex}\frac{4}{3}{{\text{R}}^3}{/tex}
(6)2 {tex}\times{/tex} 8 = {tex}\frac{4}{3}{{\text{R}}^3}{/tex}
R3 = {tex}\frac{{36 \times 8 \times 3}}{4}{/tex}
R = 6 cm

questions.question: A right circular cone 8cm high and with base radius 2cm is melted and recast into a sphere. The radius of the sphere is
questions.explanation:
Let the radius of cone be r cm
and the radius of the sphere is R cm.
Then according to question,
The volume of cone = Volume of a sphere
{tex}\frac{1}{3}\pi {r^2}h = \frac{4}{3}\pi {{\text{R}}^3}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{1}{3}{r^2}h = \frac{4}{3}{{\text{R}}^3}{/tex}
R3 = {tex}\frac{{{r^2}h}}{4}{/tex}
R3 = {tex}\frac{{2 \times 2 \times 8}}{4}{/tex}
R = 2 cm

questions.question: A spherical ball of diameter 21cm is melted and recast into cubes each of side 1cm. The numbers of cubes so formed are
questions.explanation:
Let the radius of the spherical ball be r
and side of the cube is a
According to the question,
No. of cubes = {tex}\frac{{{\text{Volume of spherical ball}}}}{{{\text{Volume of a cube}}}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{{\frac{4}{3}\pi {r^3}}}{{{a^3}}}{/tex}
No. of cubes = {tex}\frac{{4 \times 22 \times 21 \times 21 \times 21}}{{3 \times 7 \times 2 \times 2 \times 2 \times {{\left( 1 \right)}^3}}}{/tex} = 4851

questions.question: If a cone is cut into two parts by a horizontal plane passing through the mid-point of its axis, the ratio of the volumes of the upper part and the cone is
questions.explanation:
In the figure, C and D are the mid-points and CD || AB which divide the cone into two parts
 
Height OO' = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} OP and diameter CD = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} AB
Let h be the height and r be the radius of the cone, then
{tex}\frac{h}{2}{/tex} will be the height of the smaller cone and {tex}\frac{r}{2}{/tex} be its radius, then
Volume of bigger cone = {tex}\frac{1}{3} \pi r^{2} h{/tex}
and volume of smaller cone
{tex}=\frac{1}{3} \pi\left(\frac{r}{2}\right)^{2}\left(\frac{h}{2}\right){/tex}
{tex}\therefore \frac{\frac{1}{3} \pi\left(\frac{r}{2}\right)^{2}\left(\frac{h}{2}\right)}{\frac{1}{3} \pi r^{2} h}=\frac{\frac{1}{3} \pi \frac{r^{2}}{4} \times \frac{h}{2}}{\frac{1}{3} \pi r^{2} h}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{\frac{1}{8}\left(\frac{1}{3} \pi r^{2} h\right)}{\frac{1}{3} \pi r^{2} h}=\frac{1}{8}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Ratio = 1 : 8

questions.question: The curved surface area of a right circular cone of height 15 cm and base diameter 16 cm is
questions.explanation:
Diameter of base of a right circular cone = 16 cm
Radius (r) = {tex}\left(\frac{16}{2}\right){/tex} = 8 cm
and height (h) = 15 cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Slant height (l) = {tex}\sqrt{r^{2}+h^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{(8)^{2}+(15)^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{64+225}=\sqrt{289}{/tex} = 17 cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Curved surface area = {tex}\pi rl{/tex}
= {tex}\pi{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 8 {tex}\times{/tex} 17 = 136{tex}\pi{/tex} cm2

questions.question: A right triangle with sides 3 cm, 4 cm and 5 cm is rotated about the side of 3 cm to form a cone. The volume of the cone so formed is
questions.explanation:
A cone is formed be rotating the right angled triangle above the side 3 cm
Height of cone = (h) = 3 cm
and radius (r) = 4 cm

{tex}\therefore{/tex} Volume = {tex}\frac{1}{3} \pi r^{2} h=\frac{1}{3} \pi \times(4)^{2} \times 3 \mathrm{cm}^{3}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{3} \pi \times 16 \times 3=16 \pi \mathrm{cm}^{3}{/tex}

questions.question: The volumes of two spheres are in the ratio 64 : 27. The ratio of their surface areas is
questions.explanation:
Ratio in volumes of two spheres = 64 : 27
= (4)3 : (3)3
{tex}\because{/tex} Volume is in cubic units
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Length will be units while areas are in square units
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Areas will be in the ratio = (4)2 : (3)2 = 16 : 9

questions.question: The volume of the greatest sphere that can be cut off from a cylindrical log of wood of base radius 1 cm and height 5 cm is
questions.explanation:
Radius of cylindrical log (r) = 1 cm
and height (h) = 5 cm
The radius of the greatest sphere cut off from the cylindrical log will be = radius of the log = 1 cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Volume = {tex}\frac{4}{3} \pi r^{3}=\frac{4}{3} \pi(1)^{3}=\frac{4}{3} \pi \mathrm{cm}^{3}{/tex}

questions.question: A right circular cylinder of radius r and height h (h > 2r) just encloses a sphere of diameter
questions.explanation: Because the sphere enclose in the cylinder, therefore the diameter of sphere is equal to diameter of cylinder which is 2r.

questions.question: If each edge of a cube is increased by 50%, the percentage increase in the surface area is
questions.explanation:
Let the original edge of the cube be a units.
Then, the original surface area of the cube = 6a2 units
New edge of the cube = 150% of a
= {tex}\frac{150 a}{100}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{3 a}{2}{/tex}
Hence, new surface area = 6 {tex}\times\left(\frac{3 a}{2}\right)^{2}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{27 a^{2}}{2}{/tex}
Increase in area ={tex}\left(\frac{27 a^{2}}{2}-6 a^{2}\right){/tex}
= {tex}\frac{15 a^{2}}{2}{/tex}
% increase in surface area= {tex}\left(\frac{15 a^{2}}{2} \times \frac{1}{6 a^{2}} \times 100\right) \%{/tex}
= 125%

questions.question: The volume of a hemisphere is 19404 cm3. The total surface area of the hemisphere is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{2}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \times R^{3}=19404 \Rightarrow R^{3}=\left(19404 \times \frac{21}{44}\right){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} R3 =(21)3 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} R = 21 cm.
Total surface area = {tex}3 \pi R^{2}=\left(3 \times \frac{22}{7} \times 21 \times 21\right) \mathrm{cm}^{2}=4158 \mathrm{cm}^{2}{/tex}

questions.question: Two cubes have their volumes in the ratio 1 : 27. The ratio of their surface areas is
questions.explanation:
1 : 9
Let the edges of the two cubes be a and b.
Then, ratio of their volumes = {tex}\frac{a^{3}}{b^{3}}{/tex}
Therefore
{tex}\frac{a^{3}}{b^{3}}=\frac{1}{27}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow\left(\frac{a}{b}\right)^{3}=\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^{3}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{a}{b}=\frac{1}{3}{/tex}
The ratio of their surface areas= {tex}\frac{6 a^{2}}{6 b^{2}}{/tex}
Therefore
{tex}\frac{6 a^{2}}{6 b^{2}}=\frac{1^{2}}{3^{2}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{9} \quad\left[\text { Since } \frac{a}{b}=\frac{1}{3}\right]{/tex}
= 1 : 9
Hence, the ratio of their surface areas is 1:9.

questions.question: The length of the longest pole that can be kept in a room (12 m {tex}\times{/tex} 9 m {tex}\times{/tex} 8 m) is
questions.explanation:
Length of the longest pole = length of diagonal of the room
= {tex}\sqrt{l^{2}+b^{2}+h^{2}}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{(12)^{2}+9^{2}+8^{2}}{/tex} m = {tex}\sqrt{289}{/tex}m = 17 m

questions.question: The area of the base of a right circular cone is 154 cm2 and its height is 14 cm. Its curved surface area is
questions.explanation:
Area of the base of a right circular cone={tex}\pi{/tex}r2
Therefore,
{tex}\pi{/tex}r2 = 154
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{22}{7} \times{/tex} r2 = 154
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r2 = (154 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{7}{22}{/tex})
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r2 = 49
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 7 cm
Now, r = 7 cm and h = 14 cm
Then, slant height of the cone, l = {tex}\sqrt{r^{2}+h^{2}}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{(7)^{2}+(14)^{2}}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{49+196}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{245}{/tex}
= 7 {tex}\sqrt{5}{/tex} cm
Hence, the curved surface area of the cone = {tex}\pi{/tex}rl
= {tex}\left(\frac{22}{7} \times 7 \times 7 \sqrt{5}\right){/tex} cm2
= 154 {tex}\sqrt{5}{/tex} cm2

questions.question: The surface area of a sphere is 154 cm2. The volume of the sphere is
questions.explanation:
{tex}4 \pi R^{2}=154 \Rightarrow 4 \times \frac{22}{7} \times R^{2}=154{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow R^{2}=\left(154 \times \frac{7}{88}\right) \Rightarrow R=\frac{7}{2} {/tex}
Volume of the sphere = {tex}=\frac{4}{3} \pi R^3{/tex}
{tex}=\left(\frac{4}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \times \frac{7}{2} \times \frac{7}{2} \times \frac{7}{2}\right) \mathrm{cm}^{3}=179 \frac{2}{3} \mathrm{cm^3}{/tex}.

questions.question: The length of the diagonal of a cube is 6{tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex} cm. Its total surface area is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\sqrt{3} a=6 \sqrt{3} \Rightarrow a=6{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} surface area = 6a2 cm2 = (6 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 6) cm2 = 216 cm2

questions.question: On increasing each of the radius of the base and the height of a cone by 20% its volume will be increased by
questions.explanation:
Given: Radius of the base and the height of a cone is increased by 20%
The volume of the cone is : {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex}{tex}\pi{/tex}r2h
New radius = r + {tex}\frac{20}{100}{/tex}r = {tex}\frac{120}{100}{/tex}r = {tex}\frac{6}{5}{/tex}r
Similarly, new height = {tex}\frac{6}{5}{/tex}h
New Volume = {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\pi{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} ({tex}\frac{6}{5}{/tex}r)2 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{6}{5 }h{/tex} = {tex}\frac{216}{125} \times \frac{1}{3}{/tex}{tex}\pi{/tex}r2h
Increase in volume = {tex}\frac{216}{125} \times \frac{1}{3}{/tex}{tex}\pi{/tex}r2h – {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex}{tex}\pi{/tex}r2h = {tex}\frac{91}{125} \times \frac{1}{3}{/tex}{tex}\pi{/tex}r2h
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Increase in % =( {tex}\frac{\frac{91}{125} \times \frac{1}{3} \pi r 2 h}{\frac{1}{3} \pi r 2 h}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 100 )% = 72.8%
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Volume will be increased by 72.8%

questions.question: The volume of a cube is 2744 cm3. Its surface area is
questions.explanation:
a3 = 2744 = (23 {tex}\times{/tex} 73) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = (2 {tex}\times{/tex} 7) = 14 cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} surface area = 6a2 cm2 = (6 {tex}\times{/tex} 14 {tex}\times{/tex} 14) cm2 = 1176 cm2

questions.question: The total surface area of a solid hemisphere of radius 7 cm is:
questions.explanation: Total surface area = {tex}3 \pi R^{2}{/tex} =  ( 3{tex}\pi{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 7) cm2 = 147{tex}\pi{/tex} cm2

questions.question: The height of a conical tent is 14 m and its floor area is 346.5 m2. How much canvas, 1.1m wide, will be required for it?
questions.explanation:
Given: The height of the conical tent is 14m and its floor area is 346.5m2.

Let S be the area required for the tent.
The canvas of width 1.1m
The surface area of the tent is: {tex}\pi{/tex}rl
Area of the floor is: {tex}\pi{/tex}r2= 346.5 m2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\pi{/tex}r2= 346.5
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r2 = {tex}\frac{346.5}{\pi}=\frac{441}{4}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = {tex}\sqrt{\left(\frac{441}{4}\right)}=\frac{21}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} l = {tex}\sqrt{( h^{2}+r^{2})}{/tex}(here l is the slant height and h , r are height and radius respectively of cone)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} l = {tex}\sqrt{( 14^{2}+\left(\frac{21}{2}\right)^{2})}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{( 196+\frac{441}{4})}=\sqrt{\frac{1225}{4}}=\frac{35}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} S = {tex}\pi{/tex}rl = {tex}\pi{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex}{tex}\frac{21}{2} \times \frac{35}{2}=\frac{33 \times 35}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex}  Area of the canvas required to cover the tent is: {tex}\frac{33 \times 35}{2}{/tex} m2
{tex}\therefore{/tex} l {tex}\times{/tex} b = {tex}\frac{33 \times 35}{2}{/tex} m2 (here l is length and b is breadth or width of the canvas)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} l {tex}\times{/tex} 1.1 = {tex}\frac{33 \times 35}{2}{/tex} ( width of the canvas is 1.1m)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} l = {tex}\frac{33 \times 35}{2 \times 1.1}{/tex} = 525m
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Length of the canvas required to cover the tent is 525m

questions.question: If the surface area of a sphere is 616 cm2, its diameter (in cm) is
questions.explanation:
{tex}4 \pi r^{2}=616 \Rightarrow{/tex} r2 = {tex}616 \times \frac{1}{4} \times \frac{7}{22}{/tex} = 49 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}r = 7 cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Diameter = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} r = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 = 14 cm

questions.question: If the radius of a sphere becomes 3 times then its volume will become
questions.explanation:
Volume of a sphere of radius r, V = {tex}\frac{4}{3} \pi r^{3}{/tex} 
Volume of a sphere of radius 3r = {tex}\frac{4}{3} \pi \times(3 r)^{3}=27 \times \frac{4}{3} \pi r^{3}=27 V{/tex}

It will be 27 times.

questions.question: The sum of length, breadth and height of a cuboid is 19 cm and its diagonal is 5{tex}\sqrt 5{/tex} cm. Its surface area is
questions.explanation:
Let l, b and h be the length, breadth and height of the cuboid.
Then,
l + b + h = 19
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (l + b + h)2 = (19)2
Therefore
(l2 + b2 + h2) + 2(lb + bh + lh) = 361
{tex}\Rightarrow(5 \sqrt{5})^{2}{/tex} + 2(lb + bh + lh) = 361
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2 (lb + bh + lh) = (361 − 125)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2(lb + bh + lh) = 236 cm2

questions.question: A solid right circular cone is cut into two parts at the middle of its height by a plane parallel to its base. The ratio of the volume of the smaller cone to the whole cone is
questions.explanation: Ratio of volumes = {tex}\frac{\frac{1}{3} \pi \times\left(\frac{r}{2}\right)^{2} \times\left(\frac{h}{2}\right)}{\frac{1}{3} \pi \times r^{2} \times h}=\frac{1}{8}{/tex}

questions.question: The diameter of a sphere is 14 cm. Its volume is
questions.explanation: Volume = {tex}\left(\frac{4}{3} \pi r^{3}\right)=\left(\frac{4}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \times 7 \times 7 \times 7\right) \mathrm{cm}^{3}=1437 \frac{1}{3} \mathrm{cm}^{2}{/tex}.

questions.question: The radius of the base of a cone is 5 cm and its height is 12 cm. Its curved surface area is
questions.explanation:
Given: r = 5 cm, h = 12 cm
Slant height of the cone, {tex}l{/tex}= {tex}\sqrt{r^{2}+h^{2}}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{(5)^{2}+(12)^{2}}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{25+144}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{169}{/tex}
= 13 cm
Hence, the curved surface area of the cone = {tex}\pi{/tex}rl
= {tex}(\pi \times 5 \times 13){/tex} cm2
= 65 {tex}\pi{/tex} cm2

questions.question: The diameter of the base of a cone is 42 cm and its volume is 12936 cm3. Its height is
questions.explanation:
Let h be the height of the cone.
Diameter of the cone = 42 cm
Radius of the cone = 21 cm
Then, volume of the cone = {tex}\frac{1}{3} \pi r^{2} h{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \times 21 \times 21 \times h{/tex}
= 22 {tex}\times{/tex} 21 {tex}\times{/tex} h
Therefore
22 {tex}\times{/tex} 21 {tex}\times{/tex} h = 12936
{tex}\Rightarrow h=\left(\frac{12936}{22 \times 21}\right){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} h = 28 cm
Hence, the height of the cone is 28 cm

questions.question: Three equal cubes are placed adjacently in a row. The ratio of the total surface area of the resulting cuboid to that of the sum of the surface areas of three cubes, is
questions.explanation:
Let the side of cube be a, three cube placed in row then breadth of cuboid be a, length of cuboid is 3a, height of cuboid is a.
Sum of total surface area of three cubes = 6a2 + 6a2 + 6a2 = 18a2
Total surface area of resulting cuboid = 2( lb + bh + lh) = 2( 3a×a + a×a + 3a×a) = 2(7a2) = 14a2
Ratio of total surface area of cuboid to that of cube = 18a2 : 14a2​ = 9 : 7

questions.question: If the length of a diagonal of a cube is {tex}8 \sqrt{3}{/tex} cm, then its surface area is
questions.explanation:
Diagonal  = s{tex}\sqrt3{/tex}  where s = side
Here s = 8
Surface area of cube = 6a2 = 6×8×8 = 384 cm2

questions.question: The number of surfaces of a cone has, is
questions.explanation:
The surface or faces that a cone has are:
(i) Base, (ii) Slanted surface
So, the number of surfaces that a cone has is 2.

questions.question: The area of the curved surface of a cone of radius 2r and slant height {tex}\frac{l}{2}{/tex}, is
questions.explanation:
The formula of the curved surface area of a cone with base radius 'r' and slant height '1' us given as
Curved Surface Area = {tex}\pi rl{/tex}
Hence the base radius is given as '2r' and the slant height is given as '{tex}\frac{l}{2}{/tex}'
Substituting these values in the above equation we have
Curved Surface Area {tex}=\frac{(\pi)(2)(r)(l)}{2}{/tex}
{tex}=\pi rl{/tex}

questions.question: If the radius of the base of a right circular cone is 3r and its height is equal to the radius of the base, then its volume is
questions.explanation:
The formula of the volume of a cone with base radius 'r' and vertical height 'h' is given as
Volume of cone {tex}=\frac{1}{3} \pi r^{2} h{/tex}
Here it is given that the base radius is '3r' and that the vertical height is '3r'
Substituting these values in the above equation we get
Volume of cone {tex}=\frac{1}{3} \pi(3 r)^{2}(3 r){/tex}
{tex}=9 \pi r^{3}{/tex}

questions.question: The sum of the length, breadth and depth of a cuboid is 19 cm and its diagonal is 5{tex}\sqrt{5}{/tex} . Its surface area is.
questions.explanation:
Given,
l + b + h  = 19
squaring we get
l2 + b2 + h2 + 2 {lb+bh+hl} = 361.......(i)
Also we know that
l2  + b2 + h2 = d2  125  .   (since d = {tex}5\sqrt5{/tex})
and Total Surface Area  = 2(lb + bh +hl)
Using in (i) we get  T.S.A = 361 - 125 = 236cm2

questions.question: If the volumes of two cones are in the ratio 1 : 4 and their diameters are in the ratio 4 : 5, then the ratio of their heights, is
questions.explanation:
The formula of the volume of a cone with base radius 'r' and vertical height 'h' is given as 
Volume {tex}=\frac{1}{3} \pi r^{2} h{/tex}
Let the volume, base radius and the height of the two cones be V1, r1, h1 and V2, r2, h2 respectively.
It is given that the ratio between the volume of the two cones is 1 : 4.
Since only the ratio is gives, to use then in our equation we introduce a constant 'k'.
So V1 = 1k
V2 = 4k
It is also gives that the ratio between the base diameters of the two cones is 4 : 6
Hence the ratio between the base radius will also be 4 : 5.
Again, since only the ratio is given, to use them in our equation we introduce another constant 'p'.
So, r1 = 4p
r1 = 5p
Substituting these values in the formula for volume of cone we get.
{tex}\frac{\text { Volume of cone } 1}{\text { Volume of cone } 2}{/tex}{tex}=\frac{(\pi)(4 p)(4 p)\left(h_{1}\right)(3)}{(3)(\pi)(5 p)(5 p)\left(h_{2}\right)}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{V_{1}}{V_{0}}=\frac{16 h_{1}}{25 h_{2}}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{1 k}{4 k}=\frac{16 h_{1}}{25 h_{2}}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{h_{1}}{h_{2}}=\frac{25}{64}{/tex}

questions.question: The curved surface area of one cone is twice that of the other while the slant height of the latter is twice that of the former. The ratio of their radii is
questions.explanation:
The formula of the curved surface area of a cone with base radius 'r' and slant height 'l' us given as
Curved Surface Area = {tex}\pi rl{/tex}
Now there are two cones with base radius, slant height and Curved Surface Area (C.S.A) as r1, l1, C.S.A1 & r2, l2, c, S, A2 respectively.
It is given that C.S.A1 = 2(c, S, A2) and also that l2 = 2(l1). Or this can also be written as 
{tex}\frac{l_{2}}{l_{1}}=2{/tex}
{tex}\frac{C . S . A_{1}}{C . S . A_{2}}=\frac{\pi r_{1} l_{1}}{\pi r^{2} l^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}2 \frac{C . S . A_{2}}{C . S . A_{2}}=\frac{\pi r_{1} l_{1}}{\pi r^{2} l^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}x\frac{r_{1}}{r_{2}}=\frac{2 l_{2}}{l_{1}}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{r_{1}}{r_{2}}=\frac{(2)(2)}{1}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{r_{1}}{r_{2}}=\frac{4}{1}{/tex}

questions.question: If the height and radius of a cone of volume V are doubled, then the volume of the cone, is
questions.explanation:
The formula of the volume of a cone with base radius 'r' and vertical height 'h' is given as
Volume of cone {tex}=\frac{1}{3} \pi r^{2} h=V{/tex}
since it is given that the radius and height are doubled we have the radius as '2r' and the vertical height as '2h'
Volume of modified cone {tex}=\frac{1}{3} \pi(2 r)^{2}(2 h){/tex}
{tex}=\frac{8}{3} \pi r^{2} h{/tex}
= 8 V

questions.question: The diameters of two cones are equal. If their slant heights are in the ratio 5 : 4, the ratio of their curved surface areas, is
questions.explanation:
The formula of the curved surface area of a cone with base radius 'r' and slant height 'l' is given as
Curved Surface Area {tex}= \pi rl{/tex}
Now there are two cones with base radius and slant heights as r1, l1 & r2, l2 respectively.
The ratio between slant heights of the two cones is given as 5: 4, we shall use them by introducing a constant 'k'
So, now l1 = 5k
l2 = 4k
since the base diameters of both the cones are equal we get that r1 = r2 = r
Using these values we shall evaluate the ratio between the curved surface areas of the two cones
{tex}\frac{C . S . A_{1}}{C . S . A_{2}}=\frac{\pi r_{1} l_{1}}{\pi r_{2} l_{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{\pi r(5 k)}{\pi r(4 k)}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{5}{4}{/tex}

questions.question: If the heights of two cones are in the ratio of 1: 4 and the radii of their bases are in the ratio 4 : 1, then the ratio of their volumes is
questions.explanation:
The formula of the volume of a cone with base radius 'r' and vertical height 'h' is given as
Volume {tex}=\frac{1}{3} \pi r^{2} h{/tex}
Let the base radius and the height of the two cones be r1, h1 and r2, h2 respectively
It is given that the ratio between the heights of the two cones is 1 : 4
since only the ratio is given, to use them in our equation we introduce a constant 'k'
So, h1 = 1k
h2 = 4k
It is also given that the ratio between the base radius of the two cones is 4 : 1
Again, since only the ratio is given, to use them in our equation we introduce another constant 'p'
So, r1 = 4p
r2 = 1p
Substituting these values in the formula for volume of cone we get,
{tex}\left(\frac{\text { Volume of cone }_{-} 1}{\text { Volume of cone } 2}\right){/tex}{tex}=\frac{(\pi)(4 p)(4 p)(1 k)(3)}{(3)(\pi)(1 p)(1 p)(4 k)}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{4}{1}{/tex}

questions.question: The slant height of a cone is increased by 10%. If the radius remains the same, the curved surface area is increased by
questions.explanation:
The formula of the curved surface area of a cone with base radius 'r' and slant height '' is given as
Curved Surface Area {tex}= \pi rl{/tex}
Now, it is said that the slant height has increase by 10%. So the new slant height '1.1 l'
So,now
New Curved Surface Area = 1.1 {tex}\pi rl{/tex}
We see that the percentage increase of the Curved Surface Area is 10%

questions.question: If h, S and V denote respectively the height, curved surface area and volume of a right circular cone, then {tex}3 \pi V h^{3}-S^{2} h^{2}+9 V^{2}{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
Here we are asked to find the value for a given specific equation which is in terms
of V, h and Srepresenting the volume, vertical height and the Curved Surface Area of a cone.F
We know {tex}V=\frac{1}{3}\left(\pi r^{2} h\right){/tex} and S = pirl'.
Also, {tex}l=\sqrt{r^{2}+h^{2}}{/tex}
Now, the given equation is
{tex}3 \pi V h^{3}-S^{2} h^{2}+9 V^{2}{/tex}
So, 
{tex}x =3 \pi V h^{3}-S^{2} h^{2}+9 V^{2}{/tex}
{tex}=3 \pi\left(\frac{1}{3} \pi r^{2} h\right) h^{3}{/tex}{tex}-(\pi r l)^{2} h^{2}+9\left(\frac{1}{3} \pi r^{2} h\right)^{2}{/tex}
{tex}=\pi^{2} r^{2} h^{4}-\pi^{2} r^{2} l^{2} h^{2}{/tex}{tex}+9\left(\frac{1}{9} \pi^{2} r^{4} h^{2}\right){/tex}
{tex}=\pi^{2} r^{2} h^{4}-\pi^{2} r^{2} h^{2}(\sqrt{r^{2}+h^{2}})^{2}{/tex}{tex}+\pi^{2} r^{4} h^{2}{/tex}
{tex}=\pi^{2} r^{2} h^{4}-\pi^{2} r^{2} h^{2}(\sqrt{r^{2}+h^{2}})^{2}{/tex}{tex}+\pi^{2} r^{4} h^{2}{/tex}
{tex}=\pi^{2} r^{2} h^{4}-\pi^{2} r^{2} h^{2}\left(r^{2}+h^{2}\right){/tex}{tex}+\pi^{2} r^{4} h^{2}{/tex}
{tex}=\pi^{2} r^{2} h^{4}-\pi^{2} r^{4} h^{2}{/tex}{tex}+\pi^{2} r^{2} h^{4}+\pi^{2} r^{4} h^{2}{/tex}
= 0

questions.question: In a sphere the number of faces is
questions.explanation:
Faces are the flat surface on a 3D figure.
Thus, cone has one flat surface, i.e., the base.
Number of faces of a sphere = 1

questions.question: The total surface area of a hemisphere of radius r is
questions.explanation:
Total surface area of a hemisphere = curved surface area + base area
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} total surface area of a hemisphere of radius r is = 2{tex}\pi{/tex}r2 + {tex}\pi{/tex}r2 = 3{tex}\pi{/tex}r2

questions.question: The ratio of the total surface area of a sphere and a hemisphere of same radius is
questions.explanation:
Total surface area of a sphere = 4{tex}\pi{/tex}r2
Total surface area of a hemisphere = 2{tex}\pi{/tex}r2 + {tex}\pi{/tex}r2 = 3{tex}\pi{/tex}r2
Ratio of the total surface area of a sphere and a hemisphere of same radius = 4 : 3

questions.question: A sphere and a cube are of the same height. The ratio of their volumes is
questions.explanation:
Volume of a cube = side3
Volume of a sphere = (4/3){tex}\pi{/tex}r3
Given, sphere and a cube are of the same height.
Side = diameter = 2r
Ratio of their volumes = {tex}\frac{\frac{4}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \times r^{3}}{(2 r)^{3}}{/tex} = 11 : 21

questions.question: The largest sphere is cut off from a cube of side 6 cm. The volume of the sphere will be
questions.explanation:
Largest sphere that can be cut out of a cube will have its diameter as the side of the cube.
Radius of the largest sphere cut out of a cube of side 6 cm = 6/2 = 3 cm
Volume of a sphere = (4/3){tex}\pi{/tex}r3
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Volume of the largest sphere that is cut off from a cube of side 6 cm = {tex}\frac{4}{3} \times \pi \times 3^{3}{/tex} = 36{tex}\pi{/tex}cm3

questions.question: The height of a solid cone is 12 cm and the area of the circular base is 64{tex}\pi{/tex} cm2. A plane parallel to the base of the cone cuts through the cone 9 cm above the vertex of the cone, the area of the base of the new cone so formed is
questions.explanation:
Height of a solid cone (h) = 12 cm
Area of circular base = {tex}64 \pi cm^2 {/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Radius {tex}=\sqrt{\frac{\text { Area }}{\pi}}=\sqrt{\frac{64 \pi}{\pi}} \mathrm{cm}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{64}=8 \mathrm{cm}{/tex}
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}OAB and {tex}\triangle{/tex}OCD

{tex}\angle 0=\angle 0{/tex} (Common)
{tex}\angle \mathrm{OAB}=\angle \mathrm{OCD}{/tex} (Each 90°)
{tex}\therefore \Delta \mathrm{OAB} \sim \Delta \mathrm{OCD}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \frac{\mathrm{OA}}{\mathrm{OC}}=\frac{\mathrm{AB}}{\mathrm{CD}} \Rightarrow \frac{12}{9}=\frac{8}{\mathrm{CD}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{CD}=\frac{9 \times 8}{12}=6 \mathrm{cm}{/tex}
Now in cone OCD,
Radius = 6 cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Base area = {tex}\pi{/tex} (radius)2
{tex}=\pi \times 6 \times 6=36 \pi \mathrm{cm}^{2}{/tex}

questions.question: If the ratio of volumes of two spheres is 1 : 8, then the ratio of their surface areas is
questions.explanation:
Ratio of volume of spheres = (ratio of radius)3
Given, ratio of volumes of two spheres is 1: 8
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (ratio of radius)3 = 1 : 8
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} ratio of radius = 1 : 2
Ratio of surface area = (ratio of radius)2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Ratio of surface area = 1 : 4

questions.question: If the surface area of a sphere is 144{tex}\pi{/tex}m2, then its volume (in m3) is
questions.explanation:
Surface area of a sphere = 4{tex}\pi{/tex}r2
Given, surface area of a sphere is 144{tex}\pi{/tex}m2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4{tex}\pi{/tex}r2 = 144{tex}\pi{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 6 m
Volume of the sphere = (4/3) × {tex}\pi{/tex} × 63
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Volume of the sphere = 288{tex}\pi{/tex} m3

questions.question: If a solid sphere of radius 10 cm is moulded into 8 spherical solid balls of equal radius, then the surface area of each ball (in sq. cm) is
questions.explanation:
Volume of sphere = (4/3){tex}\pi{/tex}r3
Given, solid sphere of radius 10 cm is moulded into 8 spherical solid balls of equal radius
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (4/3){tex}\pi{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 103 = 8 {tex}\times{/tex} (4/3){tex}\pi{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} r3
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r= 10/2 = 5cm
Surface area of a sphere = 4{tex}\pi{/tex}r2
Thus, the surface area of each sphere = 4 {tex}\times{/tex}{tex}\pi{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 52 = 100{tex}\pi{/tex}

questions.question: The ratio between the volume of a sphere and volume of a circumscribing right circular cylinder is
questions.explanation:
Volume of a sphere = (4/3){tex}\pi{/tex}r3
Volume of a cylinder = {tex}\pi{/tex}r2h
If a cylinder circumscribes a sphere of radius r , then its base radius is ‘r’ and height is diameter = 2r
Ratio between the volume of a sphere and volume of a circumscribing right circular cylinder
= {tex}\frac{\frac{4}{3} \pi r^{3}}{\pi \times r^{2} \times 2 r}{/tex} = 2 : 3

questions.question: A cone and a hemisphere have equal bases and equal volumes the ratio of their heights is
questions.explanation:
Volume of a hemisphere = (2/3){tex}\pi{/tex}r3
Volume of a right circular cone = (1/3){tex}\pi{/tex}r2h
Given, cone and a hemisphere have equal bases and equal volume
Height of a hemisphere is the radius and equal bases implies equal base radius.
(2/3){tex}\pi{/tex}r3 = (1/3){tex}\pi{/tex}r2h
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r : h = 1 : 2

questions.question: A cuboid is 12 cm long, 9 cm broad and 8 cm high. Its total surface area is
questions.explanation:
Total surface area of the cuboid = 2(lb + bh + lh) cm2 
= 2(12 {tex}\times{/tex} 9 + 8 {tex}\times{/tex} 9 + 12 {tex}\times{/tex} 8) cm2 
= 2(108 + 72 + 96) cm2 
= 552 cm2

questions.question: The length, breadth and height of a cuboid are 15m, 6m and 5 dm respectively. The lateral surface area of the cuboid is
questions.explanation:
Lateral surface area of the cuboid = [2(l + b) {tex}\times{/tex} h)]
= {2(15 + 6) {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{5}{10}{/tex}}m2 = 21 m2

questions.question: What is the maximum length of a pencil that can be placed in a rectangular box of dimensions (8 cm {tex}\times{/tex} 6 cm {tex}\times{/tex} 5 cm)? (Given {tex}\sqrt{5}{/tex} = 2.24.)
questions.explanation:
Maximum length of a pencil = diagonal of the cuboid
Now, the diagonal of cuboid is = {tex}\sqrt{1^{2}+b^{2}+h^{2}}{/tex} 
Thus,
Length of longest rod = {tex}\sqrt{\left(8^{2}+6^{2}+5^{2}\right)}{/tex} 
= {tex}\sqrt{(64+36+25)}{/tex} 
= {tex}\sqrt{125}{/tex} 
= 5{tex}\sqrt{5}{/tex} cm
= 5(2.24) cm
= 11.2 cm

questions.question: The volume of a cube is 512 cm3. Its total surface area is
questions.explanation:
Volume of a cube = (side)3 
Given volume = 512 cm3 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (side)3 = 512 cm3 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} side = {tex}\sqrt[3]{512}{/tex} = 8 cm
Total surface area of a cube = 6(side)2 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Total surface area = 6(8)2 cm2 = 384 cm2

questions.question: The height of a cone is 24 cm and the diameter of its base is 14 cm. The curved surface area of the cone is
questions.explanation:
l2 = (r2 + h2) = 72 + (24)2 = (49 + 576) = 625
{tex}\therefore{/tex} l = {tex}\sqrt{625}{/tex} = 25 cm
Curved surface area {tex}\pi{/tex}rl = {tex}\left(\frac{22}{7} \times 7 \times 25\right){/tex} cm2 = 550 cm2

questions.question: The volume of a right circular cone of height 12 cm and base radius 6 cm, is
questions.explanation:
Volume of the cone = {tex}\frac{1}{3} \pi r^{2} h{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{3} \pi \times 6^{2} \times 12{/tex} 
= {tex}\pi \times 36 \times 4{/tex} 
= 144 {tex}\pi{/tex} cm3

questions.question: How much cloth 2.5 m wide will be required to make a conical tent having base radius 7 m and height 24 m?
questions.explanation:
Let the length of the required cloth be L m and its breadth be B m.
Here, B = 2.5 m
Radius of the conical tent = 7 m
Height of the tent = 24 m
Area of cloth required = curved surface area of the conical tent
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} L {tex}\times{/tex} B = {tex}\pi{/tex}rl
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} L {tex}\times{/tex} 2.5 = {tex}\frac{22}{7} \times 7 \times \sqrt{7^{2}+24^{2}}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2.5L = 22 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\sqrt{49}{/tex} + 576
{tex}\therefore{/tex} L = {tex}\frac{22 \times 25}{2.5}{/tex} = 220 m

questions.question: The volume of a cone is 1570 cm3 and its height is 15 cm. What is the radius of the cone? (Use {tex}\pi{/tex} = 3.14.)
questions.explanation:
Let r cm be the radius of the cone.
Volume = 1570 cm3
Then {tex}\frac{1}{3} \times 3.14 \times 1^{2} \times 15{/tex} = 1570
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r2 = {tex}\frac{1570}{3.14 \times 5}{/tex} = 100
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 10 cm

questions.question: The height of a cone is 21 cm and its slant height is 28 cm. The volume of the cone is
questions.explanation:
Volume of cone = {tex}\frac{1}{3} \pi r^{2} h{/tex}
slant height = L = {tex}\sqrt{r^{2}+h^{2}}{/tex}
Substituting values we get = r = {tex}\sqrt{343}{/tex} cm
Volume of cones = {tex}\frac{1}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \times 343 \times 21{/tex} = 7546 cm3

questions.question: The volume of a right circular cone of height 24 cm is 1232 cm3. Its curved surface area is
questions.explanation:
Radius of cone = r cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Volume of cone = {tex}\frac{1}{3} \pi r^{2} h{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1232 = {tex}\frac{1}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \times r^{2} \times 24{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r2 = {tex}\frac{1232 \times 3 \times 7}{22 \times 24}{/tex} = 49
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = {tex}\sqrt{49}{/tex} = 7 cm
Area of curved surface area = {tex}\pi{/tex}rl
l = {tex}\sqrt{r^{2}+h^{2}}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{7^{2}+24^{2}}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{49+576}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{625}{/tex} = 25
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{22}{7} \times 7 \times 25{/tex} = 550 cm2

questions.question: If the height of a cone is doubled then its volume is increased by
questions.explanation:
Let the radius of the cone is r and height is h then 
Volume of the cone = {tex}\frac{1}{3} \pi r^{2} h{/tex} 
If the height of the cone is double the
New volume = {tex}\frac{1}{3} \pi r^{2}(2 h){/tex} = {tex}\frac{2}{3} \pi r^{2} h{/tex} 
Increment of the volume = {tex}\frac{2}{3} \pi r^{2} h-\frac{1}{3} \pi r^{2} h{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{3} \pi r^{2} h{/tex} 
% increase = {tex}\frac{\frac{1}{3} \pi r^{2} h}{\frac{1}{3} \pi r^{2} h} \times 100{/tex} = 100%

questions.question: A conical tent is to accommodate 11 persons such that each person occupies 4 m2 of space on the ground. They have 220 m3 of air to breathe. The height of the cone is
questions.explanation:
Suppose that the height of the cone is h m.
Area of the ground = 11 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 = 44 m2 
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\pi r^{2}{/tex} = 44 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r2 = {tex}\frac{44 \times 7}{22}{/tex} = 14
Also, {tex}\frac{1}{3} \pi r^{2} h{/tex} = 220
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \times 14 h{/tex} = 220 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} h = {tex}\frac{220 \times 21}{22 \times 14}{/tex} = 15 m
Hence, the height of the cone is 15 m.

questions.question: The volume of a sphere of radius 10.5 cm is
questions.explanation:
Volume of a sphere = {tex}\frac{4}{3} \pi r^{3}{/tex} 
Volume = {tex}\frac{4}{3} \pi(10.5)^{3}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{4}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \times(10.5)^{3}{/tex} 
= 4851 cm3

questions.question: The surface area of a sphere is 1386 cm2. Its volume is
questions.explanation:
Surface area of  sphere = 4{tex}\pi{/tex}r2
Given surface area = 1386 cm2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}4 \times \frac{22}{7} \times r^{2}{/tex} = 1386
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r2 = {tex}\frac{1386 \times 7}{88}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{441}{4}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = {tex}\frac{21}{2}{/tex}
Volume of a sphere = {tex}\frac{4}{3} \pi r^{3}{/tex}
Volume = {tex}\frac{4}{3} \pi\left(\frac{21}{2}\right)^{3}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{4}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \times\left(\frac{21}{2}\right)^{3}{/tex}
= 4851 cm3

questions.question: The volume of a sphere is 38808 cm3. Its curved surface area is
questions.explanation:
Volume of a sphere = {tex}\frac{4}{3} \pi r^{3}{/tex}
Given Volume = 38808 cm3
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{4}{3} \pi r^{3}{/tex} = 38808
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r3 = {tex}38808 \times \frac{21}{88}{/tex} = 9261
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 21 cm
Surface area of a sphere  = 4{tex}\pi{/tex}r3
Surface area = {tex}4 \times \frac{22}{7}(21)^{2}{/tex} = 5544 cm2

questions.question: A solid metal ball of radius 8 cm is melted and cast into smaller balls, each of radius 2 cm. The number of such balls is
questions.explanation:
Volume of a sphere = {tex}\frac{4}{3} \pi r^{3}{/tex}
Volume of the solid metal bll = {tex}\frac{4}{3} \pi(8)^{3}{/tex}
Volume of smaller ball = {tex}\frac{4}{3} \pi(2)^{3}{/tex} 
No. of balls = {tex}\frac{\text { Volume of the solid metal ball }}{\text { Volume of smaller ball }}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{\frac{4}{2} \pi(8)^{3}}{\frac{4}{3} \pi(2)^{3}}{/tex}
= 64

questions.question: The height of a cone of diameter 10 cm and slant height 13 cm, is
questions.explanation:
Diameter of cone = 10cm
Radius of cone, r  {tex}=\frac{10}{2} \mathrm{cm}=5 \mathrm{cm}{/tex}
Slant height of cone, l = 13cm
Suppose height of the cone is h 
{tex}\Rightarrow h^{2}=l^{2}-r^{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow h=\sqrt{13^{2}-5^{2}}=\sqrt{169-25}=\sqrt{144}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow h=12 \mathrm{cm}{/tex}

questions.question: The radius of a hemispherical balloon increases from 6 cm to 12 cm as air is being pumped into it. The ratios of the surface areas of the balloon in the two cases is.
questions.explanation:
Total surface area of a hemisphere {tex}=2 \pi r^{2}+\pi r^{2}=3 \pi r^{2}{/tex}
r = 6, surface area = {tex}3 \pi(6)^{2}=36{/tex}
r = 12, surface area {tex}=3 \pi(12)^{2}=144{/tex}
Ratio = 36:144
= 1:4

questions.question: The length of the longest pole that can be put in a room of dimensions (10m {tex}\times{/tex} 10 m {tex}\times{/tex} 5m) is
questions.explanation:
Length of room = 10m
Breadth of room = 10m
Height of room = 5m
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Length of the longest pole = length of diagonal
{tex}=\sqrt{l^{2}+b^{2}+h^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{10^{2}+10^{2}+5^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{100+100+25}=\sqrt{225}=15 \mathrm{m}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The length of the longest pole that can be put in the room with given dimensions = 15 m.

questions.question: If V is the volume of a cuboid of dimensions x, y, z  and A is its surface area, then A / V.
questions.explanation:
Sides of a cuboid are x, y, z
Volume of cuboid = xyz = V
Surface Area = 2(xy + yz + zx) = A
{tex}\frac{A}{V}=\frac{2(x y+y z+z x)}{x y z}{/tex} {tex}=2\left(\frac{1}{z}+\frac{1}{x}+\frac{1}{y}\right)=2\left(\frac{1}{x}+\frac{1}{y}+\frac{1}{z}\right){/tex}

questions.question: If each edge of a cuboid of surface area S is doubled, then surface area of the new cuboid is
questions.explanation:
Let the edges of Cuboid be a, b, c
S = Surface Area = 2(ab + bc + ca)
If a' = 2a, b' = 2b, c' = 2c, then
S' = 2(a'b' + b'c' + c'a')
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} S' = [(2a) (2b) + (2b)(2c) + (2c)(2a)]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} S' = 2[4ab + 4bc + 4ca]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} S' = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 2(ab + bc + ca)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} S' = 4 S

questions.question: The length,width and height of a rectangular solid are in the ratio of 3 : 2 : 1. If the volume of the box is 48 cm3, the total surface area of the box is
questions.explanation:
let 3x, 2x, x be length, breadth and height.
then lbh = 6x3 =88 
x3 = 8
x = 2
Now surface area = 2{lb + by + hl} = 2{24 + 8 + 12} = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 44 = 88 cm2

questions.question: Two cubes have their volumes in the ratio 1 :27. The ratio of their surface area is
questions.explanation:
Let the side of one cube be a, and another cube be a2.
So, {tex}a_{1}^{3}: a_{2}^{3}=1: 2.7{/tex}
or, {tex}\frac{a_{1}^{3}}{a_{2}^{3}}=\frac{1}{27}{/tex}
or, {tex}\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}}=\frac{1}{3}{/tex}
So, {tex}\frac{a_{1}^{2}}{a_{2}^{2}}=\frac{1}{9}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow a_{1}^{2}: a_{2}^{2}=1: 9{/tex}
This is the required ratio of their surface areas.

questions.question: If each edge of a cube is increased by 50%, then the percentage increase in its surface area is
questions.explanation:
Let the original side be a then
Original surface area S = 6a2
When side is increased by 50%, side becomes {tex}\frac{3}{2} a{/tex} then 
Surface area  s {tex}=\frac{27}{2} a^{2}{/tex}
Hence,
Surface area is increases by x {tex}=\frac{\frac{27}{2} a^{2}-6 a^{2}}{6 a^{2}} \times 100=125 \%{/tex}

questions.question: The length of the longest rod that can be fitted in a cubical vessel of edge 10 cm long, is
questions.explanation:
The longest rod that can be fitted in the cubical vessel is its diagonal.
Side of the cube (1) = 10 cm
So, the diagonal of the cube,
{tex}=\sqrt{3 l}{/tex}
{tex}=10 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{cm}{/tex}
So, the length of the longest rod that can be fitted in the cubical box is {tex}10 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{cm} .{/tex}

questions.question: What is the volume of the box?
How much area did Suresh paint?
How much area did Deepak paint?
What amount did Harsh charge?
What amount did Veena pay for painting?
What was the surface area of one ball of 7 mm radius?
What was the surface area of one ball of 3.5 mm radius?
How much Cloth was used for the floor?
What was the volume of the tent?
What was the area of the floor?
What was the total surface area of the tent?
What was the latent height of the tent?
What was the volume of 10 balls of radius 7 mm?
What was the volume of 20 balls of radius 3.5 mm?
How much steel was kept for future use?
What is the area of a cube?
questions.explanation: 2(lb + bh + hl)

questions.question: What is the curved surface area of hemispherical dome?
questions.explanation:
Diameter = 44 m
Radius = 22 m
Curved surface area of hemispherical dome
= 2{tex}\pi{/tex}r2
= 2{tex}\pi{/tex}(22)2
= 968{tex}\pi{/tex} m2

questions.question: What is the height of the cuboidal part?
questions.explanation:
Total height of monument = 51 m
Radius of hemispherical dome part = height of hemispherical dome = 22 m
Height of the cuboidal part = 51 m - 22 m
= 29 m

questions.question: What is the circumference of the base of the dome?
questions.explanation:
Circumference of the base of the dome
= 2{tex}\pi{/tex}r
= 2{tex}\pi{/tex}(22)
= 44{tex}\pi{/tex}

questions.question: Find the cost of painting the dome, given the cost of painting is ₹100 per cm2.
questions.explanation:
Curved surface area of hemispherical dome = 968{tex}\pi{/tex} m2
Cost of painting 100 cm2 = ₹10
Cost of painting 1 m2 = ₹1000
Thus, cost of painting the dome = ₹1000 {tex}\times{/tex} 968{tex}\pi{/tex} cm2
= ₹ 968000{tex}\pi{/tex}

questions.question: The radius of a sphere (in cm) whose volume is 12{tex}\pi{/tex} cm3, is
questions.explanation:
Volume of sphere = {tex}\frac 43 \pi r^3{/tex}
12{tex}\pi{/tex} = {tex}\frac 43 \pi r^3{/tex}
r3 = 9
r3 = 32
r = {tex}(3^2)^{\frac 13}{/tex}
r = {tex}3^\frac{2}{3}{/tex} cm

questions.question: The curved surface area of a cone of radius 7 cm is 550 cm2. Its slant height is:
A hemispherical bowl is made of steel of 0.25 cm thickness. The inner radius of the bowl is 5 cm. The volume of steel used is ________. (Use {tex}\pi{/tex} = 3.14)
questions.explanation:
Let r and R be inner and outer radius of bowl respectively.
Then, r = 5 cm, R = 5 + 0.25 = 5.25 cm.
Volume of steel used = Outer volume - Inner volume
= {tex}\frac{2 \pi}{3}(5.25)^3-\frac{2 \pi}{3}(5)^3{/tex} = 41.25 cm3

questions.question: The height of a cone is 30 cm. A small cone is cut off at the top by a plane parallel to the base. If its volume be {tex}\frac 1{27}{/tex} of the volume of the given cone, then at what height above the base is the section made?
questions.explanation:
Let OC = h and AB = r 
Now, {tex}\triangle {/tex}OAB {tex}\sim \triangle {/tex}OCD

{tex}\therefore \frac{A B}{C D}=\frac{O A}{O C}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{r}{C D}=\frac{30}{h}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow C D=\frac{r h}{30}{/tex}
Since, Volume of smaller cone = {tex}\frac 1 {27}{/tex} (Volume of whole cone)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{3} \pi(C D)^2 h=\frac{1}{27} \times \frac{1}{3} \pi r^2 \times 30{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}27 \times\left(\frac{r h}{30}\right)^2 h=r^2 \times 30 {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow h^3=\frac{30 \times 30 \times 30}{27}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} h3 = 10 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} h = 10 cm
Required height, AC = 30 - 10 = 20 cm

questions.question: In a marriage ceremony of her daughter Priyanka, Ashok has to make arrangements for the accommodation of 150 persons. For this purpose, he plans to build a conical tent in such a way that each person have 4 sq. metres of the space on ground and 20 cubic metres of air to breath. What should be the height of the conical tent?
questions.explanation:
Total number of people = 150
Space required for each person on the ground = 4 m2
Area of base of the cone = 150 {tex}\times{/tex} 4
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\pi{/tex}r2 = 600 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r2 = {tex}\frac{2100}{11}{/tex}
Volume of air required by 150 persons = 150 {tex}\times{/tex} 20
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{3} \pi r^2 h=3000 \Rightarrow \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \times \frac{2100}{11} \times{/tex} h = 3000
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 200h = 3000 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} h = 15 m

questions.question: If the height of a cone is equal to its base diameter, then its slant height is
questions.explanation:
Let radius of the cone be r
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Height of the cone (h) = diameter = 2r
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Slant height of the cone (h) = {tex}\sqrt{h^2+r^2}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{(2 r)^2+r^2}=\sqrt{5 r^2}=\sqrt{5} r{/tex}

questions.question: The volume of two spheres are in the ratio 216 : 125. The difference of their surface areas, if the sum of their radii is 11 units, is _____.
questions.explanation:
Let r1 and r2 be radii of two spheres. According to question,
{tex}\frac{\frac{4}{3} \pi r_1^3}{\frac{4}{3} \pi r_2^3}=\frac{216}{125} \Rightarrow\left(\frac{r_1}{r_2}\right)^3=\frac{216}{125} \Rightarrow \frac{r_1}{r_2}=\frac{6}{5}{/tex} ...(i)
Given, r1 + r2 = 11 ...(ii)
From (i) and (ii), we get r1 = 6 units, r2 = 5 units
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Required difference = {tex}4 \pi r_1^2-4 \pi r_2^2{/tex}
{tex}=4 \pi\left(6^2-5^2\right)=4 \pi \times 11=44 \pi \text { sq. units. }{/tex}

questions.question: How many metres of cloth, 5 m wide, will be required to make a conical tent, the radius of whose base is 7 m and height is 24 m?
questions.explanation:
Radius of cone (r) = 7 m
Height of cone (h) = 24 m
{tex}\therefore{/tex}Now slant height of the cone, l = {tex}\sqrt{h^2+r^2}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{(24)^2+(7)^2}=\sqrt{576+49}=\sqrt{625}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} l = 25 m
Curved surface area of conical tent {tex}=\pi r l{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{22}{7} \times 7 \times 25=550 \mathrm{~m}^2{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Required length of cloth
{tex}=\frac{\text { Curved surface area of conical tent }}{\text { Width of cloth }}=\frac{550}{5}{/tex} = 110 m

questions.question: A hemispherical dome of a building needs to be painted from outside. If the circumference of the base of the dome is 17.6 m, then find the cost of painting it at the rate of ₹ 8 per 100 cm2.
questions.explanation:
We have,
Circumference of base = 17.6 m
{tex}=2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times\left(\frac{14}{5}\right)^2=\frac{1232}{25} \mathrm{~m}^2{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow r=\frac{17.6 \times 7}{2 \times 22}=\frac{14}{5}{/tex}
So, outer curved surface area {tex}2 \pi r^2{/tex}|
{tex}=2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times\left(\frac{14}{5}\right)^2=\frac{1232}{25} \mathrm{~m}^2{/tex}
{tex}=\left(\frac{12320000}{25}\right) \mathrm{cm}^2=492800 \mathrm{~cm}^2{/tex}
Now, cost of painting 100 cm2 = ₹ 8
Now, cost of painting 492800 cm2 = {tex}\frac{8}{100} \times 492800{/tex}
= ₹ 39424

questions.question:
Statement-1: If diameter of a sphere is decreased by 25%, then its curved surface area is decreased by 43.75%.
Statement-ll: Curved surface area is increased when diameter decreases.

options.option: Statement-1 is false but Statement-ll is true.
Statement-1 is true but Statement-ll is false.

questions.explanation:
Statement-1: Let the diameter of sphere be 2r.
Decreased diameter = {tex}\left(2 r-\frac{25}{100} \times 2 r\right)=2 r-\frac{r}{2}=\frac{3 r}{2}{/tex}
New diameter = {tex}\frac{3 r}{2}{/tex}
So, new radius (r) = {tex}\frac{3 r}{4}{/tex}
Curved surface area of sphere = {tex}4 \pi r^2{/tex}
New curved surface area = {tex}4 \pi\left(\frac{3 r}{4}\right)^2{/tex}
Now, decreased in curved surface area
{tex}=\frac{4 \pi r^2-4 \pi\left(\frac{3 r}{4}\right)^2}{4 \pi r^2} \times 100 \%=\frac{4 \pi r^2\left(1-\frac{9}{16}\right)}{4 \pi r^2} \times 100 \%{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{7}{16} \times 100 \%{/tex} = 43.75%
Statement-ll: If diameter is decreased, then curved surface area is also decreased.

questions.question: The radius of a sphere is {tex}\frac{7}{2} \mathrm{~cm} {/tex}. The volume of the sphere is:

radius = {tex}\frac 72{/tex}cm
And
Volume of sphere = {tex}\frac{4}{3} \pi r^3{/tex}

questions.question: The ratio of total surface area of a solid hemisphere to the square of its radius is:

{tex}T S A=3 \pi r^2{/tex}
Ratio of TSA to R2

questions.question: A solid sphere is cut into two hemispheres. The ratio of the surface areas of sphere to that of two hemispheres taken together, is:
questions.explanation:
SA of Sphere {tex}=4 \pi r^2 {/tex}
SA of two hemisphere {tex}=2 \times 3 \pi r^2 {/tex}
{tex} =6 \pi r^2 {/tex}
{tex} \frac{S A \text { of sphere }}{\text { SA of Hemisphere }} =\frac{4 \pi r^2}{6 \pi r^2}=\frac{4}{6}=\frac{2}{3}{/tex}

questions.question: The volume of the largest right circular cone that can be carved out from a solid cube of edge 2 cm is:
questions.explanation:
Radius of Base of cone = {tex}\frac 22{/tex} = 1 cm
height of cone = 2 cm
Volume of cone {tex}=\frac{1}{3} \pi r^2 h {/tex}
{tex} =\frac{1}{3} \pi(1)^2 \times 2 {/tex}
{tex} =\frac{2 \pi}{3} {~cm}^3{/tex}

questions.question: The total surface area of a cube of side 20 cm is
questions.explanation:
TSA of cube = 6a2
= 6 {tex}\times{/tex} (20)2
= 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 400
= 2400 cm2

questions.question: If the volume of a sphere is {tex}\frac{11}{21}{/tex} cm3, then the radius of the sphere is:
questions.explanation:
Volume of sphere = {tex}\frac{11}{21} \mathrm{~cm}^3{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \frac{4}{3} \pi r^3=\frac{11}{21} {/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \frac{4}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \times r^3=\frac{11}{21} {/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow 8 r^3=1 {/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow (2 r)^3=1^3 {/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow 2 r=1 \Rightarrow r=\frac{1}{2} {~cm}{/tex}

questions.question: From a solid cube of side 14 cm, a sphere of maximum diameter is carved out. The radius of sphere is:
questions.explanation:
Diameter of sphere = side of Cube 
= 14 cm

= 7 cm

questions.question: In a histogram, each class rectangle is constructed with base as
questions.explanation:

the class mark of the corresponding class interval
the class size of the corresponding class interval

questions.explanation: A histogram is a display of statistical information that uses rectangles to show the frequency of data items in successive numerical intervals of equal size. In the most common form of histogram, the independent variable is plotted along the horizontal axis and the dependent variable is plotted along the vertical axis.

questions.question: In a histogram the class intervals or the groups are taken along
questions.explanation: Histogram states that a two dimensional frequency density diagram is called as a histogram. The histograms are diagrams which represent the class interval and the frequency in the form of a rectangle. There will be as many adjoining rectangles as there are class intervals.

questions.question: A histogram is a pictorial representation of the grouped data in which class intervals and frequency are respectively taken along
questions.explanation: In a histogram the class limits are marked on the horizontal axis and the frequency is marked on the vertical axis. Thus, a rectangle is constructed on each class interval.

questions.question:
To draw a histogram to represent the following frequency distribution :

Class interval 5-10 10-15 15-25 25-45 45-75
Frequency 6 12 10 8 15

The adjusted frequency for the class 25-45 is
questions.explanation:
Adjusted frequency = {tex}\left(\frac{\text{frequency of the class}}{\text{width of the class}}\right)\times5{/tex}
Therefore, Adjusted frequency of 25 - 45 = {tex}\frac 8{20}\times 5=2{/tex}

questions.question: If a person is selected at random, find the probability that the person is of 40 years or more

questions.question: Under 40 years of age

For under 40 years of age required probability = {tex}\frac{38+27}{200}=\frac{65}{200}{/tex} = 0.325

questions.question: Having age from 30 to 39 years
questions.explanation:

questions.question: Under 60 but more than 39

questions.question: Out of 35 students participating in a debate, 10 are girls. The probability that the winner is a boy is

So, the probability that the winner is a boy = {tex}\frac{25}{35}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{5}{7}{/tex}

questions.question: There are 5 balls, each of the colours white, blue, green, red and yellow in a bag. If one ball is drawn from the bag, then the probability that the ball drawn is red is:

Probability of getting red ball = {tex}\frac{1}{5}{/tex}
